## PAGE 00590: Extract Definition and Usage

- page_id: `249577197`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/249577197/Extract+Definition+and+Usage
- version_number: 5
- version_date: `2025-12-17T15:01:46.008+01:00`
- ancestors: SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > Element management > Working with elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Extract Definition and Extract Usage commands improve feature reusability when the model grows to contain usage elements with repetitive features.

- The Extract Definition commands allow you to extract a definition element with the features of the usage element, allowing you to easily generate definition elements that can be used for typing other usage elements.
- The Extract Usage commands allow you to extract a usage element with the features of the usage element, allowing you to easily generate action, use case, and stateusage elements that can be reused and adapted to different contexts.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']On the left: the same attributes *diameter*and *width* are each created for the parts *leftFrontWheel*and *rightFrontWheel*, resulting in duplicated elements that serve the same purpose. 
On the right: once the Extract Definition command **Extract All Features** is executed on the *rightFrontWheel*element, the definition is extracted with the part's attributes. 
The *rightFrontWheel*element is then defined by the extracted definition, inheriting its attributes. The *leftFrontWheel*part can then be defined by the definition as well.

##### Extract Definition commands

Extract Definition commands are:

- Extract All Features to extract a definition element with all of the usage's features.
- Extract Only Attributes and Parameters to extract a definition element only with attributes and parameters of the usage.

Once you execute the Extract Definition commands:

- The features, except for the redefined ones, are moved to the definition, which is then set as the type for the usage. All features that have been moved are then inherited instead of being owned by the usage.

To extract a definition element from a usage element

- Right-click a usage element(s) or its symbol(s). In the shortcut menu, click Refactor , then in the Extract Definition group, click one of the following:
  - Extract All Features . The appropriate definition element is created.
  - Extract Only Attributes and Parameters . The appropriate definition element is created only with the extracted attributes and parameters.

##### Extract Usage commands

You can also use the Extract Usage commands:

- Extract All Features to extract a usage element with all of the usage's features.
- Extract Only Attributes and Parameters to extract a usage element only with attributes and parameters of the usage.

The commands are especially useful when reusing and adapting [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Modeling actions with swimlanes' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Use case scenario' space=''], and states to different contexts.

Once you execute the Extract Usage commands:

- The extracted usage is owned by the same package and named after the element from which it was extracted;
- The extracting usage reference subsets the extracted usage;
- All owned features of the extracting usage are moved to the extracted usage, except for the redefined ones;
- All features that have been moved are then inherited instead of owned by the extracting usage.

The **Extract Usage**commands are only available for [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Perform action' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Include use case' space=''], and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Exhibit state' space=''], unless they reference other elements.

To extract a usage element from a perform action usage, include use case usage, or exhibit state usage element

- Right-click a perform action usage, include use case usage, or exhibit state usage element(s) or their symbol(s). In the shortcut menu, click Refactor , then in the Extract Usage group, click one of the following:
  - Extract All Features . The appropriate action, use case, or state usage element is created.
  - Extract Only Attributes and Parameters . The appropriate action, use case, or state usage element is created only with the extracted attributes and parameters.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=296125670 space=SYSML2P version=17 -->
## PAGE 00591: Extracting style sheets

- page_id: `296125670`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/296125670/Extracting+style+sheets
- version_number: 17
- version_date: `2026-06-05T17:17:14.984+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > Model composition > Views > Symbol styles > Custom style sheets
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

In a view displaying elements with applied [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Metadata' space=''], you can automatically extract a style sheet based on the applied metadata, applied metadata feature value, specialized library element, or library element feature value. The extracted style sheet is automatically applied to the view and the displayed symbols. If you prefer to apply it to the entire project, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Applying style sheets' space=''] page.

##### Extracting style sheets

To extract style sheets via the Style Sheet symbol compartment

1. Open a view displaying elements with applied metadata.
2. Click the Style Sheets [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button in the view toolbar . In the dropdown menu, click the **Extract and** Apply Style Sheet command. The Extract Style Sheets dialog opens. [ATTACHMENT filename='Extract Style Sheets.png']
3. In the dialog, select the needed check box to extract a style sheet.
4. Click Extract .

A part definition element for the new style sheet is created, and its symbol is displayed in the view. The new styles are applied to the displayed symbols based on the applied metadata feature value.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Extracting style sheet rules

If you have already created a style sheet, you can supplement its existing rules by extracting metadata-based rules.

To extract a style sheet rule via the Style Sheet symbol compartment

1. Open a view displaying elements with applied metadata.
2. In a view, select the style sheet's part definition element symbol and click the Create Element button on it.
3. In the dropdown menu, click on the extract rule . The Extract Rule dialog opens. [ATTACHMENT filename='extract rule.png']
4. In the dialog, select the needed check box to extract a style sheet rule.
5. Click Extract .

A rule is extracted in the selected style sheet and applied to element symbols based on the applied metadata feature value.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249577307 space=SYSML2P version=2 -->
## PAGE 00592: Feature membership

- page_id: `249577307`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/249577307/Feature+membership
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-19T15:27:50.409+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > Model composition > Relations
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Use feature memberships to specify element features.

- Feature membership relationship is created automatically in the tool when you create a feature for an element.
  - For element creation procedures, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing elements' space=''] page. You can also create features through the creation of a feature membership itself. This is especially useful for element decomposition, such as [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Part decomposition' space=''] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Requirement decomposition' space=''].
- For element ownership change procedures, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Element ownership change' space=''] page.
- The feature membership display can differ based on the related elements. If one of the elements related via a feature membership is a referential usage, it is displayed with a hollow diamond icon, whereas a composite usage is displayed with a filled diamond icon. For procedures on referential usages, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Modifiers' space=''] section on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Modifiers' space=''] page.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Specifying feature memberships

###### Specifying feature memberships via the Textual Editor

To specify feature memberships via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, use curly braces after element declaration to specify the element body. Features specified within the element body become related to the owning element via the feature membership. To indicate a referential (non-composite) usage, use the keyword *ref*before the element keyword. For more procedures on referential usages, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Modifiers' space=''] section on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Modifiers' space=''] page.
2. Click the Synchronize button. part'input unit'{ 
partmouse;// composite usagerefpartmonitor;// referential (non-composite) usage}

###### Specifying feature memberships via the smart manipulator in a view

Creating features via the smart manipulator is only available for a limited selection of elements (e.g., parts, requirements).

To specify feature memberships via the smart manipulator in a view

1. In a view, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating parts' space=''] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying elements in symbolic views' space=''] the part for which you want to specify a feature membership.
2. In the selected symbol's smart manipulator, click the feature membership icon and do one of the following:
  1. To create a composite feature, click elsewhere in the view pane. The new composite feature of the same type is created.
  2. To designate an existing element as a composite one, click on the element you want to designate as the composite feature.

###### Specifying feature memberships via the view palette

To specify feature memberships via the view palette

1. In the view palette, under the Other Relationships group, click the **feature membership** button.
2. In a view, click on the element symbol you want to designate as the owner, then the one you want to designate as the owned feature. A feature membership path symbol is created between the elements.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

 </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// composite usage<br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">ref</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">monitor</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">; A feature membership path symbol is created between the elements.</li></ol>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249577342 space=SYSML2P version=8 -->
## PAGE 00593: Feature typing

- page_id: `249577342`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/249577342/Feature+typing
- version_number: 8
- version_date: `2026-07-02T17:46:58.868+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > Model composition > Relations > Specializations
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Use thefeature typing relationship tospecify a definition for a usage.

##### Feature typing

###### Defining an element via the Textual Editor

To define an element via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, identify the usage element that you want to define.
2. Follow the element name with either one of the following:
  1. keyword defined by
  2. textual symbol : (colon)
3. Follow the keyword with the name of the definition element you want to be the defining one.
4. Click the Synchronize button. partdieselEngine:Engine; 
/* or */ 
partdieselEnginedefined byEngine;

###### Defining an element via the smart manipulator in a view

To define an element via the smart manipulator in a view

1. In a view , select an element symbol, and in its smart manipulator, click the Select Type button. The Type Selection menu appears.
2. In the Type Selection menu, click the needed type to apply it.

- The Type Selection menu displays both the applied types and those available for selection. Clicking an already applied type removes it from the element. To remove all applied types, click <UNSPECIFIED> .
- The red Select Type button [ATTACHMENT filename='Red Select Type button.png'] indicates that the element has no applied types. A blue button [ATTACHMENT filename='Blue Select Type button.png'] indicates that the element is typed.
- The Type Selection menu has the Hide Uncommon Elements filter option enabled by default, which restricts the available selection to compatible types.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Defining an element via the Drag and Drop handler in a view

To define an element via the Drag and Drop handler in a view

- Drag and drop an element from the Containment tree or use its symbol's [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Drag and Drop handler' space=''] on an element symbol executing the Set Type command.

###### Defining an element via the view palette

To define an element via the view palette

1. In the view palette, under the Specializations group, select feature typing .
2. Draw the line with the arrowhead going from the element you want to be defined to the defining one. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Defining an element via the Create Relation command

To define an elementvia the Create Relation command

1. Right-click the element that you want to define.
2. In the shortcut menu, click Create Relation , then in the submenu, under Outgoing , click feature typing . The Create... dialog opens.
3. In the Create... dialog, do one of the following:
  1. In a view, click the element symbol you want to be the defining one. Hold Shift to select multiple elements.
  2. Click the Add [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. The element selection dialog opens. In the element selection dialog, select the element you want to be the defining one and click OK .
  3. Drag and drop the needed element you want to be the defining one from the Containment tree onto the Create... dialog.
4. In the Create... dialog, click OK .

###### Defining an element via the Specification panel

To define an element via the Specification panel

- See the procedure [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification panel' space=''] on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification panel' space=''] page.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249577783 space=SYSML2P version=5 -->
## PAGE 00594: Filtering elements for views

- page_id: `249577783`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/249577783/Filtering+elements+for+views
- version_number: 5
- version_date: `2026-01-13T10:21:03.581+01:00`
- ancestors: SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > Model composition > Views
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can specify filters for view definitions and/or specific views to narrow down what elements can be displayed by views. Once you have[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating views' space=''], specified the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Exposing elements for views' space=''], you can then optionally filter the exposed elements. Views have the capability to filter elements upon exposition based on certain conditions.Only elements that meet all filter conditions are exposed for the view.

Elements can be filtered for views only via the Textual Editor.

##### Filtering elements for views

Ways to perform element filtering for views:

- . You can specify filter conditions directly for the specific view, using the keyword filter .
- . You can also filter elements for views through inheritance, e.g., if the view is defined by a view definition that contains filter conditions, the filters are inherited by and applied to the defined view.

###### Specifying filter conditions

- Filter conditions can be used only for [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Package' space=''] ([CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Importing and filtering elements' space='']) and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Views basics' space=''] (element exposition) elements.
- Filter condition expression must be model-level evaluable. Any SysML v2 expression can be used for the filter condition specification, but it must return *true/false*.
- @ , istype , and hastype classification operators are commonly used to check if an element has the given metadata annotation.
- Element metaclass metadatas are located in the used projects at Standard Libraries > SysML > Systems . Filter conditions require a qualified name for metadata-based filtering (e.g., @SysML::Systems::PartUsage ).
- Useful filter: *filter KerML::Root::Element::isLibraryElement == false;* which filters out KerML library elements.

To specify filter conditions

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor within the body of the view.
2. Declare the keyword filter followed by filter condition expression, including the needed operator and the qualified name of the element you want to expose .
3. Click the Synchronize button.

packageMetadataDefinitions{metadatadefSafety{attributeisMandatory:Boolean;}}

packageVehicle{ 
privateimportMetadataDefinitions::**; 
partvehicle{ 
partinterior{ 
partalarm; 
partseatBelt[2] {@Safety{isMandatory=true;}} 
partfrontSeat[2]; 
partdriverAirBag{@Safety{isMandatory=false;}} 
} 
} 
}

view'Vehicle Features'{ 
exposeVehicle::**;// exposes package *Vehicle*// you can use multiple filter condition declarations: 
filter@SysML::Systems::PartUsage;// exposes part usage elements from the package *Vehicle* 
filter@MetadataDefinitions::SafetyandMetadataDefinitions::Safety::isMandatory;// exposes elements with *Safety* metadata attribute *isMandatory*value *true*from previously filtered part usage elements exposed from the package *Vehicle* 
}

###### Inheriting filter conditions

**Filters in predefined view definitions:**

- Standard predefined [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Symbolic views' space='']/[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Tabular views' space=''] view definitions (except for *RequirementsTable*) do not have any specified filters, meaning, any elements can be exposed by these views. Once you have created such a view, you may want to specify filters to limit the displayed elements.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Symbolic views' space=''] contain:
  - Element filters specified in the *DS_Views::SymbolicViewsByExpression::NonStandardLibraryElementFilter*and *DS_Views::SymbolicViewsByExpression::EssentialElementsFilter*view definitions, narrowing down what elements can be exposed by the view by discarding various standard library elements and other non-essential elements that are unlikely to be relevant for display in views.
  - Additionally, most of the predefined symbolic view definitions that use expressions contain additional filters to narrow down the exposed elements, e.g., *PartsTreeView*view definition contains filters specifying that only part usage elements can be exposed.

**Recommendations**:

- The easiest way to filter exposed elements is to reuse predefined symbolic view definitions that use expressions. See *Example 1*.
  - Keep in mind that the renderers specified for the view definitions are also inherited.
- If you only want to discard uncommon library elements from being displayed in a view, you can reuse the *DS_Views::SymbolicViewsByExpression::NonStandardLibraryElementFilter*and *DS_Views::SymbolicViewsByExpression::EssentialElementsFilter***view definitions. See *Example 2*.

#### PANEL: Example 1

Example 1

viewdefNonStandardLibraryElementFilter { 
// see the *DS_Views::SymbolicViewsByExpression::NonStandardLibraryElementFilter* view definition in the modeling tool to view all filters specified for it 
} 
viewdefTreeView :> EssentialElementsFilter, NonStandardLibraryElementFilter { // the *TreeView*view definition subclassifies the two view definitions inheriting their filters 
renderViews::asTreeDiagram; // the specified renderer will be inherited 
}

viewdefPartsTreeView :> TreeView { // the *PartsTreeView*view definition subclassifies *TreeView* inheriting all filters and renderer 
filterhastypePartUsage; 
}

viewnewView:DS_Views::SymbolicViewsByExpression::PartsTreeView{ // the specific view is defined by *PartsTreeView*view definition inheriting all filters and renderer 
exposeVehicle::**; 
}

#### PANEL: Example 2

Example 2

viewdefNonStandardLibraryElementFilter { 
// see the *DS_Views::SymbolicViewsByExpression::NonStandardLibraryElementFilter* view definition in the modeling tool to view all filters specified for it 
}

viewnewView: DS_Views::SymbolicViewsByExpression::EssentialElementsFilter, DS_Views::SymbolicViewsByExpression::NonStandardLibraryElementFilter{ // the specific view is defined by the view definitions inheriting their filters 
exposeVehicle::**; 
}

// the view is defined by the *Interconnection View* view definition (inheriting its renderer) and view definitions specifying filters:viewnewView: DS_Views::SymbolicViews::iv, DS_Views::SymbolicViewsByExpression::EssentialElementsFilter, DS_Views::SymbolicViewsByExpression::NonStandardLibraryElementFilter{exposeVehicle::**;}

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

package <em>Vehicle </em><br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// you can use multiple filter condition declarations:</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">filter</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">@ </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">SysML::Systems::PartUsage</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// exposes part usage elements from the package <em>Vehicle</em></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">filter</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">@ </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">MetadataDefinitions::Safety</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">and</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">MetadataDefinitions::Safety::isMandatory</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// exposes elements with <em>Safety</em> metadata attribute <em>isMandatory </em>value <em>true </em>from previously filtered part usage elements exposed from the package <em>Vehicle</em><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span><br /></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Inheriting filter conditions</h4><p><strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Filters in predefined view definitions</span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">:</span></strong></p><ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="0b0a4d63-99a1-4a64-896b-f8c377d23d09">Standard</ac:inline-comment-marker> predefined <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Symbolic views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[symbolic]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>/<ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Tabular views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[tabular]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> view definitions (except for <em>RequirementsTable</em>) do not have any specified filters, meaning, any elements can be exposed by these views. Once you have created such a view, you may want to specify filters to limit the displayed elements.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Symbolic views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Predefined symbolic view definitions that use expressions]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> contain:</span><ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Element filters specified in the <em>DS_Views::SymbolicViewsByExpression::NonStandardLibraryElementFilter </em>and <em>DS_Views::SymbolicViewsByExpression::EssentialElementsFilter </em>view definitions, narrowing down what elements can be exposed by the view by discarding various standard library elements and other non-essential elements that are unlikely to be relevant for display in views. </span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Additionally, most of the predefined symbolic view definitions that use expressions contain additional filters to narrow down the exposed elements, e.g., <em>PartsTreeView </em>view definition contains filters specifying that only part usage elements can be exposed.</span></li></ul></li></ul><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><strong>Recommendations</strong>:</span></p><ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The easiest way to filter exposed elements is to reuse predefined symbolic view definitions that use expressions. See <em>Example 1</em>. </span><ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Keep in mind that the renderers specified for the view definitions are also inherited.</span></li></ul></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">If you only want to </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">discard uncommon library elements from being displayed in a view, you can reuse the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><em>DS_Views::SymbolicViewsByExpression::NonStandardLibraryElementFilter </em>and <em>DS_Views::SymbolicViewsByExpression::EssentialElementsFilter</em></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><em> </em></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">view definitions.</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">See <em>Example 2</em>.</span></span></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="03aba2d0-8f56-4f45-8751-79ebf52d04cd"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example 1</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> NonStandardLibraryElementFilter {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">   // see the <em>DS_Views::SymbolicViewsByExpression::NonStandardLibraryElementFilter</em> view definition in the modeling tool to view all filters specified for it</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> TreeView :&gt; EssentialElementsFilter, NonStandardLibraryElementFilter { <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// the <em>TreeView </em>view definition subclassifies the two view definitions inheriting their filters</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">render</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> Views::asTreeDiagram; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// the specified renderer will be inherited</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> PartsTreeView :&gt; TreeView { <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// the <em>PartsTreeView </em>view definition subclassifies <em>TreeView</em> inheriting all filters and renderer</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">filter</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">hastype</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> PartUsage;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">newView</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">DS_Views::SymbolicViewsByExpression::PartsTreeView</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> { <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// the specific view is defined by <em>PartsTreeView </em>view definition inheriting all filters and renderer</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">expose</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Vehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">::**;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5ed62dd3-00e8-480d-ba31-7f21b2afce28"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example 2</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> NonStandardLibraryElementFilter {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">   // see the <em>DS_Views::SymbolicViewsByExpression::NonStandardLibraryElementFilter</em> view definition in the modeling tool to view all filters specified for it</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> new</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">View</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">DS_Views::SymbolicViewsByExpression::EssentialElementsFilter, DS_Views::SymbolicViewsByExpression::NonStandardLibraryElementFilter</span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> { <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// the specific view is defined by the view definitions inheriting their filters</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">expose</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Vehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">::**;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// the view is defined by the <em>Interconnection View</em> view definition (inheriting its renderer) and view definitions specifying filters:<br /></span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">newView</span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">::iv</span></span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">, <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">DS_Views::SymbolicViewsByExpression::EssentialElementsFilter, DS_Views::SymbolicViewsByExpression::NonStandardLibraryElementFilter</span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {<br /></span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249576920 space=SYSML2P version=2 -->
## PAGE 00595: Find dialog

- page_id: `249576920`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/249576920/Find+dialog
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-19T15:14:54.542+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > Model management > Working with Textual Editor > Managing Textual Editor > Textual Editor features
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can invoke the Find dialog to search for or replace specific data in the textual notation.

##### Invoking the Find dialog

To invoke the Find dialog

- Use the Ctrl+F shortcut keys to invoke the Find dialog.

##### Searching for data in the textual notation via the Find dialog

To search for data in the textual notation via the Find dialog

1. Invoke the Find dialog.
2. In the Find field, enter the word you are searching for. The found instances are highlighted throughout the notation.

##### Replacing data in the textual notation via the Find dialog

You can use the Find dialog to replace specific data in the textual notation.

To replace data in the textual notation via the Find dialog

1. Invoke the Find dialog.
2. In the Find field, input the word you want to replace.
3. On the left side of the dialog, click the right-facing arrow. The Find dialog is expanded.
4. In the Replace field, input the replacement word.
5. Do one of the following:
  1. To replace the highlighted instance of the word, either click the Replace button on the dialog or Enter on the keyboard.
  2. To replace all instances of the word, either click the **Replace** **All**button on the dialog or Ctrl+Alt+Enter on the keyboard. All instances of the word can also be changed via the **Change All Occurrences**(Ctrl+F2) command.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p style="text-align: left;">You can invoke the Find dialog to search for or replace specific data in the textual notation. </p><h3>Invoking the Find dialog</h3><p style="text-align: left;">To invoke the Find dialog</p><hr /><ul><li>Use the Ctrl+F shortcut keys to invoke the Find dialog.</li></ul><h3>Searching for data in the textual notation via the Find dialog</h3><p>To search for data in the textual notation via the Find dialog</p><hr /><ol><li>Invoke the Find dialog.</li><li>In the <strong>Find</strong> field, enter the word you are searching for.<br />The found instances are highlighted throughout the notation.</li></ol><h3>Replacing data in the textual notation via the Find dialog</h3><p>You can use the Find dialog to replace specific data in the textual notation. </p><p>To replace data in the textual notation via the Find dialog</p><hr /><ol><li>Invoke the Find dialog.</li><li>In the <strong>Find</strong> field, input the word you want to replace.</li><li>On the left side of the dialog, click the right-facing arrow. The Find dialog is expanded.</li><li>In the <strong>Replace</strong> field, input the replacement word.</li><li>Do one of the following:<ol><li>To replace the highlighted instance of the word, either click the <strong>Replace</strong> button on the dialog or Enter on the keyboard.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">To replace all instances of the word, either click the <strong>Replace</strong> <strong>All </strong>button on the dialog or Ctrl+Alt+Enter on the keyboard.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a00e8947-6778-4457-a9d6-a064b6f6f518"><ac:rich-text-body><p>All instances of the word can also be changed via the <strong>Change All Occurrences </strong>(Ctrl+F2) command.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol></li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249577301 space=SYSML2P version=14 -->
## PAGE 00596: Flow

- page_id: `249577301`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/249577301/Flow
- version_number: 14
- version_date: `2026-07-01T10:40:41.849+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > Model composition > Relations > Connectors
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A flow is a kind of connection that specifies a transfer from a source output to a target input (and, optionally, the payload). A flow is a transfer that continues while the source and target actions execute. The transferred payload can be anything (attribute value, item, part, etc.).

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']The image displays the *fuelFlow* flow connectorfrom *fuelTank.fuelOut*to*engine.fuelIn*with the payload *flowingFuel*defined by *Fuel*.Additionally, the connector is also [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying elements in symbolic views' space=''].

##### Creating flow usage and definition elements

###### Creating flow usage elements via the Textual Editor

To create flow usage elements via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare the flow keyword.
2. Specify the element name (optional), [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specializations' space=''] (optional), (optional), then the from keyword and the name of the source element and output feature. Then follow with the keyword to and the name of the target element and input feature. If the flow has no name and no specialization, the keyword *from*can be omitted.
3. Click the Synchronize button. flow fuelFlow:FuelFlowfromfuelTank.fuelOuttoengine.fuelIn; // named and specialized (feature typing) flowflow:FuelFlowfromfuelTank.fuelOuttoengine.fuelIn; // unnamed specialized(feature typing) flowflow fuelFlowfromfuelTank.fuelOuttoengine.fuelIn;// named unspecializedflowflowfuelTank.fuelOutto engine.fuelIn; // unnamed unspecializedflow can omit the keyword *from*// alternative notation for creating a flow without the source input and output, only source and target: 
partvehicle{ 
partengine{ 
outportfuelIn; 
} 
partfuelTank{ 
inportfuelOut; 
} 
flow{ 
end::>engine; 
end::>fuelTank{ 
:>>fuelOut; 
} 
} 
}

###### Creating flow usage elements via the smart manipulator in a view

To create flow usage elements via the smart manipulator in a view

1. In a view, select an element symbol, and in its smart manipulator, click the flow command.
2. A dropdown menu appears. Select the output element in the menu.
3. Click on the target element. A dropdown menu appears. Select the compatible input element in the menu. A flow usage is created between the elements. Instead of selecting the input and output elements in the **flow** command's dropdown menu, you can select **None**. This way, you can ignore the dropdown selection and select the source and the target of the flow.

###### Creating flow definition and usage elements via the view palette

To create flow definition and usage elements via the view palette

- To create a flow definition:
  1. In the view palette, under the Connectors group, click the **flow** def button to create a flow definition element.
  2. Click in the view pane where you want the flow definition symbol to be created.
- To create a flow usage:
  1. In the view palette, under the Connectors group, click the **flow** button to create a flow usage.
  2. In a view, click on the source element. A dropdown menu appears. Select the output element in the menu.
  3. Then click on the target element. A dropdown menu appears. Select the compatible input element in the menu. A flow usage is created between the elements. If the needed button is not displayed, click the down-facing arrow next to either the **flow**or the **flow****def**button and select the needed button in the dropdown menu.Instead of selecting the input and output elements in the **flow** command's dropdown menu, you can select **None**. This way, you can ignore the dropdown selection and select the source and the target of the flow.

###### Creating flow definition elements via the Textual Editor

To create flow definition elements via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare the *flow* def keyword.
2. Name the element, then specify the flow definition features in the element body:
  1. Specify the flow definition ends by declaring the end keyword, followed by the end name and/or specialization of the needed element.
3. Click the Synchronize button. flow def FuelFlow{ 
ref :>>payload:Fuel; 
 end portfuelIn:FuelIn; 
 end portfuelOut:FuelOut; 
}

###### Creating flow definition elements via the Create Element command

To create flow definition elements via the Create Element command

1. In the Containment tree, right-click an element, and in the shortcut menu, click **Create Element**.
2. In the Create Element dialog, click flow def for flow definition.

###### Auto-creating flow definition elements via the Extract Definition commands

To auto-create a flow definition element via the Extract Definition commands

- See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Extract Definition and Usage' space='']page.

##### Setting the payload for the flow

While the payload name and sign are displayed on the flow in a view by default, you can hide them using the **Show Payload** [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Modifying styles of individual symbols' space=''].

###### Setting the payload for the flow via the Textual Editor

To set the payload for the flow via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor before the keyword*from*within the flow for which you want to set a payload.
2. Declare the keyword of followed by either of the following:
  1. The name of the payload specialized by a definition element.
  2. The name of the definition element you want to set as payload for the flow.
3. Click the Synchronize button. flow:FuelFlowofFuelfromfuelTank.fuelOuttoengine.fuelIn; // specialized(feature typing) flow with payload set as a definition elementflow: FuelFlowof flowingFuel :FuelfromfuelTank.fuelOutto engine.fuelIn; // specialized(feature typing) flow with a named and specialized (feature typing) payloadflow of Fuel from fuelTank.fuelOutto engine.fuelIn; // unspecialized flowwith payload set as a definition element

###### Setting the payload for the flow via the Drag and Drop handler in a view

To set the payload for the flow via the Drag and Drop handler in a view

- Drag and drop an element from the Containment tree or use its symbol's [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Drag and Drop handler' space=''] on:
  - a flow element symbol executing the **Set Payload** **** command.
  - a binary connection path executing the **Create Flow** **** command to create a flow and to set the payload.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>A flow is a kind of connection that specifies a transfer from a source output to a target input (and, optionally, the payload). A flow is a transfer that continues while the source and target actions execute. The transferred payload can be anything (attribute value, item, part, etc.).</p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="display_flow.png" /></ac:image><span>The image displays the <em>fuelFlow</em> flow connector </span>from <em>fuelTank.fuelOut</em><span> to </span><em>engine.fuelIn </em><span>with the payload <em>flowingFuel </em>defined by <em>Fuel</em>. </span><span>Additionally, the connector is also <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Displaying elements in symbolic views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[elaborated]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></h6><h3>Creating flow usage and definition elements</h3><h4>Creating flow usage elements via the Textual Editor</h4><p>To create flow usage elements via the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Textual Editor</span></p><hr /><ol><li>In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare the <em>flow </em>keyword. </li><li>Specify the element name (optional), <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specializations" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[specialization]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> (optional), <ac:link ac:anchor="Setting the payload for the flow via the Textual Editor"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[payload]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> (optional), then the <em>from </em>keyword and the name of the source element and output feature. Then follow with the keyword <em>to </em>and the name of the target element and input feature. <br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="242ae7b3-a28c-4bb8-aa32-a6c470c46322"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If the flow has no name and no specialization, the keyword <em>from </em>can be omitted.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>Click the <strong>Synchronize </strong>button.<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="de1dc05f-b34f-4963-b560-25ddf828339d"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span>flow <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">fuelFlow</span></span></span><span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">:<span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">FuelFlow</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span>from</span><span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">fuelTank</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">.<span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">fuelOut</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span>to</span><span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">engine</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">fuelIn</span></span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;     <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// named and specialized (feature typing) flow<br /></span></span></span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span>flow</span><span> </span></span>:<span> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">FuelFlow</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span>from</span><span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">fuelTank</span>.<span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">fuelOut</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span>to</span><span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">engine</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">fuelIn</span></span></span>;                   <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// unnamed specialized<span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"> (feature typing)</span></span> flow<br /></span></span></span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span>flow <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">fuelFlow</span></span></span><span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span>from</span><span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">fuelTank</span>.<span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">fuelOut</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span>to</span><span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">engine</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">fuelIn</span></span></span>;<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">                     <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// named un<span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">specialized </span></span>flow<br /></span></span></span></span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">flow </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">fuelTank</span>.<span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">fuelOut</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">to <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">engine</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">fuelIn</span></span>; <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">                                           <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// unnamed un<span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">specialized </span></span>flow can omit the keyword <em>from</em></span></span></span></span></span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// alternative notation for creating a flow without the source input and output, only source and target:</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">vehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">engine</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">out</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">port</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">fuelIn</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">fuelTank</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">in</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">port</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">fuelOut</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">flow</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">end</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> ::&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">engine</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">end</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> ::&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">fuelTank</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">            :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">fuelOut</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;        </span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><h4>Creating flow usage elements via the smart manipulator in a view</h4><p>To create flow usage elements via the smart manipulator in a view</p><hr /><ol><li>In a view, select an element symbol, and in its smart manipulator, click the <strong>flow </strong>command.</li><li>A dropdown menu appears. Select the output element in the menu.</li><li>Click on the target element. A dropdown menu appears. Select the compatible input element in the menu. A flow usage is created between the elements.<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1f09c891-fbe6-403d-9954-b2229a417dbe"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Instead of selecting the input and output elements in the <strong>flow</strong> command's dropdown menu, you can select <strong>None</strong>. This way, you can ignore the dropdown selection and select the source and the target of the flow.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><h4>Creating flow definition and usage elements via the view palette</h4><p>To create flow definition and usage elements via the view palette</p><hr /><ul><li>To create a flow definition:<ol><li>In the view palette, under the Connectors group, click the<span> <strong>flow </strong></span><strong>def </strong><span>button to create a flow definition element.</span></li><li>Click in the view pane where you want the flow definition symbol to be created.</li></ol></li><li>To create a flow usage:<ol><li>In the view palette, under the Connectors group, click the<span> <strong>flow </strong></span><span>button to create a flow usage.</span></li><li>In a view, click on the source element. A dropdown menu appears. Select the output element in the menu.</li><li>Then click on the target element. A dropdown menu appears. Select the compatible input element in the menu. A flow usage is created between the elements.<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="769c3a3a-7b27-471d-b151-165748c95ab6"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li data-uuid="29563be4-2b9c-4dbb-a777-9255e98a7768">If the needed button is not displayed, click the down-facing arrow next to either the <span><strong>flow </strong></span>or the <span><strong>flow </strong></span><strong>def </strong>button and select the needed button in the dropdown menu.</li><li data-uuid="cfbcc360-0a21-4f1b-987e-293f572f7bac">Instead of selecting the input and output elements in the <strong>flow</strong> command's dropdown menu, you can select <strong>None</strong>. This way, you can ignore the dropdown selection and select the source and the target of the flow.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol></li></ul><h4>Creating flow definition elements via the Textual Editor</h4><p>To create flow definition elements via the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Textual Editor</span></p><hr /><ol><li>In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare the<span> <em>flow </em></span><em>def</em> keyword.</li><li>Name the element, then specify the flow definition features in the element body:<ol><li>Specify the flow definition ends by declaring the <em>end</em> keyword, followed by the end name and/or specialization of the needed element. </li></ol></li><li>Click the <strong>Synchronize </strong>button.<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c00fd817-e309-4d8e-839d-5b21596e9da9"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span>flow def</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">FuelFlow</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">{</span><br style="text-align: left;" /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">   ref</span> :&gt;&gt;<span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">payload</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">:<span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Fuel</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;</span><br style="text-align: left;" />   end port<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">fuelIn</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">:<span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">FuelIn</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;</span><br style="text-align: left;" />   end port<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">fuelOut</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">:<span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">FuelOut</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;</span><br style="text-align: left;" /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><h4>Creating flow definition elements via the Create Element command</h4><p>To create flow definition elements via the Create Element command</p><hr /><ol><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the Containment tree, right-click an element, and in the shortcut menu, click <strong>Create Element</strong>.</p></li><li>In the <strong>Create Element</strong> dialog, click <strong>flow def</strong> for flow definition.</li></ol><h4>Auto-creating flow definition elements via the Extract Definition commands</h4><p><span>To auto-create a flow definition element via the Extract Definition commands</span></p><hr /><ul><li><span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">See the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Extract Definition and Usage" /></ac:link></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">page.</span></span></li></ul><h3>Setting the payload for the flow</h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5b2449c9-18d1-417d-a7a1-208643954cb0"><ac:rich-text-body><p>While the payload name and sign are displayed on the flow in a view by default, you can hide them using the <strong>Show Payload</strong> <ac:link ac:anchor="The Symbol Style dialog"><ri:page ri:content-title="Modifying styles of individual symbols" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[symbol style]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Setting the payload for the flow via the Textual Editor</h4><p>To set the payload for the flow via the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Textual Editor</span></p><hr /><ol><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the Textual Editor, place the cursor <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">before the keyword<span> </span></span><em style="text-align: left;">from<span> </span></em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">within the flow for which you want to set a payload.</span></p></li><li>Declare the keyword <em>of </em>followed by either of the following:<ol><li>The name of the payload specialized by a definition element. </li><li>The name of the definition element you want to set as payload for the flow. </li></ol></li><li>Click the <strong>Synchronize </strong>button.<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b0ebeef5-7016-4332-ba47-0dc7e6fe8373"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span>flow</span><span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">:<span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">FuelFlow<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">of</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span>Fuel</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span>from</span><span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">fuelTank</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">.<span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">fuelOut</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span></span></span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span>to</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">engine</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">fuelIn</span></span></span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;                         <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// specialized<span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"> (feature typing)</span></span> flow with payload set as a definition element<br /></span></span></span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">flow </span>: <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">FuelFlow<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">of <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">flowingFuel :</span></span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span>Fuel</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">from </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">fuelTank</span>.<span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">fuelOut</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span></span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">to <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">engine</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">fuelIn</span></span></span></span>;   <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// specialized<span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"> (feature typing)</span></span> flow with a named and specialized (feature typing) <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">payload </span></span></span></span></span><br /></span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">flow</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">of</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Fuel</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">from</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">fuelTank</span>.<span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">fuelOut</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span></span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">to</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">engine</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">fuelIn</span></span></span>;                                         <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// unspecialized flow<span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"> with payload <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">set as a definition element</span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><h4>Setting the payload for the flow via the Drag and Drop handler in a view</h4><p>To set the payload for the flow via the Drag and Drop handler in a view</p><hr /><ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Drag and drop an element from the Containment tree or use its symbol's<span> </span></span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Drag and Drop handler" /></ac:link> <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">on:</span><ul><li data-uuid="773c3df5-6552-4ff2-9d8f-abd65fe0ca03"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">a flow <span>element symbol executing the <strong>Set Payload</strong></span></span><span><strong> </strong></span>command.</li><li data-uuid="9a07cec0-f0f6-4567-9b35-e1207914b5d6"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">a binary connection <span>path executing the <strong>Create Flow</strong></span></span><span><strong> </strong></span>command to create a flow and to set the payload.</li></ul></li></ul>
````

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<h3 style="text-align: center;"><span>The content is unavailable in the selected version</span></h3>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=261623535 space=SYSML2P version=7 -->
## PAGE 00598: Frame concern

- page_id: `261623535`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/261623535/Frame+concern
- version_number: 7
- version_date: `2026-02-17T21:53:45.922+01:00`
- ancestors: SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > Model composition > Requirements > Concerns
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A framed concern usage is a subrequirement usage, which will automatically be considered a required constraint of its containing requirement definition or usage.

There are two ways concerns can be framed:

50%

- by referencing a concern defined elsewhere in the model.
  - The element symbol is displayed with the frame keyword (unnamed element) or the frame concern keyword (named element). 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
  - See the procedures:
    - :
      - 
      - 
      - 

50%

- by containing an owned frame concern usage, i.e., the framed concern is the frame concern usage itself.
  - The element symbol is displayed with the frame concern keyword. [ATTACHMENT filename='frame concern 1.png']
  - See the procedures:
    - :
      - 
      - 
      - 

##### Framing a concern by referencing a concern defined elsewhere in the model

###### Framing concerns via the Textual Editor

To frame concerns via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, within the body of the element that you want to frame a concern, do one of the following:
  1. For an unnamed frame concern, declare the frame keyword followed by the name of the framed concern.
  2. For a named frame concern, declare the frame concern keyword, specify the element name, then [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Reference subsetting' space=''] the framed concern.
2. Specify the concern body.
3. Click the Synchronize button.

concernbrakingConcern{subjectvehicle:Vehicle;stakeholderdriver:Person;attributemaxBrakingDistance:DistanceValue;}

requirementbrakingRequirement{ 
subjectvehicle:Vehicle; 
actorenvironment:'Driving Environment'; 
attributespeedLimit:SpeedValue; 
attributemaxBrakingDistance:DistanceValue;

framebrakingConcern{ // step 1.a - unnamed usage frames another concern usage via reference subsetting 
:>>maxBrakingDistance=brakingRequirement::maxBrakingDistance; 
}

frame concernbrakingRequirementConcern ::> brakingConcern{ // step 1.b - named usage frames another concern usage via reference subsetting 
 :>> maxBrakingDistance = brakingRequirement::maxBrakingDistance; 
 } 
}

###### Framing concerns via the Drag and Drop handler in a view

To frame concerns via the Drag and Drop handler in a view

- Drag and drop a concern element from the Containment tree or use its symbol's [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Drag and Drop handler' space=''] and drop it on:
  - the requirement element symbol that you want to frame a concern by referencing it using the Create Frame Concern command.
  - the frame concern usage symbol that you want to frame a concern by referencing it using the **Set Framed Concern** command.

###### Framing concerns via the Specification panel

To frame concerns via the Specification panel

- Drag and drop a concern element from the Containment tree or use its symbol's [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Drag and Drop handler' space=''] and drop it on the Framed Concern field in the Properties section of the Specification panel for the frame concern usage. The appropriate concern is specified for the element.

##### Framing a concern via the owned frame concern usage itself as the framed concern

###### Framing concerns via the Textual Editor1Framing concerns via the Textual Editor

To frame concerns via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, within the body of the element that you want to frame a concern, declare the frame concern keyword followed by its name.
2. Specify the concern body.
3. Click the Synchronize button.

requirementbrakingRequirement{ 
subjectvehicle:Vehicle; 
actorenvironment:'Driving Environment'; 
attributespeedLimit:SpeedValue; 
attributemaxBrakingDistance:DistanceValue;

frameconcernbrakingRequirementConcern{ // named framed concern 
attributemaxBrakingDistance:DistanceValue; 
:>>maxBrakingDistance=brakingRequirement::maxBrakingDistance; 
} 
}

###### Framing concerns via the symbol compartment in a view

To frame concerns via the symbol compartment in a view

1. Do one of the following:
  1. Select a requirement symbol and click the Create Element button on it. In the dropdown menu, click the frame concern element.
  2. Select a requirement symbol and on the right side of the frames compartment, click the Create frame concern button. The new feature is created and displayed in its appropriate compartment.
2. Specify the concern. The new element is created and displayed in the frames compartment.

###### Framing concerns via the Specification panel

To frame concerns via the Specification panel

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification panel' space=''].
2. Do one of the following:
  1. In the Properties section bar, click the **Create Element** [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. The shortcut menu appears. Click the **frame concern**element in the shortcut menu.
  2. In the **frame concern**compartment section's bar, click the **Create frame concern**[IMAGE alt='' src=''] button.
3. Specify the concern. The element is created in the frames compartment.

following:<ol><li class="auto-cursor-target">Select a requirement symbol and click the <strong>Create Element</strong> button on it. In the dropdown menu, click the <strong>frame concern</strong> element.</li><li>Select a requirement symbol and on the right side of the <strong>frames </strong>compartment, click the<span> </span><strong>Create frame concern<span> </span></strong>button.<br />The new feature is created and displayed in its appropriate compartment.</li></ol></li><li data-uuid="295b213b-3d60-4de7-813c-9b34e268e6a5"><span>Specify the concern.<br /></span>The new element is created and displayed in the<span> </span><strong>frames </strong><span>compartment.</span></li></ol><h4 class="auto-cursor-target">Framing concerns via the Specification panel</h4><p>To frame concerns via the Specification panel</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="1b6f3ad9-49e6-43a8-a189-7bf1d74fba97"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Specification panel"><ri:page ri:content-title="Specification panel" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Open the Specification panel]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></li><li data-uuid="1c412fbd-3744-4ea1-81ce-139f84f0e95a"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Do one of the following:</span><ol><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In the Properties section bar, click the <strong>Create Element</strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_element_in_specification_pane_button.png" /></ac:image> button. The shortcut menu appears. </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Click the <strong>frame concern </strong>element in the shortcut menu.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In the <strong>frame concern </strong>compartment section's bar, click the <strong>Create frame concern </strong><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_element_in_compartment_via_specification_pane_button.png" /></ac:image> button.</span></li></ol></li><li data-uuid="7e150abe-0cc1-43b2-8bf7-393182c36618">Specify the concern.<br />The element is created in the <strong>frames </strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">compartment.</span></li></ol></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=263094932 space=SYSML2P version=4 -->
## PAGE 00599: Functions

- page_id: `263094932`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/263094932/Functions
- version_number: 4
- version_date: `2026-03-17T08:55:55.980+01:00`
- ancestors: SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > Model analysis
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The following libraries contain a variety of functions for your modeling needs. The libraries are included with the installation and automatically loaded with each project.

- The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Libraries & samples' space=''] provide standard SysML v2 libraries containing multiple collections of functions organized into separate namespaces (e.g., BasicFunctions, NumericalFunctions , etc.) that are available for use.
- The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Libraries & samples' space=''] library contains the <DS_Functions>DassaultSystemesFunctions namespace, which provides custom Dassault Systèmes functions that supplement those provided by the Standard Libraries .

See the pages in this section for detailed information and examples of each function.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The following libraries contain a variety of functions for your modeling needs. The libraries are included with the installation and automatically loaded with each project.</p><ul><li data-uuid="23a7f4f4-d971-4c5d-8ae0-a7151b446318">The <em><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Libraries &amp; samples" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Standard Libraries]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></em> provide standard SysML v2 libraries containing multiple collections of functions organized into separate namespaces (e.g., <em>BasicFunctions, NumericalFunctions</em>, etc.) that are available for use.</li><li data-uuid="fd9f88ce-6ff3-4499-a674-f274c2278754">The <em><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Libraries &amp; samples" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[3DS SysML Customization]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></em> library contains the <em>&lt;DS_Functions&gt;DassaultSystemesFunctions </em>namespace, which<em> </em>provides custom Dassault Systèmes functions that supplement those provided by the <em>Standard Libraries</em>. </li></ul><p>See the pages in this section for detailed information and examples of each function.</p>
````
