<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249577548 space=SYSML2P version=2 -->
## PAGE 00601: Guard conditions

- page_id: `249577548`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/249577548/Guard+conditions
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-19T15:36:04.361+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > Model composition > Behavior > Actions > Controlling actions
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A succession within the body of an action definition or usage may be given a guard condition represented as a Boolean expression, forming a conditional succession. A conditional succession****asserts that the target action can start only if the guard condition is *true*.The conditional succession shorthand notation is particularly useful for notating several conditional successions outgoing from a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Control nodes' space='']*decide* node.

##### Specifying guard conditions for successions

###### Specifying guard conditions for successions via the Textual Editor

To specify guard conditions for successions via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor between the succession's source and target and declare the *if*keyword followed by the condition. The target of a conditional succession must be specified as a qualified name or feature chain and cannot be a full action usage declaration, even when the shorthand notation is used.You can specify multiple guards by declaring multiple conditional successions.
2. (Optional) Declare the *else*keyword followed by its succession target. It indicates a succession to be taken if the guards evaluate to *false*.
3. Click the**Synchronize**button.

// ------------ Full conditional succession

actiontransportPassenger{ 
... 
decide decideNode;firstdecideNode; // specifies the succession source 
ifanotherDestinationthenmergeNode; // 'ifanotherDestination' specifies a guard condition, followed by a succession 'thenmergeNode' 
elsedone; // specifies a succession to be taken if the guard condition evaluates to *false* 
}

//------------Shorthandconditionalsuccession

actiontransportPassenger{... 
decidedecideNode; // omits the succession sourceas it is identified from the lexically previous action 'decidedecideNode' 
ifanotherDestinationthenmergeNode; // 'ifanotherDestination' specifies a guard condition, followed by a succession 'thenmergeNode' 
elsedone; // specifies a succession to be taken if the guard condition evaluates to *false* 
}

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE


<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=296125707 space=SYSML2P version=4 -->
## PAGE 00602: Hiding style attributes from the Symbol Style dialog

- page_id: `296125707`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/296125707/Hiding+style+attributes+from+the+Symbol+Style+dialog
- version_number: 4
- version_date: `2026-05-22T10:39:06.104+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > Model composition > Views > Symbol styles > Custom style sheets > Creating style sheets > Creating rules for a style sheet > Specifying styles for a rule
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Symbol Styles dialog allows you to customize the styles of individual symbols. However, you can customize a style sheet to hide unnecessary style attributes from the Symbol Style dialog for specific element kinds.

- If you hide an attribute from the Symbol Style dialog for a specific element kind, ensure the style sheet is [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Applying style sheets' space=''] , not a single view, to affect the style of the specified element symbols in all views.
- Currently, you can hide attributes from the Symbol Style dialog only via the Textual Editor.

###### Hiding style attributes from the Symbol Style dialog

To hide style attributes from the Symbol Style dialog for specific element kinds

1. Open a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating style sheets' space=''] in the Textual Editor.
2. Within the body of the part definition of a style sheet, locate the part usage element that redefines style.
3. Within the body of the redefining element, do the following:
  1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Importing and filtering elements' space=''] the *DS_Styles::CoreStylesComponents::KerMLStyles* package.
  2. Declare an attribute usage and apply the InvisibleInDialog metadata to it, then redefine the style attribute you want to hide from the Symbol Style dialog for the element kind specified in the rule's condition . The available attributes depend on the specified style definitions.For a list of primary style definitions and their attributes, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Style sheet styles tables' space=''].For more information on the Symbol Style dialog, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Modifying styles of individual symbols' space=''] page.
4. Synchronize modifications .

partdefNewStyleSheet:>DS_Styles::CoreStylesComponents::StyleSheet{ 
partpartRule:>rule{ 
part:>>condition:DS_Styles::CoreStylesComponents::Predicates::ByElementMetaclassCondition{ 
refitem:>>elementMetaclass=SysML::Systems::PartUsage.metadata; 
} 
part:>>style:DS_Styles::CoreStylesComponents::KerMLStyles::ShapeStyle{ 
privateimportDS_Styles::CoreStylesComponents::KerMLStyles::*; 
#InvisibleInDialogattribute:>>fillColor;// the style attribute *Fill Color* is hidden from the Symbol Style dialog for part usages 
} 
} 
}

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249577550 space=SYSML2P version=2 -->
## PAGE 00603: If action

- page_id: `249577550`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/249577550/If+action
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-19T15:36:19.241+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > Model composition > Behavior > Actions > Controlling actions > Structured control actions
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An if action usage evaluates a condition expression and then performs a then clause action usage if the expression evaluates to *true*, or, optionally, an else clause action usage if the expression evaluates to *false*.

##### Specifying if actions for actions

###### Specifying if actions for actions via the Textual Editor

To specify if actions for actions via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the if action.
2. Specify the if action usage:
  1. Do one of the following:
    1. For an unnamed if action usage, declare the keyword if .
    2. For a named if action usage, declare the keyword action , specify the element name, and then the keyword if .
  2. Follow with the condition expression for the if action usage.
  3. Specify a then clause by doing one of the following:
    1. For an unnamed then clause, follow with the body for the then clause.
    2. For a named then clause, declare the keyword action , specify the element name, and follow with the body for the then clause.
3. (Optional) Specify the else if clause action usage:
  1. Do one of the following:
    1. For an unnamed else if action usage, declare the keyword else if .
    2. For a named else if action usage, declare the keyword else , then the keyword action , specify the element name, and then the keyword if .
  2. Follow with the condition expression for the if action usage.
  3. Specify a then clause by doing one of the following:
    1. For an unnamed then clause, follow with the body for the then clause.
    2. For a named then clause, declare the keyword action , specify the element name, and follow with the body for the then clause.
4. (Optional) Specify the else clause action usage:
  1. Do one of the following:
    1. For an unnamed else action usage, declare the keyword else .
    2. For a named else action usage, declare the keyword else , then the keyword action , and specify the element name.
  2. Specify the body for the else clause.
5. Click the Synchronize button.

// unnamed if, then clause, and else clause action usages: 
actiontestAlarm{ 
ifthreat.level==high{// unnamed if action usage followed by the if condition expression and an unnamed then clause action usage 
performsoundAlarm{incause=threat;}// body of the then clause action usage 
}elseifthreat.level==medium{// unnamed else clause action usage which is itself an if action usage, followed by the if condition expression and an unnamed then clause action usage 
actionsendNotification{inmsg=threat;}// body of the else if action usage 
}else{// unnamed else clause action usage 
actionbeginMonitoring{intarget=threat;}// body of the else action usage 
} 
}

// named if, then clause, and else clause action usages: 
actiontestAlarm{ 
actionthreatHighifthreat.level==highactionalarm{// named 'threatHigh' if action usage followed by the if condition expression and a named 'alarm' then clause action usage 
performsoundAlarm{incause=threat;}// body of the 'alarm' then clause action usage 
}elseactionthreatMediumifthreat.level==mediumactionnotify{// named 'sendNotification' else clause action usage which is itself an if action usage, followed by the if condition expression and a named 'notify' then clause action usage 
actionsendNotification{inmsg=threat;}// body of the 'sendNotification' else if action usage 
}elseactionmonitor{// named 'monitor' else clause action usage 
actionbeginMonitoring{intarget=threat;}// body of the 'monitor' else action usage 
} 
}

###### Specifying if actions for actions via the view palette

To specify if actions for actions via the view palette

1. In the view palette, under the Other Actions group, click the if button to create an if action usage element.
2. Click on an action symbol in the view pane where you want to create the if symbol.
3. (Optional) Specify the element via the Textual Editor.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=291834096 space=SYSML2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00604: Image elements

- page_id: `291834096`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/291834096/Image+elements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-03-13T13:49:24.972+01:00`
- ancestors: SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > Element management > Working with elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

There are two ways you can import an image into your model:

- Import an image as a model element. This way, an attribute definition that contains the imported image is created in the model. The element name is the same as that of the image file.
  - See the procedures on this page.
- Import an image as a symbol in a view.
  - For procedures, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Auxiliary symbolic view symbols' space=''] section on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Auxiliary symbolic view symbols' space=''] page.

##### Add image to model

You can't add images to elements that cannot contain attribute definitions.

###### Adding an image to the model via the Containment tree

To add an image to the model via the Containment tree

- Drag and drop an image from the file system onto the Containment tree. The selected image is imported as an attribute definition.

###### Adding an image to the model via the view palette

To add an image to the model via the view palette

1. In the view palette, under the Common group, click the image attribute def from file button.
2. Click in the view pane. The Open dialog opens.
3. Select the needed image and click Open . The selected image is imported as an attribute definition.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>There are two ways you can import an image into your model:</p><ul><li data-uuid="9314efc7-8bb7-4936-90ae-e16952b646e5">Import an image as a model element. This way, an attribute definition that contains the imported image is created in the model. The element name is the same as that of the image file. <ul><li data-uuid="2f071149-7629-4693-8b87-abc5dbcf1b08">See the procedures on this page.</li></ul></li><li data-uuid="14eb22a7-9bf2-4a6f-85fb-79dbbf5d663a">Import an image as a symbol in a view. <ul><li data-uuid="d7232dae-ceb3-4c31-96b3-1a0367ea4e48">For procedures, see the <ac:link ac:anchor="Image from file"><ri:page ri:content-title="Auxiliary symbolic view symbols" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Image from file]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> section on the <ac:link ac:anchor="Image from file"><ri:page ri:content-title="Auxiliary symbolic view symbols" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Auxiliary symbolic view symbols]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> page.</li></ul></li></ul><h3>Add image to model</h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7948d41f-3adc-46ec-9fd1-426002974942"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can't add images to elements that cannot contain attribute definitions.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Adding an image to the model via the Containment tree</h4><p>To add an image to the model via the Containment tree</p><hr /><ul><li data-uuid="64c9f2d9-b907-4cda-bdaf-a95643349dbc">Drag and drop an image from the file system onto the Containment tree.<br />The selected image is imported as an attribute definition.</li></ul><h4>Adding an image to the model via the view palette</h4><p>To add an image to the model via the view palette</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="542b8a4f-4a74-48be-9d62-fbf66e766211">In the view palette, under the Common group, click the <strong>image attribute def from file</strong> button.</li><li data-uuid="1d4a87d5-8665-4e30-824f-0732794aae2f">Click in the view pane. The <strong>Open </strong>dialog opens.</li><li data-uuid="d5c37777-e6a1-4a54-8b40-b6e2ff6c4a8c">Select the needed image and click <strong>Open</strong>. <br />The selected image is imported as an attribute definition.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249577222 space=SYSML2P version=4 -->
## PAGE 00605: Importing and filtering elements

- page_id: `249577222`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/249577222/Importing+and+filtering+elements
- version_number: 4
- version_date: `2025-12-17T15:07:34.188+01:00`
- ancestors: SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > Element management > Working with elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An import relationship allows one namespace to import memberships from another namespace. The member elements from imported memberships become (unowned) members of the importing namespace in addition to being members of the imported namespace.

##### Element import kinds and visibility

###### Import kinds

The import kind is specified as a specific suffix added to the import's qualified name.

| Import kind | Suffix | Imports | Example |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Membership import | none | Only the specified namespace (i.e., element import). | package 'Vehicle Information' { part vehicleSpecs {} part vehicleRecord { item vehicleModel; }}package 'Vehicle Details' { // membership import: private import 'Vehicle Information'; // imports 'Vehicle Information'} |
| Namespace import | ::* | The content of the specified namespace. | package 'Vehicle Information' { part vehicleSpecs {} part vehicleRecord { item vehicleModel; }}package 'Vehicle Details' { // namespace import: private import 'Vehicle Information'::*; // imports vehicleSpecs, vehicleRecord} |
| Recursive membership import | ::** | The specified namespace and its content, including sub-namespaces and their elements. | package 'Vehicle Information' { part vehicleSpecs {} part vehicleRecord { item vehicleModel; }}package 'Vehicle Details' { // recursive membership import: private import 'Vehicle Information'::**; // imports 'Vehicle Information', vehicleSpecs, vehicleRecord, vehicleModel} |
| Recursive namespace import | ::*::** | The content of the specified namespace, including sub-namespaces and their elements. | package 'Vehicle Information' { part vehicleSpecs {} part vehicleRecord { item vehicleModel; }}package 'Vehicle Details' { // recursive namespace import: private import 'Vehicle Information'::*::**; // imports vehicleSpecs, vehicleRecord, vehicleModel} |

###### Import visibility

The import [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Visibility' space=''] is specified as a specific keyword declared prior to the *import* keyword. The import visibility keywords are as follows:

- public - membership is visible outside the namespace.
- private - membership is invisible from outside the namespace.
- protected - membership is invisible outside the namespace, except in inheritance, where it behaves like public.

##### Element import

###### Importing elements

To import elements

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to import the element(s).
2. Specify the import , declaring the needed keyword.
3. Declare the keyword import .
4. Specify the qualified name of the membership/namespace you want to import, followed by the .
5. (Optional) .
6. Click the Synchronize button.

You can specify multiple*import*relationships for the same view.

package'Vehicle Information'{partvehicleSpecs{}partvehicleRecord{itemvehicleModel;}}

package'Vehicle Details'{ 
privateimport'Vehicle Information'::**;// private recursive membership import of '*Vehicle Information', vehicleSpecs, vehicleRecord, vehicleModel* 
}

#### NOTE: Reviewing imported elements

Reviewing imported elements

To review the elements imported for a specific element, open its [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='']**Specification** window and check the values of the **Imported Membership**property.

###### Importing library elements

Import is especially useful for accessing library elements instead of using a qualified name each time.

#### SECTION: Import instead of qualified name

#### COLUMN: Qualified name instead of import

50%

#### PANEL: Qualified name instead of import

Qualified name instead of import

// instead of using a qualified name each time:

package'Employee Data'{ 
partemployeeData{ 
// accessing 'String' and 'Real' data types from the *ScalarValues* library via qualified name: 
itememployeeName:ScalarValues::String; 
itemID:ScalarValues::Real; 
} 
}

#### COLUMN: Import instead of qualified name

50%

#### PANEL: Import instead of qualified name

Import instead of qualified name

// use import to access library elements:

package'Employee Data'{ 
private importScalarValues::**; // importing *ScalarValues* library 
partemployeeData{ 
itememployeeName:String; // no need for qualified name 
itemID:Real; // no need for qualified name 
} 
}

##### Element import filtering

Namespaces have the capability to filter elements upon import based on certain conditions, usually defined in terms of the metadata provided by annotations of those elements. Only elements that meet all filter conditions become imported members of the namespace.

- Filter conditions can be used only for [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Package' space=''] () and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Views basics' space=''] (element exposition) elements.
- Filter condition expression must be model-level evaluable. Any SysML v2 expression can be used for the filter condition specification, but it must return*true/false*.
- @ , istype , and hastype classification operators are commonly used to check if an element has the given metadata annotation.
- Element metaclass metadatas are located in used projects at Standard Libraries > SysML > Systems . Filter conditions require a qualified name for metadata-based filtering (e.g., @SysML::Systems::PartUsage ).

###### Separately declared filter conditions

To specify separately declared filter conditions

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor within the body of the package after an import declaration.
2. Declare the keyword filter followed by filter condition expression, including the needed operator and the qualified name of the element you want to import .
3. Click the Synchronize button.

#### PANEL: Separately declared filter conditions

Separately declared filter conditions

packageMetadataDefinitions{metadatadefSafety{attributeisMandatory:Boolean;}}

packageVehicle{ 
privateimportMetadataDefinitions::**; 
partvehicle{ 
partinterior{ 
partalarm; 
partseatBelt[2] {@Safety{isMandatory=true;}} 
partfrontSeat[2]; 
partdriverAirBag{@Safety{isMandatory=false;}} 
} 
} 
}

package'Vehicle Features'{ 
privateimportVehicle::**; // imports package *Vehicle*// you can use multiple filter condition declarations:filter@SysML::Systems::PartUsage;// imports part usage elements from the package *Vehicle*filter@MetadataDefinitions::SafetyandMetadataDefinitions::Safety::isMandatory;// imports elements with *Safety*metadata attribute *isMandatory*value *true*from previously filtered part usage elements imported from the package *Vehicle*}

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">item</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">vehicleModel</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;<br /></span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    }<br /></span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">package<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">'Vehicle Details'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span>private<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span>import<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">'Vehicle Information'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">::**; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// private recursive membership import of '<em>Vehicle Information', vehicleSpecs, vehicleRecord, vehicleModel</em></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="db258086-34ec-4ea1-a064-14f563cef664"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Reviewing imported elements</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>To review the elements imported for a specific element, open its <ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Specification window"><ri:page ri:content-title="Specification window" /><ac:link-body><strong>Specification</strong> window</ac:link-body></ac:link> and check the values of the <strong>Imported Membership </strong>property.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4><span>Importing library elements</span></h4><p><span>Import is especially useful for accessing library elements instead of using a qualified name each time.</span></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="section" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5e7fa968-e593-4a36-aa91-af787dcc5b46"><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cf8aeec5-b754-4f5f-ba76-602aeaeba02c"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">50%</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="deb9aa25-bd7f-488c-b27c-339db1203e0e"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Qualified name instead of import</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// instead of using a qualified name each time:</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">package</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> '</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Employee Data'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">employeeData</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {<br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">        // accessing 'String' and 'Real' data types from the <em>ScalarValues</em> library via qualified name:</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">item</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">employeeName</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">ScalarValues::</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">String</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">item</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">ID</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">ScalarValues::Real</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}<br /></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7445873e-0ac6-4c7a-9f6b-7da5a040e90c"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">50%</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1eb6a049-d753-4d74-84ed-e805e9ca1c62"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Import instead of qualified name </ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// use import to access library elements:</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">package</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> '<span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Employee Data'</span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">private import</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">ScalarValues</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">::**;  <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// importing <em>ScalarValues</em> library</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">employeeData</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">item</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">employeeName</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">String</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;   <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// no need for qualified name</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">item</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">ID</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Real</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;   <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// no need for qualified name</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h3>Element import filtering</h3><p>Namespaces have the capability to filter elements upon import based on certain conditions, usually defined in terms of the metadata provided by annotations of those elements. Only elements that meet all filter conditions become imported members of the namespace.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="69834189-7a61-4af4-ac3a-db7ce99b7a9b"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><span>Filter conditions can be used only for <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Package" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[package]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> (<ac:link><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[element import]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>) and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Views basics" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[view]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> (element exposition) elements.</span></li><li><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Filter condition expression must be model-level evaluable. <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Any SysML v2 expression can be used for the filter condition specification, but it must return </span><em style="text-align: left;">true/false</em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">.</span></span></li><li><em>@</em>, <em>istype</em>, and <em>hastype</em> classification operators are commonly used to check if an element has the given metadata annotation.</li><li><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Element metaclass metadatas are located in used projects at </span><em style="text-align: left;">Standard Libraries &gt; SysML &gt; Systems</em><em style="text-align: left;">. </em>Filter conditions require a qualified name for metadata-based filtering (e.g., <em>@SysML::Systems::PartUsage</em><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">).</span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Separately declared filter conditions</h4><p><span>To specify separately declared filter conditions </span></p><hr /><ol><li>In the Textual Editor, place the cursor within the body of the package after an <em>import</em><em> </em>declaration.</li><li>Declare the keyword<span> </span><em>filter </em>followed by filter condition expression, including the needed operator and the qualified name of the element you want to import<span>.</span></li><li>Click the <strong>Synchronize </strong>button.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d35791f4-83c6-41f4-87e5-d3d23b9af69c"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Separately declared filter conditions</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">package</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">MetadataDefinitions</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {<br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">metadata</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Safety</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {<br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">isMandatory</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Boolean</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;<br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    }<br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">package<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Vehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span>private<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span>import<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">MetadataDefinitions</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">::**;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span>part<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">vehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span>part<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">interior</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">            </span>part<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">alarm</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">            </span>part<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">seatBelt</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> [</span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">2</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">] { </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">@</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Safety</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">{</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">isMandatory</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> = </span>true<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;} </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">            </span>part<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">frontSeat</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> [</span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">2</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">];</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">            </span>part<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">driverAirBag</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> { </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">@</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Safety</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">{</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">isMandatory</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> = </span>false<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;} </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">package<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">'Vehicle Features'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> { </span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span>private<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span>import<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Vehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">::**;  <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// imports package <em>Vehicle</em> <br /></span></span></span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">    // you can use multiple filter condition declarations:<br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">filter</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> @ </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">SysML::Systems::PartUsage</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// imports part usage elements from the package <em>Vehicle<br /></em></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">filter</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> @ </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">MetadataDefinitions::Safety</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">and</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">MetadataDefinitions::Safety::isMandatory</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// <span>imports elements with <em>Safety </em>metadata attribute <em>isMandatory </em>value <em>true </em>from previously filtered part usage elements imported from the package <em>Vehicle <br /></em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></span></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249577486 space=SYSML2P version=20 -->
## PAGE 00606: Include use case

- page_id: `249577486`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/249577486/Include+use+case
- version_number: 20
- version_date: `2026-02-17T21:22:28.983+01:00`
- ancestors: SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > Model composition > Behavior > Use cases
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An include use case usage is a use caseusage that is also a kind of perform action usage. A use case definition or usage may contain an include use case usage to specify that the behavior of the containing use case includes the behavior of the included use case. The subject of the included use case is the same as the subject of the containing use case, so the subject parameter of the included use case must have a definition that is compatible with the definition of the containing use case. Actor parameters of the included use case may be bound to corresponding actor parameters of the containing use case as necessary.

An include use case usage can be a feature of:

- a part definition or usage, specifying that the referenced use case is included in the containing part.
- a use case definition or usage, whereby the behavior of the containing use case includes the behavior of the included use case.

There are two ways a use case can be included by another element:

50%

- by referencing the use case usage defined elsewhere in the model.
  - The element symbol is displayed with the include keyword (unnamed element) or the include use case keyword (named element). [ATTACHMENT filename='include_symbol.png'] [ATTACHMENT filename='include_use_case_symbol_2.png']
  - See the procedures:
    - :
      - 
      - 
      - 
      - 
      - 
      - 

50%

- by containing an owned include use case, i.e., the included use case is the include use case usage itself.
  - The element symbol is displayed with the include use case keyword. [ATTACHMENT filename='include_use_case_symbol.png']
  - See the procedures:
    - :
      - 
      - 
      - 
      - 
      - 

##### Including a use case by referencing a use case usage defined elsewhere in the model

###### Including use cases via the Textual Editor1Including use cases via the Textual Editor

To include use cases via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, within the body of the element that you want to include a use case, do one of the following:
  1. For an unnamed included use case, declare the include keyword followed by the name of the included use case.
  2. For a named one, declare the include use case keyword, specify the element name, then [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Reference subsetting' space=''] the included use case.
2. Click the Synchronize button.

use case 'drive vehicle';//referenced use case

use case 'provide transportation' {

// unnamed included use case referencing a use case defined elsewhere in the model 
 include 'drive vehicle' {}

// named included use case referencing a use case defined elsewhere in the model 
 include use case enter references 'drive vehicle' {} 
 include use case enter ::> 'drive vehicle' {}

}

###### Including use cases via the Drag and Drop handler in a view

To include use cases via the Drag and Drop handler in a view

- Drag a use case element from the Containment tree or use its symbol's [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Drag and Drop handler' space=''] and drop it on:
  - the target element symbol that you want to include the use case by referencing it using the **Create Include Use Case** command.
  - the include use case usage symbol that you want to include the use case by referencing it using the **Set Use Case** **Included** command.

###### Including use cases via the view palette

To include use cases via the view palette

1. In the view palette, under the Cases group, click include . If the needed button is not displayed, click the down-facing arrow next to the**include use case******button and select the needed button in the dropdown menu.
2. Do one of the following:
  1. To include an existing use case, in the view pane, click on the source element symbol, then on the use case element symbol.
  2. To include a new use case, in the view pane, click on the source element symbol, then elsewhere in the view pane to create a use case element symbol. The include path is drawn between the element symbols.

###### Including use cases via the smart manipulator in a view

To include use cases via the smart manipulator in a view

- In a view , do one of the following:
  - To include an existing use case, select the use case element symbol, and in its smart manipulator, click the include command. Click on the use case element symbol.
  - To create a new include use case, select the use case element symbol, and in its smart manipulator, click the include command. Click elsewhere in the view pane to create a use case element symbol . The include path is drawn between the element symbols.

###### Including use casesvia the Create Relation command

To include use cases via the Create Relation command

1. Right-click on the client (source) element.
2. In the shortcut menu, click Create Relation , then in the submenu, under Outgoing , click include . The Create... dialog opens.
3. In the Create... dialog, do one of the following:
  1. In a view, click the element symbol you want to be the supplier (target). Hold Shift to select multiple elements.
  2. Click the Add [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. The element selection dialog opens. In the element selection dialog, select the element you want to be the supplier (target) and click OK .
  3. Drag and drop the needed element you want to be the supplier (target) from the Containment tree onto the Create... dialog.
4. In the Create... dialog, click OK .

###### Including use cases via the Specification panel1Including use cases via the Specification panel

To include use cases via the Specification panel

- Drag and drop a use case element from the Containment tree or use its symbol's [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Drag and Drop handler' space=''] handler and drop it on the **Included Use Case** field in the Properties section of the Specification panel for the include use case element. The appropriate use case is specified for the element.

##### Including a use case via the ownedinclude use caseusageitselfas theincluded use case

###### Including use cases via the Textual Editor

To include use cases via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, within the body of the element that you want to include a use case, declare the include use case keyword followed by its name.
2. Click the Synchronize button.

usecase'provide transportation'{

// included use case is the defined include use case usage itself contained by the parent 
 include use case 'drive vehicle' {} 
}

###### Including use cases via the Create Element command

To include use cases via the Create Element command

1. In the Containment tree, right-click an element, and in the shortcut menu, click **Create Element**.
2. In the Create Element dialog, select the include use case element.
3. Name the element.
4. Specify the included use case's features.

###### Including use cases via the view palette2Including use cases via the view palette

To include use cases via the view palette

1. In the view palette, under the Cases group, click the include use case button.
2. Click in the view pane where you want to create the include use case symbol.
3. Name the element.
4. Specify the included use case's features.

###### Including use cases via the symbol compartment in a view

To include use cases via the symbol compartment in a view

- Do one of the following:
  - Select a use case symbol and click the Create Element button on it. In the dropdown menu, click the **include use case** button. The element is created and displayed in the includes compartment.
  - Select a use case symbol and on the right side of the includes compartment, click the Create include use case button. The element is created and displayed in the includes compartment.

###### Including use cases via the Specification panel

To include use cases via the Specification panel

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification panel' space=''] for the use case element.
2. Do one of the following:
  1. In the Properties section bar, click the **Create Element** [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. The shortcut menu appears. Click the include use case button in the shortcut menu.
  2. In the **includes** compartment section's bar, click the **Create include use case** [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. The element is created in the **includes** compartment.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="54752598-6fdb-4b79-8bbe-0df026174a6f">An include use case usage is a use case</ac:inline-comment-marker><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> usage that is also a kind of perform action usage. A use case definition or usage may contain an include use case usage to specify that the behavior of the containing use case includes the behavior of the included use case. The subject of the included use case is the same as the subject of the containing use case, so the subject parameter of the included use case must have a definition that is compatible with the definition of the containing use case. Actor parameters of the included use case may be bound to corresponding actor parameters of the containing use case as necessary.</span></p><p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">An include use case usage can be a feature of:</span></p><ul><li>a part definition or usage, specifying that the referenced use case is included in the containing part. </li><li>a use case definition or usage, whereby the behavior of the containing use case includes the behavior of the included use case.</li></ul><p>There are two ways a use case can be included by another element:</p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="section" ac:schema-version="1" <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=306282838 space=SYSML2P version=2 -->
````
## PAGE 00607: Indirectly used projects

- page_id: `306282838`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/306282838/Indirectly+used+projects
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-05-08T13:00:57.026+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > Project management > Working with projects > Managing local used projects
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The projects used in the main project may also use other projects, called indirectly used projects. When you use a project directly that may have other used projects, these indirectly used projects are loaded in the main project. Once loaded, the indirectly used projects use the same project version that is used in the directly used project. However, if the indirectly used project is later modified and you want this change to be reflected in the main project, you must update the project version in the directly used project, then update the version of the directly used project in the main project.

In the main project, you can see the indirectly used projects:

- Under the Containment tree's Used Projects node, displayed at the same node level as the project that uses it directly.
- The Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog's Indirectly used projects category.
- The Project Usages dialog, nested under the project that uses it directly.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Using indirectly used projects directly

To use indirectly used projects directly

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing local used projects' space='']Open the **Advanced Project Usages Configuration**dialog .
2. In the Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog, do one of the following:
  1. S elect a used project and in the dialog's toolbar, click the **Use Project Directly** [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button .
  2. Right-click a used project and in the shortcut menu, click the **Use Project Directly** command . The cell in the Action column for the removed used project states ' Use Project Directly '. [ATTACHMENT filename='Using indirectly used projects directly .png']
3. (Optional) To undo the action, do one of the following:
  1. In the dialog's toolbar, click the Reset Changes for Selection [ATTACHMENT filename='reset_changes_for_selection_button.png'] button.
  2. Right-click the used project and in the shortcut menu, click the Reset Changes for Selection command.
4. Do one of the following:
  1. To save changes and keep the dialog open, click the Apply button.
  2. To save changes and close the dialog, click the OK button.
  3. To discard changes and close the dialog, click the Cancel button.
5. Click the OK button to close the Project Usages dialog.

###### Opening indirectly used projects

To open indirectly used projects

See the procedures:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Opening used projects' space=''] .
- .

###### Removing indirectly used projects

Indirectly used projects can only be removed from the main project in the following cases:

- If you [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Removing used projects' space=''] .
- If you first, then [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Removing used projects' space=''] .

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

When you use a project directly that may have other used projects, these indirectly used projects are loaded in the main project. Once loaded, the indirectly used projects use the same project version that is used in the directly used project. However, if the indirectly used project is later modified and you want this change to be reflected in the main project, you must update the project version in the directly used project, then update cell in the <strong>Action</strong> column for the removed used project states '<strong>Use Project Directly</strong>'.  <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Using indirectly used projects directly .png" /></ac:image></li></ol></li><li data-uuid="01f66e90-a866-4f65-8957-bfc60e6c4b9f">(Optional) To undo the action, do one of the following:<ol><li>In the dialog's toolbar, click the <strong>Reset Changes for Selection</strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="reset_changes_for_selection_button.png" /></ac:image> button.</li><li>Right-click the used project and in the shortcut menu, click the <strong>Reset Changes for Selection </strong>command.</li></ol></li><li data-uuid="12b8c0ab-b953-4da7-a788-012488eb2b4c">Do one of the following:<ol><li>To save changes and keep the dialog open, click the <strong>Apply </strong>button.</li><li>To save changes and close the dialog, click the <strong>OK </strong>button.</li><li>To discard changes and close the dialog, click the <strong>Cancel</strong> button.</li></ol></li><li data-uuid="92d39831-28bf-4f66-a367-959279e194fa">Click the <strong>OK </strong>button to close the <strong>Project Usages </strong>dialog.</li></ol><h4>Opening indirectly used projects</h4><p>To open indirectly used projects</p><hr /><p>See the procedures:</p><ul><li data-uuid="4f88ecf3-57ab-420d-bc35-065658b75c4b"><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening a used project via the Containment tree"><ri:page ri:content-title="Opening used projects" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Opening a used project via the Containment tree]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li data-uuid="6f951f66-2d40-4927-97c2-992c5ce62996"><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening a used project via the Project Usages dialog" />.</li></ul><h4>Removing indirectly used projects</h4><p>Indirectly used projects can only be removed from the main project in the following cases:</p><ul><li data-uuid="1e507fba-8f0b-43e7-ad0f-8c802a912389">If you <ac:link ac:anchor="Removing used projects "><ri:page ri:content-title="Removing used projects" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[remove the directly used project that uses the project]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. </li><li data-uuid="36822e57-44db-45a6-ac59-dfc323358138">If you <ac:link ac:anchor="Using indirectly used projects directly "><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[use the indirectly used project directly]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> first, then <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Removing used projects" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[remove it as a directly used project]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li></ul><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=314182393 space=SYSML2P version=4 -->
## PAGE 00608: Individual

- page_id: `314182393`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/314182393/Individual
- version_number: 4
- version_date: `2026-07-01T14:57:35.957+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > Model composition > Common elements > Occurrences
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Any occurrence can be specified as an individual, representing an entity with a distinct identity within a modeled system, allowing for its full or partial characterization.

##### Specifying elements as individuals

###### Specifying elements as individuals via the Textual Editor

To specify elements as individuals via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, declare the *individual* keyword prior to the declaration of the element that you want to designate as an individual.
2. Click the **Synchronize**button. individualpartdefMyCar:>Car;

###### Specifying elements as individuals via the element shortcut menu

To specify elements as individuals via the element shortcut menu

- Right-click an element in the Containment tree or its symbol in a view and in its shortcut menu, in the Properties group, select the Is Individual property.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Any occurrence can be specified as an individual, representing an entity with a distinct identity within a modeled system, allowing for its full or partial characterization.</p><h3>Specifying elements as individuals</h3><h4>Specifying elements as individuals via the Textual Editor</h4><p>To specify elements as individuals via the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Textual Editor</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In the Textual Editor, declare the <em>individual</em> keyword prior to the declaration of the element that you want to designate as an individual.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Click the <strong>Synchronize </strong>button. </span></span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="75f9ae3d-b9b5-4f4b-90f4-6cd962e5fe38"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">individual</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">MyCar</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Car</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><h4>Specifying elements as individuals via the element shortcut menu</h4><p>To specify elements as individuals via the element shortcut menu</p><hr /><ul><li>Right-click an element in the Containment tree or its symbol in a view and in its shortcut menu, in the <strong>Properties</strong> group, select the <strong>Is Individual </strong>property.</li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249576718 space=SYSML2P version=7 -->
## PAGE 00609: Installation and system requirements

- page_id: `249576718`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/249576718/Installation+and+system+requirements
- version_number: 7
- version_date: `2025-12-18T18:45:43.791+01:00`
- ancestors: SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > Getting started
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

For information regarding installation and system requirements, visit the [Installation, licensing, and system requirements](https://docs.nomagic.com/IL/?contextKey=installation-licensing-and-system-requirements&version=latest) page.

To use SysML v2 features in the modeling tool, the SysML v1 Plugin and Cameo Requirements Modeler must be installed in addition to the SysML v2 Plugin and the SysML v2 Evaluation Plugin. See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Prerequisites' space=''] page.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>For information regarding installation and system requirements, visit the <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/IL/?contextKey=installation-licensing-and-system-requirements&amp;version=latest">Installation, licensing, and system requirements</a> page.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8c300796-e00f-4b4b-b6e1-a434daa52ba5"><ac:rich-text-body><p>To use SysML v2 features in the modeling tool, the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">SysML v1 Plugin and Cameo Requirements Modeler</span> must be installed in addition to the SysML v2 Plugin and the SysML v2 Evaluation Plugin. See the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Prerequisites" /></ac:link> page.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249577455 space=SYSML2P version=11 -->
## PAGE 00610: Interface

- page_id: `249577455`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/249577455/Interface
- version_number: 11
- version_date: `2026-07-01T10:40:52.518+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > Model composition > Structure
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An interface is a connection whose ends are ports. As such, an interface facilitates the specification and reuse of compatible connections between parts. An interface definition is a kind of connection definition whose ends are restricted to be port definitions, while an interface usage is a kind of connection that is a usage of an interface definition with ends restricted to be port usages. As such, an interface usage connects compatible ports.

- The ends of an interface definition or usage must be port usages.
- The port keyword is optional for interface ends. The keyword is mandatory if the ends have owned [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Cross features' space=''] .

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
The image displays the *'fuel interface'* interface connector where *supplierPort*reference subsets *tankAssy.fuelTankPort*connecting to *consumerPort*which reference subsets *eng.engineFuelPort*. 
Additionally, the connector is also [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying elements in symbolic views' space=''].

##### Creating interface usage and definition elements

###### Creating interface usage elements via the Textual Editor

To create interface usage elements via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare the interface keyword.
2. Specify the element name (optional), [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specializations' space=''] (optional), then the connect keyword, then state the feature ports explicitly (using the connect...to syntax ) or in a comma-separated list of related features. If the interface has no name and no specialization, the keyword *connect*can be omitted.You can also name interface end ports, associating them to feature ports via[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Reference subsetting' space=''].
3. Click the Synchronize button.

interfacedefFuelInterface{ 
end portsupplierPort:FuelOutPort; 
end portconsumerPort:FuelInPort; 
}

partvehicle{ 
parttankAssy{portfuelTankPort;} 
parteng{portengineFuelPort;}

// unnamed unspecialized interface usage connecting feature ports with explicit declaration:interfacetankAssy.fuelTankPorttoeng.engineFuelPort;//unnamed unspecialized interface usage connecting featureports in a comma-separated list:interface(tankAssy.fuelTankPort,eng.engineFuelPort); //unnamed unspecialized interface usage with named end portsconnecting featureports in a comma-separated list:interface(sPort ::>tankAssy.fuelTankPort, cPort ::>eng.engineFuelPort);

// unnamed specialized (feature typing) interface usage with named end portsconnectingfeatureportswith explicit declaration:interface : FuelInterface connect sPort ::> tankAssy.fuelTankPort to cPort ::> eng.engineFuelPort;

// named unspecializedinterface usage connecting featureports with explicit declarations: 
interface fuelInterface connecttankAssy.fuelTankPort to eng.engineFuelPort;

// named specialized (feature typing) interface usage with named end portsconnectingfeatureportswith explicit declaration:interface 'fuel interface' : FuelInterface connect sPort ::> tankAssy.fuelTankPort to cPort ::> eng.engineFuelPort;// named specialized (feature typing) interface usage with named end portsconnecting featureportsin a comma-separated list:interface'fuel interface':FuelInterfaceconnect(sPort::>tankAssy.fuelTankPort,cPort::>eng.engineFuelPort);}

###### Creating interface usage elements via the smart manipulator in a view

To create interface usage elements via the smart manipulator in a view

1. In a view, select an element symbol, and in its smart manipulator, click the interface command.
2. Click on the element you want to connect via an interface. An interface connector is created between the elements with ports on both ends.

###### Creating interface usage elements via the Create Relation command

To create interface usage elements via the Create Relation command

1. Right-click one of the elements you want to connect.
2. In the shortcut menu, click Create Relation , then in the submenu, under Outgoing , click interface . The Create... dialog opens.
3. In the Create... dialog, do one of the following:
  1. In a view, click the element symbol you want to connect. Hold Shift to select multiple elements.
  2. Click the Add [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. The element selection dialog opens. In the element selection dialog, select the element you want to connect and click OK .
  3. Drag and drop the needed element you want to connect from the Containment tree onto the Create... dialog.
4. In the Create... dialog, click OK .

###### Creating interface usage and definition elements via the view palette

To create interface usage and definition elements via the view palette

- To create an interface definition:
  1. In the view palette, under the Connectors group, click the **interface** def button to create an interface definition element.
  2. Click in the view pane where you want the interface definition symbol to be created.
- To create an interface usage:
  1. In the view palette, under the Connectors group, click the **interface** button to create an interface usage.
  2. In a view, click on the first of the two element symbols you want to connect, then on the second. An interface connector is created between the elements with ports on both ends. If the needed button is not displayed, click the down-facing arrow next to either the **interface, n-ary interface**or the **interface****def**button and select the needed button in the dropdown menu.

###### Creating interface definition elements via the Textual Editor

To create interface definition elements via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare the keyword interface def .
2. Name the element, then specify the interface definition features in the element body:
  1. Specify the interface definition ends by declaring the end keyword, followed by the end name and/or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specializations' space=''] of the needed port.
3. Click the Synchronize button.

interfacedefFuelInterface{ // interface definition 
end portsupplierPort:FuelOutPort; 
end portconsumerPort:FuelInPort; 
}

###### Creating interface definition elements via the Create Element command

To create interface definition elements via the Create Element command

1. In the Containment tree, right-click an element, and in the shortcut menu, click **Create Element**.
2. In the Create Element submenu, click interface def for interface definition.

###### Auto-creating interface definition elements via the Extract Definition commands

To auto-create an interface definition element via the Extract Definition commands

- See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Extract Definition and Usage' space='']page.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

An interface definition is a kind of connection definition whose ends are restricted to be port definitions, while an interface usage is a kind of connection that is a usage of an interface definition with ends restricted to be port usages. As such, an interface usage connects compatible ports.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5787418a-803f-4626-9318-7948affdd4fa"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li data-uuid="7e8c53a2-dbb1-41b6-a09f-9612a0b3dd32">The ends of an interface definition or usage must be port usages.</li><li data-uuid="dbfc9b96-fa5e-48ac-a81d-0d304f71faee">The <em>port</em> keyword is optional for interface ends. view</h4><p>To create interface usage elements via the smart manipulator in a view</p><hr /><ol><li>In a view, select an element symbol, and in its smart manipulator, click the <strong>interface </strong>command.</li><li>Click on the element you want to connect via an interface. An interface connector is created between the elements with ports on both ends.</li></ol><h4>Creating interface usage elements <span>via the Create Relation command</span></h4><p>To create interface usage elements via the Create Relation command</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click one of the elements you want to connect.</li><li>In the shortcut menu, click <strong>Create Relation</strong>, then in the submenu, under <strong>Outgoing</strong>, click <strong>interface</strong>. The <strong>Create...</strong><strong> </strong>dialog opens.</li><li>In the <strong>Create...</strong><strong> </strong>dialog, do one of the following:<ol><li>In a view, click the element symbol you want to connect. Hold Shift to select multiple elements. </li><li>Click the <strong>Add <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="add_button.png" /></ac:image> </strong>button. The element selection dialog opens. In the element selection dialog, select the element you want to connect and click <strong>OK</strong>.</li><li>Drag and drop the needed element you want to connect from the Containment tree onto the <strong>Create... </strong>dialog.</li></ol></li><li>In the <strong>Create...</strong><strong> </strong>dialog, click <strong>OK</strong>. </li></ol><h4 class="auto-cursor-target">Creating interface usage and definition elements via the view palette</h4><p>To create interface usage and definition elements via the view palette</p><hr /><ul><li>To create an interface definition:<ol><li>In the view palette, under the Connectors group, click the<span> <strong>interface </strong></span><strong>def </strong><span>button to create an interface definition element.</span></li><li>Click in the view pane where you want the interface definition symbol to be created.</li></ol></li><li>To create an interface usage:<ol><li>In the view palette, under the Connectors group, click the<span> <strong>interface </strong></span><span>button to create an interface usage.</span></li><li>In a view, click on the first of the two element symbols you want to connect, then on the second. An interface connector is created between the elements with ports on both ends.<ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="769c3a3a-7b27-471d-b151-165748c95ab6"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If the needed button is not displayed, click the down-facing arrow next to either the <span><strong>interface, n-ary interface </strong></span>or the <span><strong>interface </strong></span><strong>def </strong>button and select the needed button in the dropdown menu.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol></li></ul><h4 class="auto-cursor-target">Creating interface definition elements via the Textual Editor</h4><p>To create interface definition elements via the Textual Editor</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare the keyword<span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> </span><em style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">interface def</em><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">.</span></li><li>Name the element, then specify the interface definition features in the element body:<ol><li>Specify the interface definition ends by declaring the <em>end</em> keyword, followed by the end name and/or <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specializations" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[ specialization ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>of the needed port.</li></ol></li><li>Click the <strong>Synchronize </strong>button.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="30044d15-f229-4626-9e04-2b2739874b7d"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">interface</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">FuelInterface</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {                <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// interface definition</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">end port</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">supplierPort</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">FuelOutPort</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">end port</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">consumerPort</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">FuelInPort</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Creating interface definition elements via the Create Element command</h4><p>To create interface definition elements via the Create Element command</p><hr /><ol><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the Containment tree, right-click an element, and in the shortcut menu, click <strong>Create Element</strong>.</p></li><li>In the Create Element submenu, click <strong>interface def</strong> for interface definition.</li></ol><h4>Auto-creating interface definition elements via the Extract Definition commands</h4><p><span>To auto-create an interface definition element via the Extract Definition commands</span></p><hr /><ul><li><span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">See the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Extract Definition and Usage" /></ac:link></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">page.</span></span></li></ul>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249577474 space=SYSML2P version=6 -->
## PAGE 00611: Item

- page_id: `249577474`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/249577474/Item
- version_number: 6
- version_date: `2025-09-30T14:53:23.127+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > Model composition > Structure
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An item definition defines identifiable objects that may be acted on over time, such as an input, output, or a stored item, but which do not necessarily perform actions themselves (the latter would be a part element). An item usage is a usage of one or more item definitions. Item usages may be:

- Representations of inputs and outputs (e.g., water, fuel, electrical signals, data).
- Flowing through and/or stored by a system.
- Referencial, referencing discrete items that interact with or pass through the system.

##### Creating item usage and definition elements

###### Creating item usage and definition elements via the Textual Editor

To create item usage and definition elements via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare the keyword:
  1. *item def*for an item definition.
  2. *item* for an item usage.
2. Name the element.
3. (Optional) Specify the item's features in the element body.
4. Click the Synchronize button.

partdefFuelTank{ 
itemdefFuel;// item definition 
} 
itemdefPerson;// item definition

partdefVehicle{ 
refitemdriver:Person;// referential item 
partfuelTank:FuelTank{ 
itemfuel:Fuel;// continuous item usage stored by a system 
} 
}

###### Creating item usage and definition elements via the Create Element command

To create item usage and definition elements via the Create Element command

1. In the Containment tree, right-click an element, and in the shortcut menu, click **Create Element**.
2. In the Create Element dialog, select one of the following:
  1. item def to create an item definition element.
  2. item to create an item usage element.
3. Name the element.
4. (Optional) Specify the item's features.

###### Creating item usage and definition elements via the view palette

To create item usage and definition elements via the view palette

1. In the view palette, under the Items/Ports/Attributes group, click one of the following:
  1. The **item** def button to create an item definition element.
  2. The **item** button to create an item usage element. If the needed button is not displayed, click the down-facing arrow next to either the **item**or the **item def**button and select the needed button in the dropdown menu.
2. Click in the view pane where you want to create the item symbol.
3. (Optional) Specify the item's features.

###### Creating item usage elements via the symbol compartment in a view

For more information, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Compartments' space=''] on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Compartments' space=''] page.

To create item usage elements via the symbol compartment in a view

- Do one of the following:
  - Select the element symbol for which you want to create an item and click the Create Element button on it. In the dropdown menu, click item .
  - Select the element symbol for which you want to create an item and on the right side of the items compartment, click the Create item button. The item is created and displayed in the items compartment.

###### Creating item usage elements via the Specification panel

To create item usage elements via the Specification panel

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification panel' space=''] of the element for which you want to create an item.
2. Do one of the following:
  1. In the Properties section bar, click the **Create Element** [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. The shortcut menu appears. Click the **item** element in the shortcut menu.
  2. In the **items**compartment's section bar, click the **Create item**[IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. The item is created in the **items** compartment.

###### Auto-creating item definition elements via the Extract Definition commands

To auto-create item definition elements via the Extract Definition commands

- See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Extract Definition and Usage' space='']page.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE
