def</strong> to create an item definition element.</li><li><strong>item </strong>to create an item usage element.</li></ol></li><li>Name the element.</li><li>(Optional) Specify the item's features.</li></ol><h4 class="auto-cursor-target">Creating item usage and definition elements via the view palette</h4><p>To create item usage and definition elements via the view palette</p><hr /><ol><li>In the view palette, under the Items/Ports/Attributes group, click one of the following:<ol><li>The<span> <strong>item </strong></span><strong>def </strong>button to create an item definition element.</li><li>The<span> <strong>item</strong></span> button to create an item usage element.<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="769c3a3a-7b27-471d-b151-165748c95ab6"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If the needed button is not displayed, click the down-facing arrow next to either the <strong>item </strong>or the <strong>item def </strong>button and select the needed button in the dropdown menu.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol></li><li>Click in the view pane where you want to create the item symbol.</li><li>(Optional) Specify the item's features.</li></ol><h4 class="auto-cursor-target">Creating item usage elements via the symbol compartment in a view</h4><p>For more information, see the <ac:link ac:anchor="Creating elements via compartments"><ri:page ri:content-title="Compartments" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Creating elements via compartments]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> on the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Compartments" /></ac:link> page.</p><p>To create item usage elements via the symbol compartment in a view</p><hr /><ul><li>Do one of the following:<ul><li>Select the element symbol for which you want to create an item and click the<span> </span><strong>Create Element</strong><span> </span>button on it. In the dropdown menu, click <strong>item</strong>. </li><li>Select the element symbol for which you want to create an item and on the right side of the <strong>items</strong> compartment, click the<span> </span><strong>Create <span>item </span></strong>button. <br />The item is created and displayed in the <strong>items</strong> compartment.</li></ul></li></ul><h4 class="auto-cursor-target">Creating item usage elements via the Specification panel</h4><p>To create item usage elements via the Specification panel</p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Specification panel"><ri:page ri:content-title="Specification panel" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Open the Specification panel]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> of the element for which you want to create an item.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Do one of the following:</span><ol><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In the Properties section bar, click the <strong>Create Element</strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_element_in_specification_pane_button.png" /></ac:image> button. The shortcut menu appears. </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Click the <strong>item</strong> element in the shortcut menu.<br /></span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In the <strong>items </strong>compartment's section bar, click the <strong>Create item </strong><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_element_in_compartment_via_specification_pane_button.png" /></ac:image> button. <br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The item is created in the <strong>items</strong> compartment.</span></li></ol></li></ol><h4>Auto-creating item definition elements via the Extract Definition commands</h4><p><span>To auto-create item definition elements via the Extract Definition commands</span></p><hr /><ul><li><span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">See the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Extract Definition and Usage" /></ac:link></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">page.</span></span></li></ul>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249577165 space=SYSML2P version=5 -->
## PAGE 00612: Key concepts

- page_id: `249577165`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/249577165/Key+concepts
- version_number: 5
- version_date: `2025-09-19T15:22:10.009+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > Element management > Working with elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

This page provides descriptions of basic concepts related to the SysML v2 elements that are relevant to know before you begin modeling.

For a comprehensive list and examples of SysML v2 elements, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model composition' space=''] section.

The general structure of the model is comprised of the global namespace, which encloses one or more namespaces that contain model elements optionally organized into packages.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']Model organization.

##### Key concepts

Below is a list of some of the SysML v2 elements key concepts and their descriptions.

##### Namespace

A namespace is a kind of element that can contain other elements and assign names to them.The contained elements are referred to as member elements, whichare related to a namespace via a membership relationship. It can specify both the name by which the member element is known within the containing namespace and whether the visibility of the element's membership extends beyond the namespace.

**Kinds of namespaces:**

- Global namespace
- Root namespaces
- Packages
- All definition and usage elements

###### Global namespace

A global namespace acts as a container for all root namespaces within a single project, including all SysML model libraries. There is no syntax for the global namespace, thus it is not represented in graphical or textual notation. In the modeling tool, the global namespace is represented by the top-level <project_name> package. Elements within any root namespace can refer to top-level elements of any other root namespace without qualification, as root namespaces themselves are not named. The global namespace essentially encapsulates all root namespaces and their elements, providing a mechanism for cross-reference among them.

###### Root namespace

A root namespace is defined as a namespace without an owner. It contains top-level elements, and every element that is not a root namespace must belong to an ownership tree of a top-level element in some root namespace. The declaration of a root namespace is implicit within SysML, and its content is represented by its top-level elements. Root namespaces are sorted by creation date.The root namespace is automatically named after the initial package created for it and cannot be renamed.For a procedure on root namespace creation, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing elements' space=''] page.

###### Package

A package is a specific type of namespace designed for organizing and grouping elements within a model. It serves as a container that can include both structural and behavioral elements, facilitating modular design and reuse. Packages can be used to structure the model in a hierarchical manner, enabling better organization and management of model elements. This approach allows the logical grouping of related elements, improving the clarity and maintainability of the system model.In the modeling tool, a package element can only be created for aroot namespace within the Containment tree or via Textual Editor. For procedures on package management, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Package' space=''] page.

##### Elements and relationships

The following concepts apply to all model elements.

###### Elements

- Every model element is assigned an element ID, which remains unchanged from the element's creation until its deletion, even if the element properties change.
- Every model element may have one or more alias IDs relative to one or more namespaces.

###### Relationships

- A relationship is a kind of element that relates two or more model elements.
- Relationships can be:
  - Binary - relating exactly two elements.
  - Non-binary - relating more than two elements.
  - Directed - a relationship that is directed from source to target.
  - Undirected - a relationship within which all elements are targets without any source elements.
- A relationship may also serve as the source or target of other relationships.
- There are several kinds of relationships:
  - Specializations : [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Subclassification' space=''] , [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Feature typing' space=''] , [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Subsetting' space=''] , [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Reference subsetting' space=''] , [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Redefinition' space='']
  - Connectors : [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Connection' space=''] , [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Interface' space=''] , [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Flow' space=''] , [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Succession' space=''] , [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Succession flow' space=''] , [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Binding' space=''] , [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Allocation' space=''] , [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Derive requirement' space='']
  - Other relationships : [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Annotations' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Dependency' space=''] , [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Feature membership' space='']

##### Definition and Usage

In SysML v2, definition and usage are the primary constructs facilitating reusability for different kinds of elements (requirements, use cases, interfaces, ports, attributes, etc.).

###### Definition

A Definition element classifies the element kind (requirement, use case, etc.).In the modeling tool's graphical notation, definitions are always represented as rectangles. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

In the modeling tool's Textual Editor, a definition is represented by the keyword def.

part defVehicle;

###### Usage

A Usage element is a usage of a definition element within a specific context.A usage must be defined/typed by at least one definition that corresponds in kind (requirement usage defined/typed by requirement definition).If the usage is undefined by the user, it is implicitly defined by the appropriate kind defined in the Systems Library.In the modeling tool's graphical notation, usages are always represented as rectangles with rounded corners. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

In the modeling tool's Textual Editor, usage has no keyword. It is represented simply by typing the element kind (e.g., requirement, use case, etc.).

partengine:Vehicle;

###### Features

A definition can own usage elements, which are referred to as its features.A usage can also own other usage elements as features.

###### Graphical compartment and body

An element may contain other elements, which can be represented in the:

- Graphical compartments (graphical notation). The graphical compartment(s) is displayed in the owning element's symbol. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
- Body (textual notation). The body is bound by curly braces, e.g., {...}. requirement defMassLimitReq{ 
doc/* The actual mass shall be...*/ 
}

##### Specialization relationships

SysML v2 provides specialization relationships used to specialize definition and usage elements. Specialization lets modelers build specific elements by inheriting features from more general ones. They can then tailor or adjust these features to fit different situations.Below is a list of various types of specialization relationships:

**SubclassificationSubclassification**: used to specialize a definition element. See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Subclassification' space=''] page to learn more.

**Feature TypingFeature typing**: specifies a definition for a usage. See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Feature typing' space=''] page to learn more.

**SubsettingSubsetting**: used to specialize a usage element. Inherited features can be subsetted by multiple features. See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Subsetting' space=''] page to learn more.

**Reference SubsettingReference subsetting**: is a kind of subsetting relationship. While a subsetting usage can have multiple subsetted usages, a reference subsetting usage can only have one specific subsetted usage. See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Reference subsetting' space=''] page to learn more.

**RedefinitionRedefinition**: is a kind of subsetting used to replace an inherited feature by redefining. See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Redefinition' space=''] page to learn more.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249576728 space=SYSML2P version=16 -->
## PAGE 00613: Libraries & samples

- page_id: `249576728`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/249576728/Libraries+samples
- version_number: 16
- version_date: `2026-02-17T18:49:32.878+01:00`
- ancestors: SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > Project management > Modeling tool environment
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### Libraries

Each SysML v2 project is automatically loaded with used projects:

- Standard Libraries. It contains standard SysML v2 libraries.
- *3DS SysML Customization*. It contains concepts designed for view creation and customization, symbol style customization, creation dialogs' customization, and model validation. 3DS SysML Customization structure[IMAGE alt='' src='']**<DS_Functions> DassaultSystemesFunctions**.Contains various functions used for customization elements.**<DS_Styles> DassaultSystemesStyles****CoreStylesComponents**Contains style sheet modeling and configuration concepts.**KerMLStyles**.Contains primary style definitions that can be reused (subclassified) for style sheet rules.**SysMLStyles**.Contains compartment style view definitions for style sheet rules.**Predicates**.Contains condition part definitions for style sheet rules.**StyleSheets**.Contains predefined stylesheets.**ColorDefinitions**.Contains color definitionsfor style sheet rules.**CompartmentVisibilityDefinitions**.Contains compartment visibility definitions for style sheet rules.**<DS_UIComponents> DassaultSystemesUIComponents****CoreUIComponents****Operations**.Contains concepts for view palette creation and Creation dialog customization.**Components**.Contains concepts for view palette creation and Creation dialog customization.**Palette**.Contains conceptsfor view palettecreation.**Dialogs**.Contains conceptsfor Creation dialog customization.**UIComponents**.Contains a predefined view creation dialog.**ViewsTemplates**.Contains model elements used to define the actions of templated buttons for creation dialogs, which in turn duplicate the template into the model.**<DS_Views> DassaultSystemesViews** 
**CoreViews**. Contains abstract view definitions for symbolic and tabular view definition creation; tabular view column and cell modeling concepts, their rendering definitions and usages; style sheet activation concepts.**SymbolicViews**.Contains predefined symbolic views.**SymbolicViewsByExpression**.Contains predefined symbolic views by expression.**TabularViews**.Contains predefined tabular views.**ViewPalettes**.Contains predefined view palettes.**CodeActionIdentifiers**.Containsaction IDs for the predefined buttons used in the symbolic view palettes of all predefined symbolic view definitions. They can also be used for custom view palettes. The predefined buttons contain names, descriptions, and actions for specific element creation.**ElementTemplates**. Contains certain element templates for view palettes.**<DS_Validation>DassaultSystemesValidation****CoreValidationComponents.**Contains concepts for creating custom validation suites and validation rules.******Suites.**Contains predefined validation suite instances.**<DS_Collaborator>DassaultSystemesCollaborator**. Contains customization concepts for MagicLab.

##### Samples

You can review the SysML v2 project samples provided with the installation, found at:

- <modeling tool installation directory>\samples

###### Opening sample projects

To open a sample project

1. In the Main toolbar, select Help , in the dropdown menu, click Show Samples . A new tab opens displaying all available sample projects.
2. Click an appropriate sample project under the **SysML v2** section. Use the shortcut keys Ctrl+F to open the **Find** dialog. Enter the desired keyword to locate specific samples. The sample project is opened.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=306282965 space=SYSML2P version=9 -->
## PAGE 00614: Local repository information

- page_id: `306282965`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/306282965/Local+repository+information
- version_number: 9
- version_date: `2026-06-05T14:06:36.733+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > Project management > Working with projects > Managing local projects
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Locally saved projects are stored in the local repository within the modeling tool’s configuration files. You can access this repository in your file system; however, the projects are saved in a format readable only by the modeling tool and should be opened only as described in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing projects in the local repository' space=''] procedure.

##### Accessing the configuration files

To access the local depository within the modeling tool’s configuration files in your file system

1. In the modeling tool's main menu, go to Help > About <modeling tool name> .
2. In the Environment tab, click the file path next to Configuration Files . The opened repository contains the localRepository with the local projects (e.g., ...\AppData\Local\.<modeling tool name>\<modeling tool version>\localRepository ).

##### Local repository instances

Generally, there is one local repository per modeling tool installation. However, running multiple instances of the same modeling tool at once creates additional local repository instances in your file system if it uses the same location for configuration files.

If the original location for the local repository is already used by a running instance of the modeling tool, an additional local repository instance is created in the configuration files for each additional running instance. The instances correspond to the number of applications you run at once and the order in which they are launched:

- The location of the original local repository instance is ***localRepository**, (* e.g., ...\AppData\Local\.<modeling tool name>\<modeling tool version>\localRepository).
- The second instance's location is ***instance_1**\localRepository*, ( e.g., ...\AppData\Local\.<modeling tool name>\<modeling tool version>\instance_1\localRepository).
- The third instance's location is ***instance_2**\localRepository*, ( e.g., ...\AppData\Local\.<modeling tool name>\<modeling tool version>\instance_2\localRepository), etc.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

In such a case, the following notification is displayed in the modeling tool.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

However, running multiple instances of the same modeling tool at once creates additional local repository instances in your file system if it uses the same location for configuration correspond to</span> the number of applications you run at once and the order in which they are launched:</span></span></p><ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The location of the style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">instance_1\</span>localRepository).</em></li><li data-uuid="140d56b6-e0c6-49ec-bbc2-8b31e9e6d3b3"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The third instance's <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249576737 space=SYSML2P version=4 -->
## PAGE 00615: Main menu

- page_id: `249576737`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/249576737/Main+menu
- version_number: 4
- version_date: `2025-12-17T14:25:18.617+01:00`
- ancestors: SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > Project management > Modeling tool environment > User interface
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The main menu located at the top of the modeling tool window contains the following options:

Currently, multiple commands displayed in the dropdown menus of the main menu options that are listed below are disabled.

| Menu option | Description |
| --- | --- |
| File | Contains basic project management-related commands, e.g., Local Repository, Open Project, Save Project, etc. |
| Edit | Use this menu when working with views in the view pane. The Edit menu contains the commands for selecting, cutting, copying, and pasting symbols. |
| View | Contains commands allowing you to zoom in or zoom out of a view and of your modeling tool. |
| Layout | Use this menu for managing the layout of the symbols on an active view. |
| Options | Use this menu to specify project options and environment options. |
| Tools | Use this menu to access the Patch tool. |
| Analyze | At the moment, the commands provided in this menu are not available in SysML v2. |
| Collaborate | Contains the commands for working with server projects. |
| Window | Allows you to manage the layout and display of various windows in your modeling tool. |
| Help | Among other commands, this menu allows you to open online documentation, manage resources, and access modeling tool-related information. |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249577174 space=SYSML2P version=11 -->
## PAGE 00616: Managing elements

- page_id: `249577174`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/249577174/Managing+elements
- version_number: 11
- version_date: `2025-10-14T14:38:03.763+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > Element management > Working with elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

This page outlines the basic common procedures regarding element management.

#### NOTE: Useful information for element management

Useful information for element management

- For more information on working with the Textual Editor and textual notation, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Textual Editor' space=''] section.
- For a comprehensive list of SysML v2 elements, their textual notation keywords, and relevant procedures, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model composition' space=''] section.

##### Declaring a root namespace

A root namespace contains the model elements. It does not have textual representation. For more information, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Key concepts' space=''] page.

To declare a root namespace

1. In the Containment tree, right-click the global namespace (by default, the global namespace name is the same as the project name).
2. In the shortcut menu, select Create Namespace .

##### Creating an element

The procedures below describe the basic options for creating the majority of the model elements. For more information, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Key concepts' space=''] page.

###### Creating an element via the Textual Editor

To create an element via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, type the keyword for the element you want to create followed by its name. partflashlight;
2. Click the Synchronize button.

###### Creating an element via the Create Element command

To create an element via the Create Element command

1. In the Containment tree, right-click an element, and in the shortcut menu, click **Create Element**. The **Create Element**dialog is displayed.
2. In the Create Element dialog, select the element you want to create.
3. Type the name for the newly created element.

###### Creating an element via the view palette

To create an element via the view palette

1. In the View Palette, click on the element you want to create.
2. Hover the cursor over the V iew Pane and click on the location where you want the new element to be created.
3. Type the name for the newly created element.

###### Creating an element via the smart manipulator in a view

Creating an element via the smart manipulator is only available for a limited selection of elements (e.g., ports, connectors).

To create an element via the smart manipulator in a view

- In a view , select a symbol, and in its smart manipulator, click the command for the needed element.

###### Creating an element via the symbol compartment in a view

To create an element via the symbol compartment in a view

- See the procedure [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Compartments' space=''] on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Compartments' space=''] page.

###### Creating an element via the Specification panel

To create an element via the Specification panel

- Do one of the following:
  - See the procedure [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification panel' space=''] on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification panel' space=''] page.
  - See the procedure [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification panel' space=''] on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification panel' space=''] page.

###### Creating an element via the Drag and Drop handler in a view

To create an element via the Drag and Drop handler in a view

- See the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Drag and Drop handler' space='']page.

###### Auto-creating an element via the Extract Definition or Extract Usage commands

To auto-create an element via the Extract Definition or Extract Usage commands

- See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Extract Definition and Usage' space='']page.

###### Creating a relation via the Create Relation command

To create a relation via the Create Relation command

1. Right-click element(s) or element symbol(s) for which you want to create relation(s).
2. In the shortcut menu, click Create Relation , then in the submenu, under the Incoming or Outgoing group, click the relation you want to create. The Create... dialog opens.
3. In the Create... dialog, do one of the following:
  1. In a view , click the element symbol. Hold Shift to select multiple elements.
  2. Click the Add [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. The element selection dialog opens. In the element selection dialog, select the element(s) and click OK .
  3. Drag and drop the needed element(s) from the Containment tree onto the Create... dialog.
4. In the Create... dialog, click OK . The specified relation is created between the selected elements.

##### Copying, pasting, and cutting an element

Elements cannot be copied and pasted between separate SysML v2 projects.

###### Copying an element via the Textual Editor

To copy an element via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, select the element you want to copy and do one of the following:
  1. Right-click the selection and in the shortcut menu click the Copy command.
  2. Press Ctrl+C on the keyboard.

###### Copying an element via the Containment tree

To copy an element via the Containment tree

1. In the Containment tree, select an element you want to copy and do one of the following:
  1. Right-click the element and in the shortcut menu click the Copy command.
  2. In the main menu, click Edit > Copy .
  3. Press Ctrl+C on the keyboard.
  4. Press Ctrl , then drag and drop the selected element to the element where you want the selected one to be copied and pasted.

###### Copying an element symbol in a view

To copy an element symbol in a view

1. In a view , select an element symbol you want to copy and do one of the following:
  1. In the view toolbar, click the Copy [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button.
  2. In the main menu, click Edit > Copy .
  3. Press Ctrl+C on the keyboard.

###### Pasting an element via the Textual Editor

To paste an element via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to paste the copied notation and do one of the following:
  1. Right-click and in the shortcut menu click the Paste command.
  2. Press Ctrl+V on the keyboard.

###### Pasting an element via the Containment tree

To paste an element via the Containment tree

1. In the Containment tree, select an element where you want to paste the copied element and do one of the following:
  1. Right-click the element and in the shortcut menu click the Paste command.
  2. In the main menu, click Edit > Paste .
  3. Press Ctrl+V on the keyboard.

###### Pasting an element or its symbol via a view

To paste an element or its symbol via a view

1. In a view , select an element symbol for which you want to paste the copied element symbol and do one of the following:
  1. In the view toolbar, click the Paste [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button to paste the copied symbol without creating a new element.
  2. In the main menu, click one of the following:
    1. Click Edit > Paste to paste the copied symbol without creating a new element.
    2. Click Edit > Paste with New Element to paste the copied symbol and create a new element for it.
  3. Press one of the following shortcut keys:
    1. Press Ctrl+V to to paste the copied symbol without creating a new element.
    2. Press Ctrl+E to paste the copied symbol and create a new element for it.

###### Cutting an element via the Textual Editor

To cut an element via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, select the element you want to cut and do one of the following:
  1. Right-click the selection and in the shortcut menu click the Cut command.
  2. Press Ctrl+X on the keyboard.

###### Cutting an element via the Containment tree

To cut an element via the Containment tree

1. In the Containment tree, select an element you want to cut and do one of the following:
  1. Right-click the element and in the shortcut menu click the Cut command.
  2. In the main menu, click Edit > Cut .
  3. Press Ctrl+X on the keyboard.

##### Deleting an element

###### Deleting an element via the Textual Editor

To delete an element via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, select the element keyword and its notational text. partflashlight;
2. On the keyboard, press either one of the following keys:
  1. Delete key.
  2. Backspace key.
3. Click the Synchronize button.

###### Deleting an element via the Containment tree

To delete an element via the Containment tree

1. In the Containment tree, select an element you want to delete and do one of the following:
  1. Right-click the element and in the shortcut menu click the Delete command.
  2. In the main menu, click Edit > Delete .
  3. Press Delete key or Ctrl+D on the keyboard.

###### Deleting an element or its symbol via a view

To delete an element or its symbol via a view

1. In a view , select an element symbol you want to delete and do one of the following:
  1. In the view toolbar, click one of the following:
    1. Delete button to delete the element from the model.
    2. Remove from Diagram button to remove the element symbol from the view .
  2. In the main menu, click one of the following:
    1. Click Edit > Delete to delete the element from the model.
    2. Click Edit > Remove from Diagram to remove the element symbol from the view .
  3. Press one of the following shortcut keys:
    1. Press Ctrl+D to delete the element from the model.
    2. Press Delete to remove the element symbol from the view .

###### Deleting an element via the compartment in the Specification panel

To delete an element via the compartment in the Specification panel

- See the procedure [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification panel' space=''] on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification panel' space=''] page.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="fcb9798f-fecd-44c9-9373-59d5b7ffd529">This page outlines the basic common procedures regarding element management.</ac:inline-comment-marker></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0d4c9d44-c854-46f1-bca3-5e0393a3ac3d"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Useful information for element management</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>For more information on working with the Textual Editor and textual notation, see the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Textual Editor" /></ac:link> section.</li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">For a comprehensive list of SysML v2 elements, their textual notation keywords, and relevant procedures, see the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Model composition" /></ac:link> section. </span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Declaring a root namespace</h3><p>A root namespace contains the model elements. <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="5cb6fb26-fbf4-4e63-9fbc-4fc9af17e447">It does not have</ac:inline-comment-marker> textual representation. For more information, see the <ac:link ac:anchor="Root namespace"><ri:page ri:content-title="Key concepts" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Key concepts]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> page.</p><p>To declare a root namespace</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Containment tree, right-click the global namespace (by default, the global namespace name is the same as the project name).</li><li>In the shortcut menu, select <strong>Create Namespace</strong>.</li></ol><h3>Creating an element</h3><p>The procedures below describe the basic options for creating the majority of the model elements. <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">For more information, see the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Key concepts" /></ac:link> page.</span></p><h4>Creating an element via the Textual Editor</h4><p>To create an element via the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Textual Editor</span></p><hr /><ol><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the Textual Editor, type the keyword for the element you want to create followed by its name.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="802b05e2-76b4-4c33-bb2a-fa228b066a7c"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">flashlight</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>Click the <strong>Synchronize </strong>button. </li></ol><h4>Creating an element via the Create Element command</h4><p>To create an element via the Create Element command</p><hr /><ol><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the Containment tree, right-click an element, and in the shortcut menu, click <strong>Create Element</strong>. The <strong>Create Element </strong>dialog is displayed.</p></li><li>In the <strong>Create Element</strong> dialog, select the element you want to create.</li><li>Type the name for the newly created element.</li></ol><h4>Creating an element via the view palette</h4><p>To create an element via the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">view</span> palette</p><hr /><ol><li><p <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272741885 space=SYSML2P version=22 -->
````
## PAGE 00617: Managing local projects

- page_id: `272741885`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/272741885/Managing+local+projects
- version_number: 22
- version_date: `2026-05-08T13:09:22.511+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > Project management > Working with projects
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Local projects are managed via the local repository instead of working with individual files. The projects are stored in the local repository within the modeling tool’s configuration files. The local repository serves as a central storage for all your projects, their dependencies, and standard libraries. This makes it easier to keep track of your models, review your work, and smoothly transition between local and server projects. It also provides a more robust format for exchanging models in air-gapped environments between servers.

When you open a project from the file system, the tool first imports it into the local repository. After the import completes, the tool opens the project copy from the local repository, not the file in your file system. The tool handles sample projects in the same way. As a result, every project you create, open, or import is stored in the local repository, allowing you to view and modify it.

The projects can be saved in an**.mdszip format**, where a single file contains both the project and its used projects. This file is intended only for importing projects into your local repository or server. Once imported, all ongoing work is saved directly in the repository. You only need to export to a file again if you want to share your project or create a backup.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### The Manage Projects dialog

The Manage Project dialog allows you to manage projects in the local repository. It displays all the projects in the local repository and allows you to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing projects in the local repository' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing projects in the local repository' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing projects in the local repository' space=''], , , and[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing project categories in the local repository' space=''] your projects.

- The Name column in the Manage Projects dialog displays projects and project categories. Additionally, projects are displayed with the information about their [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing project imports in the local repository' space=''] .
- The Last modified column displays the last project modification date and time.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Opening the Manage Projects dialog

To open the Manage Projects dialog

- In the main menu, click File > Local Repository . The Manage Projects dialog opens.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

The projects are stored in the local repository within the modeling tool’s configuration files. This makes it easier to keep track of your models, review your work, and smoothly transition between local and server projects. After the import completes, the tool opens the project copy from the local repository, not the file in your file system. The tool handles sample projects in the same way. This file is intended only for importing projects into your local repository or server. Once imported, all ongoing work is saved directly in the repository. You only need to export to a file again if you want to share your project or create a backup.</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:width="1000"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Local_repository_2026xHF1.png" /></ac:image><br /></span></p><p><br /></p><h3>The Manage Projects dialog</h3><p style="text-align: left;">The Manage Project dialog allows you to manage projects in the local repository. It displays all the projects in the local repository and allows you to <ac:link ac:anchor="Creating projects in the local repository"><ri:page ri:content-title="Managing projects in the local repository" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[create]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, <ac:link ac:anchor="Opening projects from the local repository"><ri:page ri:content-title="Managing projects in the local repository" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[open]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, <ac:link ac:anchor="Importing projects to the local repository"><ri:page ri:content-title="Managing projects in the local repository" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[import]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, <ac:link ac:anchor="Removing projects from the local repository"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[remove]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, <ac:link ac:anchor="Renaming projects in the local repository"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[rename]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, and<span> </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Managing project categories in the local repository" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[categorize]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> your projects.</p><ul><li style="text-align: left;" data-uuid="e2283cdd-eeae-41d7-9d66-8cb69fb30022">The <strong>Name </strong>column in the <strong>Manage Projects</strong> dialog displays projects and project categories. Additionally, projects are displayed with the information about their <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Managing project imports in the local repository" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[imports]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li style="text-align: left;" data-uuid="df6ff85b-f58f-4d17-bd01-44bf7e990c66">The <strong>Last modified</strong> column displays the last project modification date and time.</li></ul><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Manage_projects_dialog_2026xHF1.png" /></ac:image></p><h4>Opening the Manage Projects dialog</h4><p>To open the Manage Projects dialog</p><hr /><ul><li data-uuid="85d97ec5-3e79-4b54-a743-8e62a1bb9d29">In the main menu, click <strong>File </strong>&gt; <strong>Local Repository</strong>. The <strong>Manage Projects </strong>dialog opens.</li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272741938 space=SYSML2P version=32 -->
## PAGE 00618: Managing local used projects

- page_id: `272741938`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/272741938/Managing+local+used+projects
- version_number: 32
- version_date: `2026-05-08T13:16:24.191+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > Project management > Working with projects
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

It is advisable to split large projects with loosely dependent parts into used projects. Project partitioning provides additional benefits, such as reusing model parts (e.g., libraries) across multiple projects and improving performance when working with very large models. The used projects are always added from the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing local projects' space='']; thus, the projects must first be added there to be used in other projects. Once added to the main project, you can view the used projects under the Containment tree's Used Projects node.

You can manage used projects via the Containment tree, the **Project Usages**dialog, and the **Advanced Project Usages Configuration** dialog.

There can be two kinds of used projects:

- *Directly used projects*: projects used directly by the main project.
- *Indirectly used projects*: projects used by other used projects. For more information, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Indirectly used projects' space=''] subsection.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### The Project Usages dialog

The Project Usages dialog allows you to manage projects used from the local repository. You can [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using projects from the local repository' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Updating used projects to their latest versions' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Opening used projects' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Removing used projects' space=''] a used project, and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Switching between the original used project and its subsequent imports' space='']. Selecting a used project in the project usages tree displays the project's details (e.g., version, location) on the right side of the dialog.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Opening the Project Usages dialog

To open the Project Usages dialog

1. Do one of the following:
  1. In the main menu, select Options > Project Usages .
  2. In the main menu, select Collaborate > Project Usages > Project Usages .
  3. In the Containment tree, expand the Used Projects node and right-click a used project. In the shortcut menu, click Options . The Project Usages dialog opens.

##### The Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog

The Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog allows you to manage projects used from the local repository. You can [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using projects from the local repository' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Updating used projects to their latest versions' space=''], a used project, and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Switching between the original used project and its subsequent imports' space=''] of a used project. You can also review the information about the version and the imports of all used projects.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Opening the Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog

To open the Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog

1. Open the **Project Usages** dialog .
2. In the Project Usages dialog's toolbar, click the Usages Configuration [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. The Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog opens.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>It is advisable to split large projects with loosely dependent parts into used projects. Project partitioning provides additional benefits, such as reusing model parts (e.g., libraries) across multiple projects and improving performance when working with very large models. The used projects are always added from the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Managing local projects" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[local repository]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>; thus, the projects must first be added there to be used in other projects. Once added to the main project, you can view the used projects under the Containment tree's Used Projects node. </p><p>You can manage used projects via the Containment tree, <ac:link ac:anchor="The Project Usages dialog"><ac:link-body>the <strong>Project Usages </strong>dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link>, and <ac:link ac:anchor="The Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog"><ac:link-body>the <strong>Advanced Project Usages Configuration</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link>.</p><p>There can be two kinds of used projects:</p><ul><li><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"><em>Directly used projects</em>: projects used directly by the main project.<br /></span></li><li data-uuid="8731e327-e94a-4ce8-a9bf-11d0b15b2241"><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"><em>Indirectly used projects</em>: projects used by other used projects. For more information, see the </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Indirectly used projects" /></ac:link><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> subsection.</span></li></ul><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Used Projects node in the Containment tree.png" /></ac:image></p><h3>The Project Usages dialog</h3><p>The Project Usages dialog allows you to manage projects used from the local repository. You can <ac:link ac:anchor="Using a project from the local repository via the Project Usages dialog"><ri:page ri:content-title="Using projects from the local repository" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[use]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, <ac:link ac:anchor="Updating a used project to its latest version via the Project Usages dialog"><ri:page ri:content-title="Updating used projects to their latest versions" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[update]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, <ac:link ac:anchor="Opening a used project via the Project Usages dialog"><ri:page ri:content-title="Opening used projects" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[open]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, <ac:link ac:anchor="Removing a used project via the Project Usages dialog"><ri:page ri:content-title="Removing used projects" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[remove]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> a used project, and <ac:link ac:anchor="Switching between the original used project and its subsequent imports via the Project Usages dialog"><ri:page ri:content-title="Switching between the original used project and its subsequent imports" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[switch between the original used project and its subsequent imports]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. Selecting a used project in the project usages tree displays the project's details (e.g., version, location) on the right side of the dialog.</p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="The Project Usages dialog.png" /></ac:image></p><h4>Opening the Project Usages dialog</h4><p>To open the Project Usages dialog</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="7e600da9-6479-4977-85a9-aa4216b9f102">Do one of the following:<ol><li>In the main menu, select <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Project Usages</strong>.</li><li>In the main menu, select <strong>Collaborate</strong> &gt; <strong>Project Usages </strong>&gt; <strong>Project Usages</strong>.</li><li>In the Containment tree, expand the <strong>Used Projects </strong>node and right-click a used project. In the shortcut menu, click <strong>Options</strong>.<br />The <strong>Project Usages</strong> dialog opens.</li></ol></li></ol><h3>The Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog</h3><p>The Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog allows you to manage projects used from the local repository. You can <ac:link ac:anchor="Using a project from the local repository via the Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog"><ri:page ri:content-title="Using projects from the local repository" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[use (and undo this command)]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, <ac:link ac:anchor="Updating a used project to its latest version via the Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog"><ri:page ri:content-title="Updating used projects to their latest versions" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[update]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, <ac:link ac:anchor="Removing a used project via the Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[remove (and undo this command)]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> a used project, and <ac:link ac:anchor="Switching between the original used project and its subsequent imports via the Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog"><ri:page ri:content-title="Switching between the original used project and its subsequent imports" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[switch between the original used project and its imports]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> of a used project. You can also review the information about the version and the imports of all used projects.</p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="The Advanced Usages Configuration dialog.png" /></ac:image></p><h4>Opening the Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog</h4><p>To open the Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="653adb75-bc64-445c-87c4-0acfe4338636"><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Project Usages dialog"><ac:link-body>Open the <strong>Project Usages</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link>. </li><li data-uuid="ad169f26-2f7e-4642-a7e5-6da6e9653ca1">In the <strong>Project Usages </strong>dialog's toolbar, click the <strong>Usages Configuration <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="usages_configurations_button.png" /></ac:image> </strong>button. <br />The <strong>Advanced Project Usages Configuration</strong> dialog opens.</li></ol><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=306282943 space=SYSML2P version=3 -->
## PAGE 00619: Managing project categories in the local repository

- page_id: `306282943`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/306282943/Managing+project+categories+in+the+local+repository
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2026-05-08T13:13:15.962+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > Project management > Working with projects > Managing local projects
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Project categories in the local repository help you organize and structure your local projects, providing a more efficient project management experience. You can create, delete, rename, and move projects between them. Category management commands are available in the toolbar of the **Manage Projects** dialog and the shortcut menus of categories and projects.

The*Standard Libraries*category is pre-populated with the SysML v2 standard libraries required for modeling. It is created automatically when the SysML v2 Plugin is first launched and cannot be edited or deleted.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The project categories are highlighted in the image.

###### Creating new project categories in the local repository

To create new project categories in the local repository

1. Open the **Manage Projects**dialog and do one of the following:
  1. In the dialog's toolbar, click the Create Category [ATTACHMENT filename='create category button.png'] button. The Create Category dialog opens.
  2. Right-click a category and in the shortcut menu, click the Create Category command. The Create Category dialog opens.
2. In the dialog, specify the parent category and the name for the new category, then click OK .

###### Deleting project categories in the local repository

To delete project categories in the local repository

1. Open the **Manage Projects**dialog and do one of the following:
  1. Select the needed category and in the dialog's toolbar, click the Delete Category [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. The Delete Category question dialog opens.
  2. Right-click a category and in the shortcut menu, click the Delete Category command. The Delete Category question dialog opens.
2. In the Delete Category dialog, click Yes to confirm the deletion.

###### Renaming project categories in the local repository

To rename project categories in the local repository

1. Open the **Manage Projects**dialog and do one of the following:
  1. Select the needed category and in the dialog's toolbar, click the Rename Category [ATTACHMENT filename='rename category button.png'] button. The Rename Category dialog opens.
  2. Right-click a category and in the shortcut menu, click the Rename Category command. The Rename Category dialog opens.
2. In the Rename Category dialog, specify the new name and click OK .

###### Moving projects to other project categories in the local repository

Moving the original project to another project category in the local repository moves all of its subsequent imports, and vice versa. Learn more in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing project imports in the local repository' space=''] page.

To move projects to other project categories in the local repository

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing projects in the local repository' space='']Open the **Manage Projects**dialog and do one of the following:
  1. Select the needed project and in the dialog's toolbar, click the Move To Category [ATTACHMENT filename='move to category button.png'] button. The Move To Category dialog opens.
  2. Right-click the project and in the shortcut menu, click the Move To Category command. The Move To Category dialog opens.
2. In the Move To Category dialog, select the needed category and click OK .

###### Managing empty project categories in the local repository

To manage empty project categories in the local repository

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing projects in the local repository' space='']Open the **Manage Projects**dialog .
2. In the dialog's toolbar, click the Show empty categories [ATTACHMENT filename='Show empty categories button.png'] button to enable or disable the display of empty categories.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Project categories in the local repository help you organize and structure your local projects, providing a more efficient project management experience. You can create, delete, rename, and move projects between them. Category management commands are available in the toolbar of the <strong>Manage Projects</strong> dialog and the shortcut menus of categories and projects.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ae7eef1c-6c85-4c82-b820-b9265d06c858"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The<span> </span><em>Standard Libraries</em><span> </span>category is pre-populated with the SysML v2 standard libraries required for modeling. The <strong>Delete Category </strong>question dialog opens. </li><li data-uuid="08d8f53f-f461-4cd5-ac1e-5db06f089abf">Right-click a category and in the shortcut menu, click the <strong>Delete Category </strong>command. The <strong>Delete Category </strong>question dialog opens. </li></ol></li><li data-uuid="946d2d08-2b5a-4b2d-b750-73b6c12b81e3">In the <strong>Delete Category </strong>dialog, click <strong>Yes </strong>to confirm the deletion.</li></ol><h4>Renaming project categories in the local repository</h4><p>To rename project categories in the local repository</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="fe96a45c-728b-4af1-9e92-9a37c7b3ca8c"><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Manage Projects dialog"><ac:link-body>Open the <strong>Manage Projects </strong>dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> and do one of the following: <ol><li data-uuid="89d0df79-e1d5-4e87-8b4d-e534217baac3">Select the needed category and in the dialog's toolbar, click the <strong>Rename Category</strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="rename category button.png" /></ac:image> button. The <strong>Rename </strong><strong>Category</strong><strong> </strong>dialog opens.</li><li data-uuid="36e28752-59cd-46ac-83c5-0d7c452b10bb">Right-click a category and in the shortcut menu, click the <strong>Rename Category</strong> command. The <strong>Rename </strong><strong>Category</strong><strong> </strong>dialog opens.</li></ol></li><li data-uuid="cea5f60c-bad1-4d94-a458-1cc60261d57e">In the <strong>Rename </strong><strong>Category</strong><strong> </strong>dialog, specify the new name and click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol><h4>Moving projects to other project categories in the local repository</h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2ddbc4c8-3b0c-4931-804e-bbbae91126b4"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Moving the original project to another project category in the local repository moves all of its subsequent imports, and vice versa. Learn more in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Managing project imports in the local repository" /></ac:link> page.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To move projects to other project categories in the local repository</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="7a065692-e865-404c-a1df-d32a7629d177"><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Manage Projects dialog"><ri:page ri:content-title="Managing projects in the local repository" /><ac:link-body>Open the <strong>Manage Projects </strong>dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> and do one of the following:<ol><li data-uuid="eca0be86-c920-4901-993c-f460d9853825">Select the needed project and in the dialog's toolbar, click the <strong>Move To Category</strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="move to category button.png" /></ac:image> button. The <strong>Move To Category</strong><strong> </strong>dialog opens.</li><li data-uuid="bfedbfd9-e537-4483-9164-eeb13ff9b922">Right-click the project and in the shortcut menu, click the <strong>Move To Category</strong> command. The <strong>Move To Category</strong><strong> </strong>dialog opens.</li></ol></li><li data-uuid="bae0f61d-5f77-43cd-acfe-860c675844b2">In the <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Move To Category </strong>dialog, select the needed category and click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol><h4>Managing empty project categories in the local repository</h4><p>To manage empty project categories in the local repository</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="ed4fc3f2-d89a-4655-93c4-a9ec83852219"><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Manage Projects dialog"><ri:page ri:content-title="Managing projects in the local repository" /><ac:link-body>Open the <strong>Manage Projects </strong>dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link>.</li><li data-uuid="dd856de9-a5e3-4c55-a5ef-8976a0bb1230">In the dialog's toolbar, click the <strong>Show empty categories</strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Show empty categories button.png" /></ac:image> button to enable or disable the display of empty categories. </li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=306282920 space=SYSML2P version=3 -->
## PAGE 00620: Managing project imports in the local repository

- page_id: `306282920`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/306282920/Managing+project+imports+in+the+local+repository
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2026-05-08T13:12:14.501+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > Project management > Working with projects > Managing local projects
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Each originally created or imported project in the local repository may have multiple subsequent imports of the same project. For example, you might [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing projects in the local repository' space=''] a project from the local repository, share it with a colleague who makes changes while you continue working on the original, and then later [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing projects in the local repository' space=''] your colleague’s modified copy back into your local repository. In this case, the local repository will display both projects: the original and the subsequent import of the same project. You can modify both projects independently, and although they are displayed as separate projects, they are treated as distinct but still interdependent instances of the same project.

- The original project**** is the first instance of the project that exists in the local repository, which can be created in or imported into the local repository.
  - If the original project was created in the local repository, it is displayed only by indicating the project name.
  - If the original project was imported into the local repository, it is displayed using the following structure: [<file_name>.mdszip imported @<date and time of the import>] .
- Subsequent imports of the same project are any later instances of the project that are imported into the local repository.
  - Subsequent imports of the same project originate only by importing a project into the local repository. They are displayed using the following structure: [<file_name>.mdszip imported @<date and time of the import>] .

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing projects in the local repository' space=''] the original project in the local repository renames all of its subsequent imports, and vice versa.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing projects in the local repository' space=''] the original project from the local repository removes all subsequent imports of the same project. Removing any subsequent import of the same project does not affect the original project.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing project categories in the local repository' space=''] the original project to another project category in the local repository moves all of its subsequent imports, and vice versa.

You can view the project details in the **Manage Projects** dialog.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Each originally created or imported project in the local repository may have multiple subsequent imports of the same project. For example, you might <ac:link ac:anchor="Exporting projects from the local repository as .mdszip files"><ri:page ri:content-title="Managing projects in the local repository" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[export]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> a project from the local repository, share it with a colleague who makes changes while you continue working on the original, and then later <span><ac:link ac:anchor="Importing projects to the local repository"><ri:page ri:content-title="Managing projects in the local repository" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[import]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span> your colleague’s modified copy back into your local repository. In this case, the local repository will display both projects: the original and the subsequent import of the same project. <span>You can modify both projects independently, and although they are displayed as separate projects, they are treated as distinct but still interdependent instances of the same project. </span></span></p><ul><li>The <strong>original project<strong> </strong></strong>is the first instance of the project that exists in the local repository, which can be created in or imported into the local repository. <ul><li data-uuid="6d2344a0-8bbf-4b3f-ad6c-da695ec0f405">If the original project was created in the local repository, it is displayed only by indicating the project name. </li><li data-uuid="a0cfe82f-74bf-4858-b3cb-8aeca3cef2d3">If the original project was imported into the local repository, it is displayed using the following structure: <em>[&lt;file_name&gt;.mdszip imported @&lt;date and time of the import&gt;]</em>.</li></ul></li><li data-uuid="7e3f09e6-0e40-466b-bf11-f21b66c60206"><strong>Subsequent imports of the same project</strong> are any later instances of the project that are imported into the local repository.<ul><li data-uuid="e8b0764b-3f97-4d0a-9eb4-4fd3f9ac1221">Subsequent imports of the same project originate only by importing a project into the local repository. They are displayed using the following structure: <em>[&lt;file_name&gt;.mdszip imported @&lt;date and time of the import&gt;]</em>.</li></ul></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="23eef6fe-aa62-4b4e-a7ac-c2375bd7d749"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li data-uuid="508ee9cc-674c-43e2-bb5d-dc300f89e591"><ac:link ac:anchor="Renaming projects in the local repository"><ri:page ri:content-title="Managing projects in the local repository" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Renaming]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> the original project in the local repository renames all of its subsequent imports, and vice versa.</li><li data-uuid="f2e7ae5e-faba-49e9-af65-4ed168ea7f01"><ac:link ac:anchor="Removing projects from the local repository"><ri:page ri:content-title="Managing projects in the local repository" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Removing]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> the original project from the local repository removes all subsequent imports of the same project. Removing any subsequent import of the same project does not affect the original project.</li><li data-uuid="e04f2e61-e594-48d4-8c70-b394c1d5061d"><ac:link ac:anchor="Moving projects to other project categories in the local repository"><ri:page ri:content-title="Managing project categories in the local repository" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Moving]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> the original project to another project category in the local repository moves all of its subsequent imports, and vice versa.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>You can view the project details in the <strong>Manage Projects</strong> dialog.</p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="project_instances_2026xHF1.png" /></ac:image></p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=306282899 space=SYSML2P version=4 -->
## PAGE 00621: Managing projects in the local repository

- page_id: `306282899`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/306282899/Managing+projects+in+the+local+repository
- version_number: 4
- version_date: `2026-06-10T10:34:59.329+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > Project management > Working with projects > Managing local projects
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

See the procedures on this page for creating, opening, importing, removing, renaming, saving, and exporting local projects.

###### Creating projects in the local repository

Create projects in the local repository to create your models.

To create projects in the local repository

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing local projects' space='']Open the **Manage Projects**dialog .
2. In the dialog, click the New button. The New Project dialog opens.
3. In the dialog, specify the project name and category, then click OK .

###### Opening projects from the local repository

Open projects from the local repository to view and modify them in the tool.

To open projects from the local repository

1. Open the **Manage Projects**dialog .
2. In the dialog, do one of the following:
  1. Select the needed project and click the Open button.
  2. Double-click the needed projects.

###### Importing projects to the local repository

- The project from the file system is first imported into the local repository, and then opened from there. Therefore, the project file you open and work on in the tool is the project copy in the local repository, not the file in your file system.
- Imported projects are displayed in the local repository using the following structure: [<file_name>.mdszip imported @<date and time of the import>] .

To import projects to the local repository

Do one of the following:

- Open the **Manage Projects**dialog . In the dialog, click the Import button. The Open dialog opens. Select the needed file from the file system and click OK .
- In the main menu, click File > Open Project . In the Open dialog, select the project and click Open .
- In the main toolbar, click the Open Project button.
- On the keyboard, press Ctrl+O .
- Drag the project from the open window of your file system to the modeling tool. The project opens.

###### Removing projects from the local repository

Removing the original project from the local repository removes all subsequent imports of the same project. Removing any subsequent import of the same project does not affect the original project. See in the subsection.

To remove projects from the local repository

1. Open the **Manage Projects**dialog .
2. In the dialog, select the needed project and click the Remove button. The question dialog opens. Click the Delete button to confirm the removal of the project.

###### Renaming projects in the local repository

Renaming the original project in the local repository renames all of its subsequent imports, and vice versa. See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing project imports in the local repository' space=''] page.

To rename projects in the local repository

1. Open the **Manage Projects**dialog .
2. Select the needed project and click the Rename button. The Rename dialog opens.
3. In the dialog, update the project name and click OK .

###### Saving projects in the local repository

To save a local project in the local repository

Do one of the following:

1. In the main menu, click File > Save Project .
2. On the keyboard, press Ctrl+S.
3. In the main toolbar, click the Save Project button.

Use the **Change Sets** tab to track project modifications. The latest changes are compared against the latest project version saved in the local repository.

- The panel legend explains the modification markings.
- Click the Refresh icon to update the display.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Exporting projects from the local repository as .*mdszip* files

Export projects from the local repository as *.mdszip* files, which can be stored anywhere on your file system as a backup or shared with colleagues.

To export projects from the local repository as .*mdszip* files

1. In the main menu, click File > Save Project As . To export a project to a file, its changes must first be saved in the local repository. If it is not, clicking the **Save As Project** command opens a question dialog. Click **Save** in the dialog to save changes in the local repository and enable the project export to a file.
2. The Save dialog opens. Select the file location in the file system and click Save .

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE


<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249577758 space=SYSML2P version=43 -->
## PAGE 00622: Managing tabular view cells

- page_id: `249577758`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/249577758/Managing+tabular+view+cells
- version_number: 43
- version_date: `2026-05-06T21:23:12.086+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > Model composition > Views > Creating views > Tabular views
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Tabular views' space=''] cells are the intersections of every row and column, representing the appropriate element values. Tabular view cells may contain singular or multiple values.

You can modify a table cell either directly through the cell or via the **Review and Modify**dialog; however, the availability of these functionalities depends on the feature displayed by the cell's column. For more information, see the notes in and subsections.

Tabular view cells are modeled via the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Modeling tabular view columns' space=''].

##### Modifying a table cell directly via the cell

You can modify a table cell directly via the cell if it is a*[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Tabular view columns' space='']* column primitive-type cell (String, Integer, Boolean, Real) or a cell with Element or Enum values. E.g., declared name, ID, etc. It doesn't apply to cells listed in the note for the subsection.

###### Modifying a table cell value directly via the cell

To modify a table cell value directly via the cell

1. In a tabular view, double-click the cell whose value you want to modify.
2. Specify the new value.
3. Click elsewhere on the table to save the changes.

###### Modifying a table cell multiplicity value directly via the cell

To modify a table cell multiplicity value directly via the cell

1. In a tabular view, double-click the cell whose value you want to modify and do one of the following:
  1. Input the new value.
  2. Click the value field to display the dropdown menu with suggested applicable multiplicity values.
2. Click elsewhere on the table to save the changes. [ATTACHMENT filename='Modifying a table cell multiplicity value directly via the cell.png']

##### Managing cells via the Review and Modify dialog

You can review and modify all values in the cell through the **Review and Modify**dialog.

You can modify a table cell via the**Review and Modify**dialog if it is:

- Element-type cell of a column representing the following meta-features:
  - SysML::Definition::owned<ElementKind>, e.g., SysML::Definition::ownedAction
  - SysML::Usage::nested<ElementKind>, e.g., SysML::Usage::nestedAction
  - KerML::Element::ownedElement
  - KerML::Type::ownedFeature
  - KerML::Namespace::ownedMember
- Multi-primitive-type cell of a column representing the following meta-features:
  - KerML::Element::aliasIds (multi-string)
  - KerML::Element::documentation (multi-string) - allows you to edit the owned documentation body's text, not the documentation element itself. For example, creating a new string creates a new documentation element for the rowElement with the specified documentation body.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Opening the Review and Modify dialog

To open the Review and Modify dialog

Do one of the following:

- Double-click the cell or an element in the cell. The More button appears. Click the button. [ATTACHMENT filename='Opening the Review and Modify dialog 1.png']
- In a tabular view, click the +<number> more button at the bottom of the cell. Applicable only for multi-valued cells. [ATTACHMENT filename='Opening the Review and Modify dialog 2.png'] The Review and Modify dialog opens, displaying all values of the cell.

###### Creating new elements in multi-valued cells via the Review and Modify dialog

To create new elements in multi-valued cells via the Review and Modify dialog

1. for the multi-valued cell.
2. Click the Add New [ATTACHMENT filename='Review and Modify dialog Add New button.png'] button. The Create Element dialog opens.
3. In the dialog, click the needed element. The element is created in the cell at the bottom of the list.
4. Click OK . Otherwise, to discard changes, click Cancel .

###### Deleting elements in multi-valued cells via the Review and Modify dialog

To delete elements in multi-valued cells via the Review and Modify dialog

1. for the multi-valued cell.
2. In the numbering column, hover the cursor over the row number of the element you want to delete, and select the check box. You can select multiple elements.
  1. (Optional) To select all elements, select the check box in the header of the numbering column. [ATTACHMENT filename='Review and Modify dialog select element.png']
3. Click the Delete [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button.
4. Click OK . Otherwise, to discard changes, click Cancel .

###### Renaming elements in multi-valued cells via the Review and Modify dialog

To rename elements in multi-valued cells via the Review and Modify dialog

1. for the multi-valued cell.
2. Click the row of the element and rename as needed.
3. Click OK . Otherwise, to discard changes, click Cancel .

##### Managing multi-valued cells

Tabular view cells may contain multiple values. By default, multi-valued cells display up to 10 values. You can review and modify all values in the cell through the **Review and Modify**dialog.

###### Filtering elements in multi-valued cells via the Review and Modify dialog

To filter elements in multi-valued cells via the Review and Modify dialog

1. for the multi-valued cell.
2. At the top of the dialog, input the filter keyword. The filter updates as you type.
  1. To reset the filter, click the Clear [IMAGE alt='' src='']**** button.
3. Click OK or Cancel .

###### Reordering elements in multi-valued cells via the Review and Modify dialog

To reorder elements in multi-valued cells via the Review and Modify dialog

1. for the multi-valued cell.
2. In the numbering column, hover the cursor over the row number of the element you want to move and click the Move [IMAGE alt='' src=''] icon. Drag and drop the row element to the needed position. [ATTACHMENT filename='Review and Modify dialog move element.png']
3. Click OK . Otherwise, to discard changes, click Cancel .

###### Modifying the value count in multi-valued cells

By default, multi-valued cells display up to 10 values. You can modify the limit via the *maxDisplayedValues* attribute specified for the *DS_Views::CoreViews::Base Table*, which is reused by all predefined tabular views.

To modify the value count in multi-valued cells

1. Within the body of a view, declare an attribute redefining the maxDisplayedValues attribute and set the default limit.
2. Click the Synchronize button.

view'Drone Structure':DS_Views::TabularViews::gt{ 
exposeDroneStructure::**; 
filterKerML::Root::Element::isLibraryElement==false; 
attribute:>>maxDisplayedValuesdefault3;// the default value limit is set to 3 for the table's multi-value cells 
renderrendering:>>asTable{...} 
}

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Tabular view cell errors

You may encounter the following icons and buttons in tabular view column cells:

| Icon / Button | Message | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  | Click to load the cell. | Cell loading was canceled after exceeding 1000 ms. Click the button to restart loading; the modeling tool will wait until the cell has finished loading.If the table is only partially loaded and its data is modified (e.g., via the Containment tree, Textual Editor, etc.) without refreshing the table afterward, the icon is displayed in the cells that were not loaded. In this case, the cell cannot be reloaded; click the Refresh button in the table toolbar to refresh the entire table. |
|  | Failed to evaluate expression. | Resolve model errors related to the column expression. The error may also appear if the expression specified for the tabular view column does not apply to the specific element. |
|  | Filtering or sorting applied on partially loaded cells. | Tabular views load only the content that is immediately visible; additional rows and cell data are loaded as you scroll. If you filter or sort partially loaded column cells, a notification icon appears in the column header.To resolve the issue, see the Tabular view loading warning box in the subsection on the page. |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

