and do one of the following:<ol><li data-uuid="68a89be3-ef45-4840-87f0-f82d1c7688ea">Input the new value.</li><li data-uuid="529de0ee-382b-43a8-8f22-00ccc10dd842">Click the value field to display the dropdown menu with suggested applicable multiplicity values.</li></ol></li><li data-uuid="1b739330-1b98-43e2-a083-d8d31d485a32">Click elsewhere on the table to save the changes.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Modifying a table cell multiplicity value directly via the cell.png" /></ac:image></li></ol><h3>Managing cells via the Review and Modify dialog</h3><p>You can review and modify all values in the cell through the <strong>Review and Modify </strong>dialog.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5435340a-1fe5-4bf2-9523-137bc90118d4"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span>You can modify a table cell via the </span><strong>Review and Modify </strong>dialog if it is:</p><ul><li data-uuid="b362761d-0899-4387-8a2f-628943d227f2"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Element-type cell of a </span>column representing the following meta-features:<ul><li>SysML::Definition::owned&lt;ElementKind&gt;, e.g., SysML::Definition::ownedAction</li><li>SysML::Usage::nested&lt;ElementKind&gt;, e.g., SysML::Usage::nestedAction</li><li>KerML::Element::ownedElement</li><li>KerML::Type::ownedFeature</li><li>KerML::Namespace::ownedMember</li></ul></li><li data-uuid="1a5898a3-0ae8-4c8d-8a4a-cb04e7de32fe"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Multi-primitive-type cell </span>of a column representing the following meta-features:<ul><li>KerML::Element::aliasIds (multi-string)</li><li>KerML::Element::documentation (multi-string) - allows you to edit the owned documentation body's text, not the documentation element itself. For example, creating a new string creates a new documentation element for the <em>rowElement</em> with the specified documentation body.</li></ul></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Review and Modify dialog.png" /></ac:image></p><h4>Opening the Review and Modify dialog</h4><p>To open the Review and Modify dialog</p><hr /><p>Do one of the following:</p><ul><li data-uuid="44f5af1c-80fc-4be9-9ed4-4b68dcce9b66">Double-click the cell or an element in the cell. The <strong>More</strong> button appears. Click the button.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Opening the Review and Modify dialog 1.png" /></ac:image></li><li data-uuid="3d825b4a-ed1b-4529-aebe-23bfeabdb4b9">In a tabular view, click the <strong>+&lt;number&gt; more </strong>button at the bottom of the cell. Applicable only for multi-valued cells. <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Opening the Review and Modify dialog 2.png" /></ac:image><br />The <strong>Review and Modify </strong>dialog opens, displaying all values of the cell.</li></ul><h4>Creating new elements in multi-valued cells via the Review and Modify dialog</h4><p>To create new elements in multi-valued cells via the Review and Modify dialog</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="632e46ac-efaf-47ac-b11a-eaa7bc7d6e88"><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Review and Modify dialog"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Open the Review and Modify dialog]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> for the multi-valued cell.</li><li data-uuid="11269334-32df-4a59-91d4-dab56d11d71e">Click the <strong>Add New</strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Review and Modify dialog Add New button.png" /></ac:image><strong><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-red-bolder,#c9372c);"> </span></strong>button. The <strong>Create Element </strong>dialog opens.</li><li data-uuid="f2eecf93-d076-46a1-8568-8898135a7b7a">In the dialog, click the needed element. The element is created in the cell at the bottom of the list.</li><li data-uuid="e6607e7f-3544-41cc-aa80-155ad86d8d36">Click <strong>OK</strong>. Otherwise, to discard changes, click <strong>Cancel</strong>.</li></ol><h4>Deleting elements in multi-valued cells via the Review and Modify dialog</h4><p>To delete elements in multi-valued cells via the Review and Modify dialog</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="ba49e992-4fe4-4ba8-9c83-039987b38eb9"><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Review and Modify dialog"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Open the Review and Modify dialog]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> for the multi-valued cell.</li><li data-uuid="64a6bc19-f350-4864-9763-bfe7bbab8034">In the numbering column, hover the cursor over the row number of the element you want to delete, and select the check box. You can select multiple elements.<ol><li data-uuid="d5319137-919d-4088-b905-920c90b54afb">(Optional) To select all elements, select the check box in the header of the numbering column.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Review and Modify dialog select element.png" /></ac:image></li></ol></li><li data-uuid="096eb0aa-5fe9-41aa-b6b3-8a46e6067ba1">Click the <strong>Delete <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Review and Modify dialog Delete button.png" /></ac:image> </strong>button.</li><li data-uuid="1aa28012-0c54-4fd9-8ab4-b89adb560eb5">Click <strong>OK</strong>. Otherwise, to discard changes, click <strong>Cancel</strong>.</li></ol><h4>Renaming elements in multi-valued cells via the Review and Modify dialog</h4><p>To rename elements in multi-valued cells via the Review and Modify dialog</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="21b5f411-3986-4b48-8708-769b464edbad"><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Review and Modify dialog"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Open the Review and Modify dialog]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> for the multi-valued cell.</li><li data-uuid="a2395c6c-bfc5-4433-941d-c057f4af2aab">Click the row of the element and rename as needed.</li><li data-uuid="6f8c57d1-77b3-4452-bb23-c71690e42785">Click <strong>OK</strong>. Otherwise, to discard changes, click <strong>Cancel</strong>.</li></ol><h3>Managing multi-valued cells</h3><p>Tabular view cells may contain multiple values. By default, multi-valued cells display up to 10 values. You can review and modify all values in the cell through the <strong>Review and Modify </strong>dialog.</p><h4>Filtering elements in multi-valued cells via the Review and Modify dialog</h4><p>To filter elements in multi-valued cells via the Review and Modify dialog</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="7bb85797-4630-4e41-821d-89fb02374387"><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Review and Modify dialog"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Open the Review and Modify dialog]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> for the multi-valued cell.</li><li data-uuid="25d1ea15-e756-4798-8848-99e9ed55b021">At the top of the dialog, input the filter keyword. The filter updates as you type.<ol><li data-uuid="17db893d-87d5-46e6-892e-91b5ed540ef5">To reset the filter, click the <strong>Clear <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Review and Modify dialog Clear button.png" /></ac:image><strong> </strong></strong>button.</li></ol></li><li data-uuid="ff061a26-4659-44eb-b9f7-b4fe5df65603">Click <strong>OK </strong>or <strong>Cancel</strong>. </li></ol><h4>Reordering elements in multi-valued cells via the Review and Modify dialog</h4><p>To reorder elements in multi-valued cells via the Review and Modify dialog</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="ab171e68-87fb-41c7-b593-f0e165510d5d"><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Review and Modify dialog"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Open the Review and Modify dialog]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> for the multi-valued cell.</li><li data-uuid="c77f2758-e8be-4f3f-a9a0-0755d05ec447">In the numbering column, hover the cursor over the row number of the element you want to move and click the <strong>Move <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Review and Modify dialog move icon.png" /></ac:image></strong> icon. Drag and drop the row element to the needed position.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Review and Modify dialog move element.png" /></ac:image></li><li data-uuid="22cbb639-f078-49e0-8966-724a589a9537">Click <strong>OK</strong>. Otherwise, to discard changes, click <strong>Cancel</strong>.</li></ol><h4>Modifying the <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="2cc45aa1-d383-49b0-967a-edc7817035a1">value</ac:inline-comment-marker> count in multi-valued cells </h4><p>By default, multi-valued cells display up to 10 values. You can modify the limit via the <em>maxDisplayedValues</em> attribute specified for the <em>DS_Views::CoreViews::Base Table</em>, which is reused by all predefined tabular views.</p><p>To modify the value count in multi-valued cells</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="16c5123d-67af-4351-9765-9bf10c373641">Within the body of a view, declare an attribute redefining the <em>maxDisplayedValues</em> attribute and set the default limit.</li><li data-uuid="fae7c2a5-973e-420a-8839-bda4fcb20ccf">Click the <strong>Synchronize </strong>button.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0a39e7e3-9da5-4b94-befd-a157a6df2456"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'Drone Structure'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">DS_Views::TabularViews::gt</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">expose</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">DroneStructure</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">::**;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">filter</span><span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">KerML::Root::Element::isLibraryElement</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> == </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">false</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">maxDisplayedValues</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">3</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;  </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// the default value limit is set to 3 for the table's multi-value cells</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">render</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">rendering</span><span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">asTable</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> { </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">... </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="maxDisplayedValues attribute.png" /></ac:image></p><h3>Tabular view cell errors</h3><p>You may encounter the following icons and buttons in tabular view column cells:</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th scope="col">Icon / Button</th><th scope="col">Message</th><th scope="col">Description</th></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Click to load the cell icon.png" /></ac:image></p></div></td><td>Click to load the cell.</td><td><p>Cell loading was canceled after exceeding 1000 ms. Click the button to restart loading; the modeling tool will wait until the cell has finished loading.</p><p>If the table is only partially loaded and its data is modified (e.g., via the Containment tree, Textual Editor, etc.) without refreshing the table afterward, the icon is displayed in the cells that were not loaded. In this case, the cell cannot be reloaded; click the <strong>Refresh</strong> button in the table toolbar to refresh the entire table. </p></td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="20"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Failed to evaluate expression icon.png" /></ac:image></p></div></td><td>Failed to evaluate expression.</td><td>Resolve model errors related to the column expression. The error may also appear if the expression specified for the tabular view column does not apply to the specific element.</td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="filtering or sorting partially loaded cells notification.png" /></ac:image></p></div></td><td>Filtering or sorting applied on partially loaded cells.</td><td><p>Tabular views load only the content that is immediately visible; additional rows and cell data are loaded as you scroll. If you filter or sort partially loaded column cells, a notification icon appears in the column header.</p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">To resolve the issue, see the <strong>Tabular view loading </strong>warning box in the <ac:link ac:anchor="Filtering tabular view column cells"><ri:page ri:content-title="Modeling tabular view columns" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Filtering tabular view column cells]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> subsection on the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Modeling tabular view columns" /></ac:link> page.</span></p></td></tr></tbody></table>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=283247030 space=SYSML2P version=48 -->
## PAGE 00623: Managing tabular view rows

- page_id: `283247030`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/283247030/Managing+tabular+view+rows
- version_number: 48
- version_date: `2026-05-06T21:21:59.101+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > Model composition > Views > Creating views > Tabular views
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Each table row appearing along the side of the table represents a model element. The procedures on this page describe how to manage elements in tabular views, such as how to add, remove, delete them, etc.

##### Adding elements to the table

###### Adding elements to the table via drag and drop

Add already created elements to the table.

To add elements to the table via drag and drop

- S elect element(s) in the Containment tree or element symbol(s) in a view, then drag and drop them onto the diagrammatic representation of a tabular view. 
The element is added to the table. The element position may change after reopening or reloading the table, depending on the row sorting.If the dropped elements do not comply with the filter conditions specified for the table or the elements are already added to the table, the elements are not added to the table, and the following notification is displayed.[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Adding elements to the table via the Add New command

Create a new element and add it to the table.

To add elements to the table via the Add New command

- In the tabular view's toolbar, click the Add New button, and in the dropdown menu, click the element you want to create and add to the table. The element is created in the model and displayed in the table by exposing it.

- The Add New dropdown menu complies with the filters specified for the view. If certain elements are filtered out for the view, they do not appear in the dropdown menu. [ATTACHMENT filename='Table Row Management Add New Button.png']
- By default, the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Custom Element Creation dialog' space=''] that is [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Activating Element Creation dialog' space=''] is used to suggest elements to be created in the Add New dropdown menu. If you want a custom table-specific [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Custom Element Creation dialog' space=''] to be used in the specific table's (tabular view usage or definition) Add New dropdown menu instead, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Custom table-specific Add New button dropdown menu' space=''] page.

##### Hiding elements from the table

Hiding elements from the table filters out the selected elements from the table view without permanently .

###### Hiding elements from the table via the Remove From Table command

To hide elements from the table via the Remove From Table command

- In a tabular view, do one of the following:
  - In the numbering column, hover the cursor over the row number of the element you want to hide, and select the check box. You can select multiple elements. In the table toolbar, click the Remove From Table button.
    - (Optional) To select all elements, select the check box in the header of the numbering column. [ATTACHMENT filename='selecting check boxes in the numbering column.png']
  - Right-click an element cell, and in the shortcut menu, click Remove From Table . [ATTACHMENT filename='Hiding elements from the table via the Remove From Table command 2.png'] [ATTACHMENT filename='Hiding elements from the table via the Remove From Table command 3.png'] The row element is hidden from the table.

##### Deleting elements from the table

###### Deleting elements from the table via the Delete command

To delete elements from the table via the Delete command

- In a tabular view, do one of the following:
  - In the numbering column, hover the cursor over the row number of the element you want to delete, and select the check box. You can select multiple elements. In the table toolbar, click the Delete button.
    - (Optional) To select all elements, select the check box in the header of the numbering column. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
  - Right-click an element cell, and in the shortcut menu, click Delete . [ATTACHMENT filename='Deleting elements from the table via the Delete command 2.png'] [ATTACHMENT filename='Deleting elements from the table via the Delete command 3.png'] The row element is deleted from the table and the model.

##### Selecting table elements in the Containment tree

###### Selecting table elements in the Containment tree via the Select in Containment Tree command

To select table elements in the Containment tree via the Select in Containment Tree command

- In a tabular view, right-click the element cell, and in the shortcut menu, click the Select in Containment Tree command.
  - If the cell is a ColumnByFeatureView column primitive-type cell (String, Integer, Boolean, Real) or a cell with Element or Enum values, the row element is selected in the Containment tree (click [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing tabular view cells' space=''] to learn more). [ATTACHMENT filename='Selecting table elements in the Containment tree via the Select in Containment Tree command 3.png']
  - Otherwise, you can choose to select the row element or the cell value in the Containment tree (click [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing tabular view cells' space=''] to learn more). [ATTACHMENT filename='Selecting table elements in the Containment tree via the Select in Containment Tree command 2.png']

##### Opening the Specification Panel for table elements

###### Opening the Specification Panel for table elements via the Specification command

To open the Specification Panel for table elements via the Specification command

- In a tabular view, right-click the element cell, and in the shortcut menu, click the Specification command.
  - If the cell is a ColumnByFeatureView column primitive-type cell (String, Integer, Boolean, Real) or a cell with Element or Enum values, you can open the Specification Panel for the row element (click [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing tabular view cells' space=''] to learn more). [ATTACHMENT filename='Opening the Specification Panel for table elements via the Specification command 3.png']
  - Otherwise, you can choose to open the Specification Panel for the row element or the cell value (click [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing tabular view cells' space=''] to learn more). [ATTACHMENT filename='Opening the Specification Panel for table elements via the Specification command 2.png']

via drag and drop</h4><p>Add already created elements to the table. </p><p>To add elements to the table via drag and drop</p><hr /><ul><li>S<span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">elect element(s) in the /></span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e491f91d-60b2-4b00-9e8c-55f0dc276ef4"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li data-uuid="69878c9f-ec2a-476d-9257-c30639f85571">The ac:macro-id="6c41d89e-970f-4c80-bb58-75e3860b728e"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li data-uuid="9ed768c5-5367-4589-aa98-532583729951">The <strong>Add New</strong> dropdown menu complies with the filters If you want a custom table-specific <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Custom Element Creation dialog" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Element Creation dialog]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> to be used in the specific table's (tabular view usage or definition) <strong>Add New </strong>dropdown menu instead, see the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Custom table-specific Add New button dropdown menu" /></ac:link> page.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Hiding elements from the table</h3><p>Hiding elements from the table filters out the selected elements from the table view without permanently <ac:link ac:anchor="Deleting elements from the table"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[deleting them from the table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p><h4>Hiding elements from the table via the Remove From Table command</h4><p>To hide elements from the table via the Remove From Table command</p><hr /><ul><li data-uuid="96fdbaf6-d0b0-4579-9655-3aea1ed22f7a">In a tabular view, do one of the following:<ul><li data-uuid="56683c77-cdbf-4c30-8f3f-0b47f115158b">In the numbering column, hover the cursor over the row number of the element you want to hide, and select the check box. You can select multiple elements. In the table toolbar, click the <strong>Remove From Table </strong>button.<ul><li data-uuid="cea998a3-304c-40b8-8a9e-f85203667cf3">(Optional) To select all elements, select the check box in the header of the numbering column.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="selecting check boxes in the numbering column.png" /></ac:image></li></ul></li><li data-uuid="c7880c5b-f984-4bce-b7d3-be9a5be94809">Right-click an element cell, and in the shortcut menu, click <strong>Remove From Table</strong>. <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Hiding elements from the table via the Remove From Table command 2.png" /></ac:image> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Hiding elements from the table via the Remove From Table command 3.png" /></ac:image><br />The row element is hidden from the table. </li></ul></li></ul><h3>Deleting elements from the table</h3><h4>Deleting elements from the table via the Delete command</h4><p>To delete elements from the table via the Delete command</p><hr /><ul><li data-uuid="a7c2deff-2958-4f73-9cb1-43e192fcd682">In a tabular view, do one of the following:<ul><li data-uuid="89217221-ed56-4347-b1f5-c2086f3a78d5">In the numbering column, hover the cursor over the row number of the element you want to delete, and select the check box. You can select multiple elements. In the table toolbar, click the <strong>Delete </strong>button.<ul><li data-uuid="df582fbe-a0d4-4d75-ba86-ce8d165e0df5">(Optional) To select all elements, select the check box in the header of the numbering column.<br /><strong><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="selecting check boxes in the numbering column.png" /></ac:image></strong></li></ul></li><li data-uuid="50e98430-fff3-4802-9180-a245f97b3dbc">Right-click an element cell, and in the shortcut menu, click <strong>Delete</strong>. <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Deleting elements from the table via the Delete command 2.png" /></ac:image> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Deleting elements from the table via the Delete command 3.png" /></ac:image><br />The row element is deleted from the table and the model.</li></ul></li></ul><h3>Selecting table elements in the Containment tree</h3><h4>Selecting table elements in the Containment tree via the Select in Containment Tree command</h4><p>To select table elements in the Containment tree via the Select in Containment Tree command</p><hr /><ul><li data-uuid="249a6a48-4da8-4d8e-8d6e-641252d50fd6">In a tabular view, right-click the element cell, and in the shortcut menu, click the <strong>Select in Containment Tree</strong> command.<ul><li data-uuid="8fa4a825-0332-4d0d-ae52-d7d2c3b433e6">If the cell is a <em>ColumnByFeatureView</em> column primitive-type cell (String, Integer, Boolean, Real) or a cell with Element or Enum values, the row element is selected in the Containment tree (click <ac:link ac:anchor="Modifying a table cell directly via the cell"><ri:page ri:content-title="Managing tabular view cells" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[here]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> to learn more).<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Selecting table elements in the Containment tree via the Select in Containment Tree command 3.png" /></ac:image></li><li data-uuid="4b31f036-e374-4e03-a063-a56474d6078a">Otherwise, you can choose to select the row element or the cell value in the Containment tree (click <ac:link ac:anchor="Managing cells via the Review and Modify dialog"><ri:page ri:content-title="Managing tabular view cells" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[here]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> to learn more).<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Selecting table elements in the Containment tree via the Select in Containment Tree command 2.png" /></ac:image></li></ul></li></ul><h3>Opening the Specification Panel for table elements</h3><h4>Opening the Specification Panel for table elements via the Specification command</h4><p>To open the Specification Panel for table elements via the Specification command</p><hr /><ul><li data-uuid="d62535b3-d79c-447d-a87e-b13796aa3824">In a tabular view, right-click the element cell, and in the shortcut menu, click the <strong>Specification</strong> command.<br /><ul><li data-uuid="b2a3eea8-089b-4822-b438-625082f3eef3">If the cell is a <em>ColumnByFeatureView</em> column primitive-type cell (String, Integer, Boolean, Real) or a cell with Element or Enum values, you can open the Specification Panel for the row element (click <ac:link ac:anchor="Modifying a table cell directly via the cell"><ri:page ri:content-title="Managing tabular view cells" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[here]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> to learn more).<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Opening the Specification Panel for table elements via the Specification command 3.png" /></ac:image></li><li data-uuid="2c66a2ee-792e-48ae-9992-0dd90095b7d9">Otherwise, you can choose to open the Specification Panel for the row element or the cell value (click <ac:link ac:anchor="Managing cells via the Review and Modify dialog"><ri:page ri:content-title="Managing tabular view cells" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[here]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> to learn more).<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Opening the Specification Panel for table elements via the Specification command 2.png" /></ac:image></li></ul></li></ul><p><br /></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249576921 space=SYSML2P version=11 -->
## PAGE 00624: Managing Textual Editor

- page_id: `249576921`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/249576921/Managing+Textual+Editor
- version_number: 11
- version_date: `2026-03-03T15:30:57.491+01:00`
- ancestors: SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > Model management > Working with Textual Editor
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

This page outlines the basic procedures for working with the Textual Editor and the textual notation.

For SysML v2 Textual Editor-related environment options, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Environment options' space=''] page.

##### Managing the Textual Editor

###### Opening the Textual Editor

To open the Textual Editor

- In the Containment tree, right-click an element. In the shortcut menu, under Textual Editor , click Open .
- On a view , right-click an element symbol. In the shortcut menu, under Textual Editor , click Open .
- In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Tabular views' space=''] , click the **Open in Textual Editor** button.
- In a tabular view, right-click a column header. In the shortcut menu, click the **Open in Textual Editor** button.
- In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification panel' space='']'s Navigation bar, click the **Open in Textual Editor** button.
- In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification panel' space=''], click the [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button next to a compartment element or the element specified in the **Type** field and, in its submenu, click the **Open in Textual Editor** button.
- In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification panel' space=''], click the [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button next to the **Result Expression**field.

###### Saving changes in the Textual Editor

To save changes in the Textual Editor

Do one of the following:

- In the Textual Editor's toolbar, click the Synchronize button [ATTACHMENT filename='sync_button.png'] .
- Use the Alt+S shortcut keys. You must synchronize the textual notation before saving your project. Otherwise, the project will be saved without the changes made via the Textual Editor. If the **Synchronize model with errors** environment option is set to:*true*(default), an informational message is displayed informing you the notation contains errors. The notation cannot be synchronized. Fix the errors and synchronize the changes again.*false*, the notation is synchronized as it is. This may lead to an incorrect model.

###### Updating changes in the Textual EditorUpdating changes in the Textual Editor

Making changes both in the textual notation and elsewhere in the model with the editor tab still open results in concurrent editing, which may lead to data loss. If you do make changes in the model while the Textual Editor is open, an informational message is displayed requesting you to refresh the textual notation displayed in the Textual Editor.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

In this case, there are two options:

- Click the **Refresh** button in the message. This updates the textual notation with the changes made elsewhere in the model, removing any changes made in the Textual Editor that were not previously saved.
- Click the **Synchronize** [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button in the editor toolbar. This updates the model with changes made in the textual notation, removing any changes made elsewhere in the model after making modifications in the Textual Editor. You can detect previously unsaved changes made in the Textual Editor that will be lost after clicking the **Refresh**button by looking for elements that do not have display IDs in the textual notation.[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Commenting in the Textual Editor

- There can be two types of comments:
  - A line note.
    - This type of comment is for the Textual Editor only; it does not create a comment element in your model.
    - Two ways to write it:
      - Write it using double forward slashes followed by the text you want to leave in the textual notation.
      - Place the cursor on the line you want to comment out and press Ctrl+/ on the keyboard.
  - A comment element.
    - This type of comment creates a comment element in your model.
    - For more information, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Annotations' space=''] page.
- By default, comments are indicated in #248000 color, differentiating them from other notational text. // May require revision - line note; a comment that exists only in the Textual Editor. 
// the following two options are equivalent; both create a *comment*element with the 'May require revision' text: 
/* May require revision */ 
comment/* May require revision */

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

The notation cannot be synchronized. Fix the errors and synchronize the changes again.</li><li><em>false</em>, the notation is synchronized as it is. This may lead to an incorrect model.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ul><h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f536a4a9-4efd-4978-9d8b-60f50c40df2d"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Updating changes in the Textual Editor</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Updating changes in the Textual Editor</h4><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Making changes both in the textual notation and elsewhere in the model with the editor tab still open results in concurrent editing, which may lead to data loss. If you do make changes in the model while the Textual Editor is <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="cb2491d2-77c3-4698-9763-79f22cae5b7c">open</ac:inline-comment-marker>, an informational message is displayed requesting you to refresh the textual notation displayed in the Textual Editor.<br /></span></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="outdated_textual_notation.png" /></ac:image></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In this case, there are two options:</span></p><ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Click the <strong>Refresh</strong> button in the message. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249576940 space=SYSML2P version=8 -->
## PAGE 00625: Managing textual notation

- page_id: `249576940`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/249576940/Managing+textual+notation
- version_number: 8
- version_date: `2026-02-17T15:47:38.552+01:00`
- ancestors: SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > Model management > Working with Textual Editor
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The sections on this page provide the basic information for writing the textual notation usingthe [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Textual Editor' space=''].

##### Element declaration

- An element is declared with (1) a keyword indicating the element kind, followed by (2) the element name, and then either (3) curly braces for specifying the element's body or a semicolon to indicate the end of the element's specification. part flashlight;// keyword 'part', followed by the element name, and a semicolon, indicating the end of the element specification. 
part 'LED Flashlight' {// opening curly braces for specifying the declared element's body 
 part bulb;// element's body 
}// closing curly braces indicating the end of the element specification. 
part; // declared element without a name

##### Keywords

- Keywords indicate element kind ( part def, part, flow, item , etc.), relationships ( subsets, specializes, redefines ), or additional properties, such as directions ( in, out, to, from , etc.).
- Keywords are always typed in lowercase.
- Special textual symbols may replace specialization keywords. See the subpages under the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specializations' space=''] page.
- By default, keywords are indicated in #1D0FFF color, differentiating them from other notational text.
- Multiple keywords may be specified in a defined order: (1) [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Modifiers' space=''] (direction, derived , abstract , constant , ref ), (2) [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Occurrences' space=''] , (3) [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Occurrences' space=''] (snapshot, timeslice), (4) [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Metadata' space=''] , (5) element metaclass, (6) [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Element names' space=''] , (7) [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Multiplicity' space=''] , (8) [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specializations' space=''] . individualtimeslicepartmyCardefinedbyCar;// individual, portion kind (timeslice), element metaclass (part), element name (myCar), specialization (defined by Car).
- For all element keyword examples, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model composition' space=''] section.

##### Naming

- Unrestricted element names that include whitespace, special characters, or that correspond to keywords, must be encapsulated with single quotation marks.
  - Press the quotation mark (or Shift+quotation mark) key to encapsulate a text selection with single or double quotation marks.
- Basic element names without special characters or whitespace do not have to be encapsulated. 
For more information, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Element names' space=''] page. part 'LED Flashlight' { // unrestricted element name that includes whitespace 
 part bulb; //basic element name without whitespace 
}

##### Name resolution

For name resolution-specific environment options, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Environment options' space=''] page.

Name resolution allows you to target an element by its name, which can be done in the following ways:

- Feature chaining
  - A feature chain allows you to target a specific usage element located within the scope of another usage element, as long as the targeting and the target elements are within the scope of the same parent element.
  - A feature chain can only chain usage elements.
  - A feature chain is specified as a sequence of two or more usage element names separated by dot symbols. [ATTACHMENT filename='feature_chaining.png']
- Qualified name
  - A qualified name identifies an element qualified by the namespace relative to which the element name is to be resolved.
  - A qualified name can be used to target any element (definitions, usages, packages).
  - A qualified name is specified as a sequence of segments that indicate namespaces, which are separated by the :: symbol. [ATTACHMENT filename='qualified_name.png']

##### Textual notation errors

For textual notation error management, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Environment options' space=''] page.

If a portion of the textual notation is incompatible or cannot be rendered, error messages may appear in place of the invalid content. Although rare, this may occur when importing a non-compliant model.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

/><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[specialization]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. <br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cb2f57e8-7784-40b1-8e13-35850c373a53"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">individual</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">timeslice</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">myCar</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">defined</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">by </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Car</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// individual, portion kind (timeslice), element metaclass (part), element name (myCar), specialization (defined by Car).</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">For all element keyword examples, see the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Model composition" /></ac:link> section.</p></li></ul><h3>Naming</h3><ul><li>Unrestricted element names that include whitespace, special characters, or that correspond to keywords, must be encapsulated with single quotation marks. <ul><li>Press the quotation mark (or Shift+quotation mark) key to encapsulate a text selection with single or double quotation marks.</li></ul></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Basic element names without special characters or whitespace do not have to be encapsulated. <br />For more information, see the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Element names" /></ac:link> page.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7828381c-821d-4e41-a12a-5af072f7f3aa"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">LED Flashlight</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {    <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// unrestricted element name that includes whitespace</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">bulb</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;                  <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">basic element name without whitespace</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ul><h3>Name resolution</h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6c77ed3d-e25c-4c48-b316-012b86375726"><ac:rich-text-body><p>For name resolution-specific environment options, see the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Environment options" /></ac:link> page.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Name resolution allows you to target an element by its name, which can be done in the following ways:</p><ul><li><strong>Feature chaining</strong><ul><li>A feature chain allows you to target a specific usage element located within the scope of another usage element, as long as the targeting and the target elements are within the scope of the same parent element.</li><li>A feature chain can only chain usage elements.</li><li>A feature chain is specified as a sequence of two or more usage element names separated by dot symbols.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="feature_chaining.png" /></ac:image></li></ul></li><li><strong>Qualified name</strong><br /><ul><li>A qualified name identifies an element qualified by the namespace relative to which the element name is to be resolved.</li><li>A qualified name can be used to target any element (definitions, usages, packages).</li><li>A qualified name is specified as a sequence of segments that indicate namespaces, which are separated by the :: symbol.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="qualified_name.png" /></ac:image></li></ul></li></ul><h3>Textual notation errors</h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="51788edc-f35b-487f-a93e-b1ae078b0c06"><ac:rich-text-body><p>For textual notation error management, see the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Environment options" /></ac:link> page.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>If a portion of the textual notation is incompatible or cannot be rendered, error messages may appear in place of the invalid content. Although rare, this may occur when importing a non-compliant model.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="textual_notation_errors_1.png" /></ac:image></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="textual_notation_errors_2.png" /></ac:image></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=314182414 space=SYSML2P version=14 -->
## PAGE 00626: Message

- page_id: `314182414`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/314182414/Message
- version_number: 14
- version_date: `2026-07-10T14:03:28.449+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > Model composition > Relations > Connectors
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Occurrences may collaborate by transferring information between each other via messages. The sending of such a message is an event in the lifetime of the sending occurrence, while the receipt of such a message is an event in the lifetime of the receiving occurrence.

You can create messages between occurrences in any symbolic view; however, lifeline display and message modeling between them can only be done in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Symbolic views' space=''].

Sequence View is provided as a technology preview. Reordering messages in the view does not update their order in the model. Message order is refreshed from the model during Display and Layout actions.

**[IMAGE alt='' src='']**

###### The image displays the *sensedSpeedMessage*messagefrom *speedometer.sensedSpeedSent*to*cruiseController.sensedSpeedReceived*with the payload *SensedSpeed*.Additionally, the message is also [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying elements in symbolic views' space=''].

##### Displaying lifelines

- To display lifelines, ensure the context element symbol (e.g., occurrence definition) and its features (e.g., parts) are displayed in the Sequence View.
- If you have already modeled messages between parts, you can display them using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying elements in symbolic views' space='']:
  - Display Features - selecting the command for the context symbol displays parts with lifelines and messages.
  - Display Relations / Display In-Between Relations - selecting the command for displayed parts displays their lifelines and messages.

###### Attaching lifelines via the smart manipulator in a view

The **Attach Lifeline** command is only available for part usages without attached lifelines in Sequence Views.

To attach lifelines via the smart manipulator in a view

1. In a Sequence View, select a part usage, and in its smart manipulator, click the Attach Lifeline [ATTACHMENT filename='attach lifeline command.png'] command. The lifeline is displayed for the part.

###### Attaching lifelines via the Sequence View palette

To attach lifelines via the Sequence View palette

1. In the Sequence View palette, in the Sequences group, click the lifeline button, then click on the view pane. A part usage element is created with an attached lifeline.

##### Creating messages

###### Creating messages via the smart manipulator in a view

To create messages via the smart manipulator in a view

1. In a view, select the source element symbol or lifeline, and in its smart manipulator, click the message [IMAGE alt='' src=''] command.
2. If there are no event occurrences referenced/represented by the lifeline or shape, an event occurrence is created. Otherwise, a dropdown menu is displayed containing:
  1. Select Event section contains:
    1. For lifelines - event occurrences not referenced by the event source/target of the message connected to the selected lifeline.
    2. For shapes - event occurrences not represented as nested shapes.
    3. You can also select None to ignore the dropdown selection and select the source and the target of the message.
  2. Create Event section allows you to create a new event occurrence , perform action , or exhibit action. [ATTACHMENT filename='Left-click dropdown menu.png']
3. Click on the target element symbol or lifeline. Click the appropriate event occurrence in the dropdown menu. A message is created between the elements or lifelines.

###### Creating messages via the view palette

To create messages via the view palette

1. In the view palette, under the Connectors group, click the **message** button to create a message.
2. In a view, click on the source element symbol or lifeline:
  1. If there are no event occurrences referenced/represented by the lifeline or shape, an event occurrence is created. Otherwise:
    1. Left-click displays the dropdown menu containing:
      1. Select Event section contains:
        1. For lifelines - event occurrences not referenced by the event source/target of the message connected to the selected lifeline.
        2. For shapes - event occurrences not represented as nested shapes.
        3. You can also select None to ignore the dropdown selection and select the source and the target of the message.
      2. Create Event section allows you to create a new event occurrence , perform action , or exhibit action. [ATTACHMENT filename='Left-click dropdown menu.png']
    2. Right-click displays the dropdown menu containing:
      1. Select Event section contains:
        1. For lifelines - all event occurrences (both referenced by the event source/target of the message connected to the selected lifeline, or not).
        2. For shapes - all event occurrences (both represented as nested shapes, and not).
      2. Create Event section allows you to create a new event occurrence , perform action , or exhibit action. [ATTACHMENT filename='Right-click dropdown menu.png']
3. Click on the target element symbol or lifeline. Click the appropriate event occurrence in the dropdown menu. A message is created between the elements or lifelines.

###### Creating messages via the Create Relation command

You can create messages via the Create Relation command only between elements, but not between their lifelines.

To create messages via the Create Relation command

1. Right-click one of the elements you want to connect.
2. In the shortcut menu, click Create Relation , then in the submenu, under Outgoing , click message . The Create... dialog opens.
3. In the Create... dialog, do one of the following:
  1. In a view, click the element symbol you want to connect. Hold Shift to select multiple elements.
  2. Click the Add [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. The element selection dialog opens. In the element selection dialog, select the element you want to connect and click OK .
  3. Drag and drop the needed element you want to connect from the Containment tree onto the Create... dialog.
4. In the Create... dialog, click OK .

###### Creating messages via the Textual Editor

To create messages via the Textual Editor

1. Create event occurrences:
  1. In the Textual Editor, identify the event source and target elements, then in their bodies, do one of the following:
    1. To create owned event occurrences:
      1. Declare the event occurrence keyword, then name it.
    2. To create an event occurrence by referencing an occurrence defined elsewhere in the model:
      1. For an unnamed event occurrence, declare the event keyword followed by the name of the occurrence.
      2. For a named one, declare the*event occurrence* keyword, specify the element name, then[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Reference subsetting' space='']the needed occurrence.
2. Create a message:
  1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare the message keyword.
  2. Specify the element name (optional), [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specializations' space=''] (optional), (optional), then the from keyword and the names of the event source element and its event (use feature chaining). Then follow with the keyword to and the name of the event target element and its event.
3. Click the Synchronize button.

50%

occurrencedefCruiseControlInteraction{ 
partcruiseController{ 
// event occurrence is the event occurrence usage itself contained by the parent 
eventoccurrencesensedSpeedReceived; 
} 
partspeedometer{// event occurrence is the event occurrence usage itself contained by the parent 
eventoccurrencesensedSpeedSent; 
} 
messagesensedSpeedMessagefromspeedometer.sensedSpeedSenttocruiseController.sensedSpeedReceived; // the message between event occurrences 
}

50%

occurrencedefCruiseControlInteraction{ 
partcruiseController{

// unnamed event referencing an occurrence defined elsewhere in the model 
eventsensedSpeedReceived; 
} 
partspeedometer{

// named event referencing an occurrence defined elsewhere in the model 
eventoccurrencesSpeedSentreferencessensedSpeedSent; 
// OR 
eventoccurrencesSpeedSent::>sensedSpeedSent; 
} 
occurrencesensedSpeedReceived;// referenced occurrenceoccurrencesensedSpeedSent;// referenced occurrencemessagesensedSpeedMessagefromspeedometer.sensedSpeedSenttocruiseController.sensedSpeedReceived; 
}

##### Setting the payload for the message

50%

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

50%

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Setting the payload for the message via the Drag and Drop handler in a view

To set the payload for the message via the Drag and Drop handler in a view

- Drag and drop an element from the Containment tree or use its symbol's [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Drag and Drop handler' space=''] on the message path. If the Drag and Drop menu appears, select the **Set Payload** **** command.

###### Setting the payload for the message via the Textual Editor

To set the payload for the message via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor before the keyword*from*within the message for which you want to set a payload.
2. Declare the keyword of followed by either of the following:
  1. The name of the payload specialized by a definition element.
  2. The name of the definition element you want to set as payload for the message.
3. Click the Synchronize button.

messagesensedSpeedMessageofItems::SensedSpeedfromspeedometer.sensedSpeedSenttocruiseController.sensedSpeedReceived;

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

Then follow with the keyword <em>to </em>and the name of the event target element and its event. </li></ol></li><li data-uuid="b7d4fffe-f757-406e-bea7-4f385edf63e8">Click the <strong>Synchronize </strong>button.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="section" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="aba274ae-0eb2-45a5-84fb-7581af453400"><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="column" the payload for the message</h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="section" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="06b5da10-1bd2-48df-b126-d7aa3f20bc30"><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bd1e06b9-a56a-47d7-8d09-9098a6cbbb9b"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">50%</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:image ac:title="Message without payload" ac:width="451"><ri:attachment ri:filename="without payload.png" /></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="30bfba01-c70c-4e6e-a4bb-f8b974178fdb"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">50%</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:image ac:title="Message with payload"><ri:attachment ri:filename="with payload.png" /></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Setting the payload for the message via the Drag and Drop handler in a view</h4><p>To set the payload for the message via the Drag and Drop handler in a view</p><hr /><ul><li data-uuid="23adf0aa-8f9b-49c6-b57f-91c75b77a397"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Drag and drop an element from the Containment tree or use its symbol's<span> </span></span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Drag and Drop handler" /></ac:link> <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">on </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">the message <span>path. If the Drag and Drop menu appears, select the <strong>Set Payload</strong></span></span><span><strong> </strong></span>command.</li></ul><h4>Setting the payload for the message via the Textual Editor</h4><p>To set the payload for the message via the Textual Editor</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="acfaaca2-c8fb-465d-9161-09b466a74fcf"><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the Textual Editor, place the cursor <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">before the keyword<span> </span></span><em style="text-align: left;">from<span> </span></em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">within the message for which you want to set a payload.</span></p></li><li data-uuid="57c62c48-3b46-4597-9930-579ff6fd82f9">Declare the keyword <em>of </em>followed by either of the following:<ol><li>The name of the payload specialized by a definition element. </li><li>The name of the definition element you want to set as payload for the message. </li></ol></li><li data-uuid="2a898398-28df-4202-a52f-fee728494b79">Click the <strong>Synchronize </strong>button.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="68b4768a-6363-457f-9f82-f7c42b0d7e46"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">message</span><span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">sensedSpeedMessage</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">of</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Items::SensedSpeed</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">from</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">speedometer</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">sensedSpeedSent</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">to</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">cruiseController</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">sensedSpeedReceived</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249577266 space=SYSML2P version=5 -->
## PAGE 00627: Metadata

- page_id: `249577266`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/249577266/Metadata
- version_number: 5
- version_date: `2025-09-30T14:49:19.281+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > Model composition > Common elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Metadata allows extending model elements by adding various attributes, semantics, and notation. It can be used to customize various models for specific domains or purposes.Metadata can be applied to any element. Metadata usage annotates its containing element; otherwise, an annotation relationship can be specified using the *about*syntax.

#### INFO: Semantic vs Non-Semantic Metadata

Semantic vs Non-Semantic Metadata

Semantic metadata refers to a metadata definition element that specializes the *SemanticMetadata* metaclass (e.g., *<moe> MeasureOfEffectiveness*, *<derive> DerivedRequirementMetadata*, etc.). There are several key differences in how elements are displayed in the Containment tree, Specification panel, symbols on views, and any other UI instances, depending on the kind of metadata applied to them:

If an element has at least one semantic metadata applied to it:

- The element's metaclass keyword (e.g., part ) is not displayed.
- The applied semantic metadata name is displayed with the # sign within guillemets « ». The def keyword is appended for definition elements. [ATTACHMENT filename='semantic_metadata.png']

If an element has only non-semantic metadata applied to it:

- The applied semantic metadata name is not displayed; thus, the element representation remains essentially unchanged. The element only displays metadata-related information in the metadata compartment and a metadata annotation. [ATTACHMENT filename='non-semantic_metadata.png']

##### Creating metadata definition elements

###### Creating metadata definition elements via the Textual Editor

To create metadata definition elements via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare the metadata def keyword .
2. Name the element.
3. Specify the metadata attributes in the element body.
4. Click the **Synchronize**button.

metadata defApproval{attributeapproved:Boolean; 
attributeapprover:String; 
}

###### Creating metadata definition elements via the Create Element command

To create metadata definition elements via the Create Element command

1. In the Containment tree, right-click an element, and in the shortcut menu, click **Create Element**.
2. In the Create Element dialog, select the metadata def element.
3. Name the element.
4. Specify the metadata attributes.

###### Creating metadata definition elements via the view palette

To create metadata definition elements via the view palette

1. In the view palette, under the Items/Parts/Attributes group, click the **metadata** def button to create a metadata definition element.
2. Click in the view pane where you want to create the element.
3. Specify the metadata attributes.

##### Applying metadata to an element

###### Applying metadata with attributes to an element via the Textual Editor

To apply metadata with attributes to an element via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, within the body of the element for which you want to apply metadata, do one of the following:
  1. For unnamed metadata usage, do one of the following:
    1. Declare the @ symbol followed by the metadata definition element name.
    2. Declare the keyword metadata followed by the metadata definition name. The notational difference between using the textual symbol @ or the keyword *metadata* depends on the **Display Metadata as Keywords** [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Environment options' space='']. By default, it is set to *false*, thus the notational representation of metadata in the textual notation is set to the textual symbol @. Even if you use a different notation, once the Textual Editor is reopened, the notational representation is changed to the one corresponding to the environment option. Set the environment option to *true* to use the keyword *metadata*notational representation instead.
  2. For a named metadata usage element:
    1. Declare the @ symbol followed by the element name, and then define it by the metadata definition.
    2. Declare the keyword metadata, specify the element name, and then define it by the metadata definition.
2. Specify the metadata attribute values in the element body.
3. Click the **Synchronize**button. // unnamed metadata usage defined by metadata definition:partsystem{@Approval{ // the at sign @ symbol followed by the metadata definition element name (step 1.a.i)approved=true; 
approver="John Smith"; 
}/* or */ 
metadataApproval{ // the keyword *metadata* followed by the metadata definition name (step 1.a.ii) 
approved=true; 
approver="John Smith"; 
 } 
}// named metadata usage defined by metadata definition:partsystem{@approvalSystem:Approval{ ****// the at sign @ symbol followed by the metadata usage name, defined by the metadata definition (step 1.a.i) 
approved=true; 
approver="John Smith"; 
}metadataapprovalSystem:Approval{// the keyword *metadata* followed by the metadata usage name, defined by metadata definition (step 1.b.ii) 
approved=true; 
approver="John Smith"; 
} 
}

###### Applying metadata without attributes to an element via the Textual Editor

Using the following notation applies the metadata to an element without the possibility of specifying the metadata attributes.

To apply metadata without attributes to an element via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, prior to the element keyword of the element you want to apply the metadata to, type the hash sign # followed by the metadata definition element name.
2. Click the **Synchronize**button. #Approvalpartsystem;

###### Applying metadata to an element via the Drag and Drop handler in a view

To apply metadata to an element via the Drag and Drop handler in a view using

- Drag and drop a metadata element from the Containment tree or use its symbol's [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Drag and Drop handler' space=''] on an element symbol executing the Apply Metadata command.

###### Applying annotation metadata to an element via the Textual Editor

For other element annotation options, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Annotations' space=''] page.

To apply annotation metadata to an element via the Textual Editor

Whether you want to apply annotation metadata with or without attributes, the overall procedure is generally the same as for:

- 

However, in this case:

- The element to which the annotation metadata is applied must be declared separately.
- The applied metadata keyword is followed by the keyword about and the name of the element to which the annotation metadata is to be applied.

partsystem;// element declaration

@Approvalaboutsystem{// annotation metadata applied to the element 'system' using the 'about' syntax 
approved=true; 
approver="John Smith"; 
}

/* or */

metadataApprovalaboutsystem{// annotation metadata applied to the element 'system' using the 'about' syntax 
approved=true; 
approver="John Smith"; 
}

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE
