/><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">drone</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'mission control center'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span>perform</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">action</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'run mission'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1894c83d-4a4e-4080-ac9e-ce0a6d8672d7"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">50%</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_swimlane_result.png" /></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="97dd7a3a-0d3d-4dcc-966c-4054ac644073"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'drone in any mission'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">drone</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'mission control center'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><br />    <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">part1</span>;                                  </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// new subpart represented by the newly created swimlane<br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span>perform</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">action</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'run mission'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;<br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b6e37a9b-b22f-4dc4-b1ba-251f9dd62e20"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If the part that owns or inherits the perform action owns or inherits parts that are not yet displayed as swimlanes, clicking the + button displays a dropdown menu with the following categories:</p><ul><li><strong>Display </strong>that has the following options:<ul><li><em>part &lt;the name of the part that does not yet have the corresponding swimlane displayed in the view&gt;</em>. Click to create the corresponding swimlane.</li><li><em>All</em>. Click to create the corresponding swimlanes for all parts that are not represented in the swimlane symbol.</li></ul></li><li><strong>Create </strong>that has the following option:<ul><li><em>part</em>. Click to create a part, represented by a new swimlane. </li></ul></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="section" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="36318de9-6c98-470f-8f27-ee7b29afd8a9"><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3db697dd-5754-4672-8f77-7cdc9ff49471"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">50%</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_or_display_swimlanes.png" /></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a6786fd1-f641-46a7-aa9a-de3f2422d58b"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">50%</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6aaaa2c7-48cf-4ab7-b67c-3fd8388fbe40"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'drone in any mission'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">drone</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'mission control center'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'weather station'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;   <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// subpart whose representative swimlane is not displayed yet, thus it is available in the Display dropdown menu</span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span>perform</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">action</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'run mission'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;<br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Displaying actions in swimlanes</h4><p>To display actions in swimlanes</p><hr /><ul><li>Select one swimlane by right-clicking its header or all swimlanes by right-clicking the swimlane pool. In the shortcut menu, <span>click </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Displaying elements in symbolic views" /><ac:link-body><strong>Display</strong></ac:link-body></ac:link><span>, then</span><span> </span><strong>Display Actions </strong>in the dropdown menu<span>. <br /></span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3dc1898d-3456-4fd1-8697-4223a6d0f6c7"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The specific swimlane's shortcut menu contains the same commands as the</span> part element symbol it represents<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">. </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><br /></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ul><h4><span>Displaying parameters, connectors, or other features for element symbols in swimlanes</span></h4><p><span>To display parameters, connectors, or other features for element symbols in swimlanes</span></p><hr /><ul><li>Right-click element symbol(s) in the swimlane(s). In the shortcut menu, click <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Displaying elements in symbolic views" /><ac:link-body><strong>Display</strong></ac:link-body></ac:link>, then click the appropriate command in its dropdown menu. </li></ul><h3>Modeling actions with swimlanes</h3><h4>Creating actions in swimlanes</h4><p>You may create actions and the appropriate subparts' perform actions via the Textual Editor and then display the actions in swimlanes via the <ac:link ac:anchor="Displaying actions in swimlanes" /> procedure. However, you can create actions in swimlanes using the view palette. This way, the appropriate perform actions are automatically created for the subparts in whose representative swimlanes the actions are created.</p><p>To create actions in swimlanes </p><hr /><ol><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Displaying swimlane grid and creating additional swimlanes"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Display or create swimlanes for the perform action in a view]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li>In the view palette, under the Actions group, click the button for the needed action.</li><li>Click on the swimlane representing the part that you want to perform the action.<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8538ff03-a6b8-4378-9cb3-b2761aa73c73"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Indicate the start and end of an action sequence </span>using start and done actions<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">. See the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Start and done actions" /></ac:link> page.</span></span></li><li><span>Connect and sequence actions using successions, flows, and succession flows. See the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Action succession and flow" /></ac:link> page.</span></li><li><span>Control the sequencing of actions using control nodes. See the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Control nodes" /></ac:link> page.</span></li></ul><p><span>Start and done actions, as well as control nodes, are not performed, thus parts in whose representative swimlanes they are displayed, do not have perform actions for performing them. These actions and nodes are owned by the perform action that owns the swimlane grid symbol, while their symbols are owned by the swimlane grid symbol. </span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="section" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bf68b352-00f5-4dc1-a7bf-c3d90cbd7580"><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="baa7c225-9133-4e66-8472-2a2dc2187826"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">50%</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_action_in_swimlane_before.png" /></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="be524e1a-8eaf-4c26-9488-2b7393181112"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'drone in any mission'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">drone</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'mission control center'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span>perform</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">action</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'run mission'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="86d2004f-5950-4673-94a3-2f36908a32a7"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">50%</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_action_in_swimlane_after.png" /></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9cbb8236-6503-4583-a1a7-c5b35723b86e"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'drone in any mission'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span>part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">drone</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">            </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">perform</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'run mission'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'check battery'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;      </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// perform action with applicable feature chain automatically created for the part once the</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"> action 'check battery'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">was created in the swimlane that represents the part 'drone'</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span>part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'mission control center'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">perform</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'run mission'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'start mission'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;      <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// perform action with applicable feature chain automatically created for the part once the<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"> action 'start mission' was created in the </span></span>swimlane that represents the part 'mission control center'</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span>perform</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">action</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'run mission'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">action</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'start mission'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;                               <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// action created via view</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">action</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'check battery'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;                              <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// action created via view</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">first</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'start mission'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">then</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'check battery'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;  <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// succession created via view</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h4>Deleting actions in swimlanes</h4><p>To delete actions in swimlanes</p><hr /><ul><li>To delete an action element or symbol in swimlane, see the procedure <ac:link ac:anchor="Deleting an element or its symbol via a view"><ri:page ri:content-title="Managing elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Deleting an element or its symbol via a view]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> on the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Managing elements" /></ac:link> page.<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="23004a7d-2f45-46df-a439-5977de4cbf46"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Deleting an action element via swimlane deletes the appropriate perform action from the part responsible for performing the deleted action. Any associated connectors are deleted as well.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ul><h4>Changing which parts are responsible for performing actions via swimlanes</h4><p>You can move actions between swimlanes to change which parts are responsible for performing these actions. The responsible parts are indicated in the header of the swimlane with the keyword <span>«performer»</span>.</p><p>To change which parts are responsible for performing actions via swimlanes </p><hr /><ul><li>Click and drag the action symbol itself from its source swimlane to the target swimlane of the needed perform action.</li></ul><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Details regarding the change:</strong></p><ul><li>If the source and target <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">swimlanes are in the same swimlane grid symbol owned by the common perform action, m</span>oving an action from one swimlane to another updates which part is the owner of the perform action responsible for performing the moved action. </li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="section" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b8235752-818d-45da-baad-c7a011fd392f"><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="707abf3e-cff1-4fb0-86f5-14ca1698c3eb"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">50%</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="move_action_to_another_swimlane_before.png" /></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="101feb5c-37b0-496a-89d6-7ec1e7e3701a"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'drone in any mission'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span>part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">drone</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">perform</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'run mission'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'start mission'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;   <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// perform action is owned by the part 'drone'</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'mission control center'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">perform</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">action</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'run mission'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">            </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">action</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'start mission'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9da6395a-2bb8-47d8-ad79-c3a741e36abe"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">50%</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="move_action_to_another_swimlane_after.png" /></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c9e0de7a-64e3-4018-88af-bed7e66bda57"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'drone in any mission'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">drone</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'mission control center'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">perform</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'run mission'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'start mission'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;  <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// perform action's owner is updated to the part 'mission control center'</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">perform</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">action</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'run mission'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">action</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'start mission'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><ul><li>If the source and target <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">swimlanes are in different swimlane grid symbols owned by different perform actions (see example below), m</span>oving an action updates:<ul><li>the moved action's owner to the new perform action;</li><li>which part is the owner of the perform action responsible for performing the moved action;<ul><li>the feature chain <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">for the referenced feature of the appropriate perform action</span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">. </span></li></ul></li></ul></li><li><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">The same applies if<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"> the moved action is not inherited or owned by the perform action that owns the target swimlane. </span><br /></span></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="section" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6acd558d-6f28-4660-84e1-7ae6c7e71836"><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cc63dfab-0615-4ad5-8eeb-43395ce32a79"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">50%</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="move_action_to_different_swimlane_symbol_before.png" /></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1eae025a-02e8-424d-aefe-dfcb557d00fb"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'drone mission'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span>part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'drone in any mission'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span>part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">drone</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'mission control center'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">            </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">perform</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'run mission'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'start mission'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">the part 'mission control center' is the owner of the perform action responsible for performing the action 'start mission'; </span></span>the feature chain for the referenced action is 'run mission'.'start mission'</span></span></span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">perform</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">action</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'run mission'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">            </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">action</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'start mission'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// the owner of the action 'start mission' is the perform action 'run mission'</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'drone in delivery mission'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">drone</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'distribution center'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">perform</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">action</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'run mission'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="column" ac:schema-version="1" style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'drone mission'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span>part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'drone in any mission'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">drone</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'mission control center'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// the previously owned perform action is moved out</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">perform</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">action</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'run mission'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// the previously owned <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">action 'start mission' is moved out</span></span></span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'drone in delivery mission'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">drone</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'distribution center'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">            </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">perform</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'run delivery mission'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'start mission'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">the part 'distribution center' is updated as the owner of the perform action responsible for performing the action 'start mission'; </span></span>the feature chain for the referenced action is updated to 'run delivery mission'.'start mission'</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">perform</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">action</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'run delivery mission'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">            </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">action</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'start mission'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// the owner of the action 'start mission' is updated to the perform action 'run delivery mission'</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h4>Deleting parts responsible for performing actions via swimlanes </h4><p>To delete parts responsible for performing actions via swimlanes </p><hr /><ol><li>Select the header of the swimlane representing the part you want to delete. </li><li>Follow the procedure <ac:link ac:anchor="Deleting an element or its symbol via a view"><ri:page ri:content-title="Managing elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Deleting an element or its symbol via a view]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> on the </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Managing elements" /></ac:link><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> page.</span></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5fb24ce6-d020-4b1f-b5c6-0516b68bd973"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Deleting parts responsible for performing actions via swimlanes also deletes perform actions contained by the deleted part. </p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h4>Adapting actions to specific context using swimlanes</h4><p align="justify"><span>You can adapt the existing actions to specific contexts without affecting the already modeled elements. You can do this by:</span></p><ol><li><span>First, <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Subsetting" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[subsetting]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> the part that owns the perform action, which in turn owns the actions performed by the subparts. </span><span>The subsetting part inherits the features of the subsetted part, including the subparts and the perform action.</span></li><li><span>You can then <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Redefinition" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[redefine]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> the inherited perform action to adapt the subsetting part and its inherited features to the new context. </span></li></ol><p align="justify"><span>Then you can:</span></p><ol><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Displaying swimlane grid and creating additional swimlanes"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Display the swimlane grid and create new swimlanes]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> relevant to this context, which creates subparts for the subsetting part. You can then <ac:link ac:anchor="Creating actions in swimlanes"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[create actions in these swimlanes]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Redefining a usage via the symbol shortcut menu in a view"><ri:page ri:content-title="Redefinition" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Redefine the inherited subparts]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. You can then manage actions in these swimlanes:<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9fe048c4-f991-4dc6-8e05-ed47ddfcef16"><ac:rich-text-body><p>To easily redefine the inherited subparts via the swimlane headers, <span>right-click the header of the swimlane that represents the subpart you want to redefine. In the shortcut menu, click </span><strong>Refactor</strong><span>, then </span><strong>Redefine</strong><span>.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2eb949be-e3ae-48b3-936d-0fc3c7518543"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">If an action is not owned but simply referenced by the perform action that is an inherited feature of the part represented by the swimlane, the action is displayed with the ^ prefix. See example below.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><br /></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ol><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Redefining a usage via the Textual Editor"><ri:page ri:content-title="Redefinition" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Redefine]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> the actions in these swimlanes via the Textual Editor. </li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Deleting an element or its symbol via a view"><ri:page ri:content-title="Managing elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Remove from the view]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> action symbols that are unnecessary in this context.</li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Creating actions in swimlanes"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Create actions]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> that would then be owned by the redefining perform action of the subsetting part.</li></ol></li></ol><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="actions_adapted_to_specific_context_using_swimlanes.png" /></ac:image><span style="font-weight: 600;letter-spacing: 0.0px;text-align: center;color:var(--ds-background-accent-gray-bolder,#626f86);">The part </span><em style="font-weight: 600;letter-spacing: 0.0px;text-align: center;color:var(--ds-background-accent-gray-bolder,#626f86);">drone in delivery mission</em><span style="font-weight: 600;letter-spacing: 0.0px;text-align: center;color:var(--ds-background-accent-gray-bolder,#626f86);"> is created by subsetting the part <em>drone in any mission </em>(the example of which is displayed at the top of this page). It inherits the perform action </span><em style="font-weight: 600;letter-spacing: 0.0px;text-align: center;color:var(--ds-background-accent-gray-bolder,#626f86);">run mission</em><span style="font-weight: 600;letter-spacing: 0.0px;text-align: center;color:var(--ds-background-accent-gray-bolder,#626f86);">. However, since additional procedures should be involved, the inherited perform action </span><em style="font-weight: 600;letter-spacing: 0.0px;text-align: center;color:var(--ds-background-accent-gray-bolder,#626f86);">run mission</em><span style="font-weight: 600;letter-spacing: 0.0px;text-align: center;color:var(--ds-background-accent-gray-bolder,#626f86);"> needs modifications within the context of the part </span><em style="font-weight: 600;letter-spacing: 0.0px;text-align: center;color:var(--ds-background-accent-gray-bolder,#626f86);">drone in delivery mission</em><span style="font-weight: 600;letter-spacing: 0.0px;text-align: center;color:var(--ds-background-accent-gray-bolder,#626f86);">. This is achieved by redefining the perform action </span><em style="font-weight: 600;letter-spacing: 0.0px;text-align: center;color:var(--ds-background-accent-gray-bolder,#626f86);">run mission</em><span style="font-weight: 600;letter-spacing: 0.0px;text-align: center;color:var(--ds-background-accent-gray-bolder,#626f86);"> specifically for delivery missions. This allows introducing new actions relevant to delivery missions without impacting the <em>drone in mission </em>part.</span></h6></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249577751 space=SYSML2P version=81 -->
## PAGE 00635: Modeling tabular view columns

- page_id: `249577751`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/249577751/Modeling+tabular+view+columns
- version_number: 81
- version_date: `2026-05-28T15:08:43.475+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > Model composition > Views > Creating views > Tabular views
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

See the procedures on this page for modeling tabular view columns.

#### NOTE: Prerequisites

Prerequisites

Ensure the following steps are done:

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating tabular views' space=''] .
2. The view [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Exposing elements for views' space=''] .
3. (Optional) The view has [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Filtering elements for views' space=''] .

Proceed to model table columns.

#### Adding table columns

For more information, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Tabular view columns' space=''] table on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Tabular view columns' space=''] page.

##### Adding table columns by feature

###### Adding table columns by feature via the Configure Table Columns dialog

Generally, only inherited columns or those that redefine inherited ones are available for selection in the **Configure Table Columns**dialog.

- If the needed column by feature is not available for selection in the Configure Table Columns dialog, you need to add it via the Textual Editor. See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating custom tabular view definitions' space=''] procedure on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating custom tabular view definitions' space=''] page.
- If the needed columns by feature for a metaclass or metadata, and their meta-features, are not available for selection in the Configure Table Columns dialog, you need to add them via the Textual Editor. See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customizing the suggested column list in the Configure Table Columns dialog' space=''] page.

To add table columns by feature via the Configure Table Columns dialog

1. In a tabular view's toolbar, click the Columns button, and in its dropdown menu, click Configure Table Columns . The Configure Table Columns dialog opens.
2. In the Configure Table Columns dialog, in the Column Name column, under the Element node, locate the column you want to add to the table, then select its check box in the Added To Model column.
3. Click OK . The column is added to the model and displayed in the diagrammatic representation of the tabular view. [ATTACHMENT filename='Configure Table Columns dialog_create column.png']

##### Adding table columns by expression

###### Adding table columns by expression via the Configure Table Columns dialog

To add table columns by expression via the Configure Table Columns dialog

1. In a tabular view's toolbar, click the Columns button, and in its dropdown menu, click Configure Table Columns . The Configure Table Columns dialog opens.
2. In the Configure Table Columns dialog, click the Add Custom Column button. A new custom column is added under the Custom Columns node.
3. (Optional) You can also do the following:
  1. Click the column title to rename it.
  2. Click the Delete [ATTACHMENT filename='Configure Table Columns dialog delete column button.png'] button in the Added To Model column to delete the column from the model.
  3. Click the [ATTACHMENT filename='Configure Table Columns dialog display column expression.png'] button in the Expression column to open the Column Expression dialog and view the expression specified for the column.
4. Click OK . A template is created in the model for custom column creation. You can specify the expression for the template via the Textual Editor. Custom column templateviewunnamed1:DS_Views::CoreViews::ColumnByExpressionView:>column{ 
renderrendering:DS_Views::CoreViews::StringCellRendering:>>DS_Views::CoreViews::asTableCell{ 
calc:>>getValue{inrowElement; } 
} 
}

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### Modifying table columns

##### Hiding table columns

###### Hiding table columns via the column headers in tabular views

To hide table columns via the column headers in tabular views

- Right-click the header of the column you want to hide from a tabular view, and in the shortcut menu, click **Hide Column**. 
The table column is hidden.

###### Hiding/displaying table columns via the Columns button in tabular views

To hide/display table columns via the Columns button in tabular views

1. In the tabular view's toolbar, click the **Columns** button.
2. In the dropdown menu, click the column you want to hide from or display in the table. The displayed columns are highlighted in the dropdown menu. [ATTACHMENT filename='Columns button displayed hidden columns.png']

##### Deleting table columns

###### Deleting table columns via the column headers in tabular views

The **Delete Column**command is not available for inherited columns or for columns that redefine inherited ones.

To delete table columns via the column headers in tabular views

- Right-click the header of the column you want to delete from a tabular view, and in the shortcut menu, click **Delete Column**. 
The column is deleted from the model.

###### Deleting table columns via the Configure Table Columns dialog

The **Delete**button is not available for inherited columns or for columns that redefine inherited ones.

To delete table columns via the Configure Table Columns dialog

1. In a tabular view's toolbar, click the Columns button, and in its dropdown menu, click Configure Table Columns . The Configure Table Columns dialog opens.
2. In the Configure Table Columns dialog, under the Column Name node, locate the column you want to delete from the table, then click the Delete [ATTACHMENT filename='Configure Table Columns dialog delete column button.png'] button in the Added To Model column.
3. Click OK . The column is deleted from the model.

##### Sorting table rows

###### Sorting table rows via column headers in tabular views

If the column header displays a [IMAGE alt='' src=''] notification after attempting to sort tabular rows via column headers, see the explanation and resolution in the **Tabular view loading**warning box in the procedure.

To sort table rows via column headers in tabular views

- In the tabular view, click the header of the column according to which you want to sort table rows. The Sorting icon is displayed in the header, indicating the current sorting direction. Click the column header to change the sorting direction:
  - [IMAGE alt='' src=''] indicates that cells in the column are sorted in ascending order. This is the default mode.
  - [IMAGE alt='' src=''] indicates that cells in the column are sorted in descending order. To sort according to multiple columns, hold Shift when clicking the column header.

##### Pinning table columns

###### Pinning table columns via column headers in tabular views

To pin table columns via column headers in tabular views

1. In the tabular view, right-click the header of the column you want to pin.
2. In the dropdown menu, click **Pin Column**, and in its submenu, click one of the following:
  1. **Pin to the Left**
  2. **Pin to the Right**
  3. **No Pin**

##### Modifying table column width

###### Modifying table column width via column headers in tabular views

To modify table column width via column headers in tabular views

1. In the tabular view, do one of the following:
  1. In the table header, drag the vertical grid lines separating the columns and adjust the width as needed.
  2. Right-click the header of a column and in the shortcut menu, click one of the following:
    1. Size Column to Fit to resize the column width, adjusting it to its content.
    2. Size All Columns to Fit to resize the width of all columns, adjusting them to their content.

##### Filtering tabular view column cells

#### WARNING: Tabular view loading

Tabular view loading

Tabular views load only the content that is immediately visible; additional rows and cell data are loaded as you scroll. If you filter or sort partially loaded column cells, a notification icon [IMAGE alt='' src=''] appears in the column header.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To resolve this, do one of the following:

- Right-click the column header and in the shortcut menu, click the Load All Cells command.
- Scroll through the entire table to load all content.

Once the cells are fully loaded, retry to filter or sort.

Filtering does not persist and resets automatically once you reopen the tabular view.

###### Filtering tabular view column cells

To filter tabular view column cells

1. In the tabular view, right-click the header of the column whose cells you want to filter and click Filter Column in the shortcut menu.
2. Do one of the following:
  1. Expand the Filter by Values section. Do one of the following:
    1. In the Values column, select the check boxes for the values you want to display in the column.
    2. Type the value name in the input field to narrow the search, select the Apply filter on refined values check box, and select the check boxes for the values you want to display in the column.
  2. Expand the Filter by Conditions section.
    1. Select the condition from the dropdown menu and type the value you are looking for in the input field.
3. Click OK .
4. (Optional) To reset the filter, right-click the column header with the applied filter. In the shortcut menu, do one of the following:
  1. Click Reset Column Filter .
  2. Click Filter Column , then Reset . Click OK .

Table Columns dialog_create custom column.png" /></ac:image></p><h2>Modifying table columns</h2><h3><span>Hiding table columns</span></h3><h4><span>Hiding table columns via the column headers in tabular views</span></h4><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">To hide table columns via the column headers in tabular views</span></p><hr /><ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Right-click the header of the column you want to hide from a tabular view, and in the shortcut menu, click <strong>Hide Column</strong>.<br />The table column is hidden.</span></li></ul><h4><span>Hiding/displaying table columns via the Columns button in tabular views</span></h4><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">To hide/display table columns via the Columns button in tabular views</span></p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="485f7c8d-7ce6-40c8-961c-9a818e93c44a"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">In the tabular view's toolbar, click the <strong>Columns</strong> button.</span></li><li data-uuid="03f4d5ce-096f-4f12-a3d9-2ce808fd30aa"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">In the dropdown menu, click the column you want to hide from or display in the table.<br /></span>The displayed columns are highlighted in the dropdown menu. <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Columns button displayed hidden columns.png" /></ac:image></li></ol><h3><span>Deleting table columns</span></h3><h4><span>Deleting table columns via the column headers in tabular views</span></h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="64dff24f-bce9-4a5e-9b2c-7115f796d5d1"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The <strong>Delete Column </strong>command is not available for inherited columns or for columns that redefine inherited ones.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">To delete table columns via the column headers in tabular views</span></p><hr /><ul><li data-uuid="78fb0c8c-111f-40e7-b953-0ea49e615111"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Right-click the header of the column you want to delete from a tabular view, and in the shortcut menu, click <strong>Delete Column</strong>.<br />The column is deleted from the model.</span></li></ul><h4>Deleting table columns via the Configure Table Columns dialog</h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c7715cfb-aaba-4da4-a1d5-12fb31f1e1ca"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The <strong>Delete </strong>button is not available for inherited columns or for columns that redefine inherited ones.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To delete table columns via the Configure Table Columns dialog</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="42b9baa6-445f-43bb-827f-95a5ad5d4d6b">In a tabular view's toolbar, click the <strong>Columns </strong>button, and in its dropdown menu, click <strong>Configure Table Columns</strong>. The <strong>Configure Table Columns </strong>dialog opens.</li><li data-uuid="17089a70-5b09-4558-b292-15f9782e493c">In the <strong>Configure Table Columns </strong>dialog, under the <strong>Column Name </strong>node, locate the column you want to delete from the table, then click the <strong>Delete</strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Configure Table Columns dialog delete column button.png" /></ac:image> button in the <strong>Added To Model </strong>column.</li><li data-uuid="f9f89a12-d623-4817-8a86-c5e997072a24">Click <strong>OK</strong>.<br />The column is deleted from the model.</li></ol><h3>Sorting table rows</h3><h4>Sorting table rows via column headers in tabular views </h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b35e4778-b8f6-4618-acd7-000c552e7dbf"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If the column header displays a <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="17"><ri:attachment ri:filename="filtering or sorting partially loaded cells notification.png" /></ac:image> notification after attempting to <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">sort tabular rows via column headers, see the explanation and resolution in the <strong>Tabular view loading </strong>warning box in the <ac:link ac:anchor="Filtering tabular view column cells" /> procedure.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">To sort table rows via column headers in tabular views</span></p><hr /><ul><li data-uuid="a92ea1fb-cc14-4428-8d25-4c26e5b93617">In the tabular view, click the header of the column according to which you want to sort table rows. The <strong>Sorting</strong> icon is displayed in the header, indicating the current sorting direction. Click the column header to change the sorting direction:<ul><li data-uuid="3ba11ebb-032c-4d46-b6e7-9ffa04dec96c"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="sorting_1.png" /></ac:image> indicates that cells in the column are sorted in ascending order. This is the default mode.</span></p></li><li data-uuid="8cbb771a-38a6-42ae-968d-3e76e970f4e2"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="sorting_2.png" /></ac:image> indicates that cells in the column are sorted in descending order.<br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="56b6ea12-a54a-4df5-8760-31f3850dfaab"><ac:rich-text-body><p>To sort according to multiple columns, hold Shift when clicking the column header.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ul></li></ul><h3>Pinning table columns</h3><h4>Pinning table columns via column headers in tabular views </h4><p>To pin table columns via column headers in tabular views </p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="313116b6-c0fc-4463-9191-ac762540833a">In the tabular view, right-click the header of the column you want to pin.</li><li data-uuid="972c8e56-d309-48c3-b6e4-a9e1800ef941"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">In the dropdown menu, click <strong>Pin Column</strong>, and in its submenu, click one of the following:</span><ol><li data-uuid="071a0baf-ca49-4e2c-8569-da47933827d3"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><strong>Pin to the Left</strong></span></li><li data-uuid="ef7b72c2-6403-47b7-98e6-38bf895f70e1"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><strong>Pin to the Right</strong></span></li><li data-uuid="590964f3-807a-4496-8ad7-60fccb63bdd1"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><strong>No Pin</strong></span></li></ol></li></ol><h3>Modifying table column width</h3><h4>Modifying table column width via column headers in tabular views </h4><p>To modify table column width via column headers in tabular views </p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="135cc41d-1fe1-41a7-9a11-a64a3d2e8f8d">In the tabular view, do one of the following:<ol><li data-uuid="a691bce1-75b1-478e-a768-167da04942b6">In the table header, drag the vertical grid lines separating the columns and adjust the width as needed.</li><li data-uuid="59b25b42-f061-4aa6-ba31-c9c1a0fd0427">Right-click the header of a column and in the shortcut menu, click one of the following:<ol><li data-uuid="4fbb3863-b129-4b5d-9b04-1948adedf046"><strong>Size Column to Fit </strong>to resize the column width, adjusting it to its content.</li><li data-uuid="f00a308d-d2b5-43b7-8d21-fe16055af64f"><strong>Size All Columns to Fit </strong>to resize the width of all columns, adjusting them to their content.</li></ol></li></ol></li></ol><h3 class="auto-cursor-target"><span>Filtering tabular view column cells</span></h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="72ed2037-cbfc-48ec-be3f-9ec8c2d52b6a"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Tabular view loading</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Tabular views load only the content that is immediately visible; additional rows and cell data are loaded as you scroll. If you filter or sort partially loaded column cells, a notification icon <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="filtering or sorting partially loaded cells notification.png" /></ac:image> appears in the column header.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="filtering or sorting partially loaded cells.png" /></ac:image></p><p>To resolve this, do one of the following:</p><ul><li data-uuid="86bb4d2d-b9b1-432e-adab-e6a191345bf4">Right-click the column header and in the shortcut menu, click the <strong>Load All Cells </strong>command.</li><li data-uuid="48fd03c3-8ddb-4d2c-82cb-8358d92c05d7">Scroll through the entire table to load all content.</li></ul><p>Once the cells are fully loaded, retry to filter or sort.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0cbc27c4-183e-4b89-b2c8-5ae7114bcf4b"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Filtering does not persist and resets automatically once you reopen the tabular view.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4 class="auto-cursor-target"><span>Filtering tabular view column cells</span></h4><p>To filter <span>tabular view column cells</span></p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="3ddb0343-ee20-47ff-ac7a-7044bb54801b">In the tabular view, right-click the header of the<strong> </strong>column whose cells you want to filter and click <strong>Filter Column</strong> in the shortcut menu.</li><li data-uuid="83dbf5d3-6134-4e9a-b633-72297ead9855">Do one of the following:<ol><li>Expand the <strong>Filter by Values</strong> section. Do one of the following:<ol><li>In the <strong>Values</strong> column, select the check boxes for the values you want to display in the column.</li><li>Type the value name in the input field to narrow the search, select the <strong>Apply filter on refined values</strong> check box, and select the check boxes for the values you want to display in the column.</li></ol></li><li>Expand the <strong>Filter by Conditions</strong> section.<ol><li>Select the condition from the dropdown menu and type the value you are looking for in the input field. </li></ol></li></ol></li><li data-uuid="e84a96e9-5205-440a-af82-4be444c94086">Click <strong>OK</strong>.</li><li data-uuid="8ed36fa7-96ae-4349-8848-c412eb88e588">(Optional) To reset the filter, right-click the column header with the applied filter. In the shortcut menu, do one of the following:<ol><li>Click <strong>Reset Column Filter</strong>.</li><li>Click <strong>Filter Column</strong>, then <strong>Reset</strong>. Click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol></li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249577211 space=SYSML2P version=5 -->
## PAGE 00637: Modifiers

- page_id: `249577211`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/249577211/Modifiers
- version_number: 5
- version_date: `2026-06-17T11:37:04.642+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > Element management > Working with elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Usages can have additional properties indicated by the keywords added in the usage declaration. The keywords should be specified in the following order:

1. *in, out, inout* – indicates a direction for a directed usage.
  1. *in* – specifies what is input to an instance.
  2. *out* – specifies what is output by an instance.
  3. *inout* – specifies what is both input to and output by an instance.
2. *derived* – specifies a derived usage that is typically expected to have a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Binding' space=''] expression that completely determines its value at all times.
3. *abstract* – specifies an abstract usage whose value must also be a value of a concrete usage that specializes the abstract usage. If a usage is not abstract, it is a concrete usage.
4. *constant –* specifies that the usage is constant. Its values are the same during the entire existence of the featuring instance.
5. *ref* – specifies a referential usage. Directed usages are referential by default, whether the*ref*keyword is specified or not. If a usage is not referential, it is a composite usage, which is deleted once its featuring instances are deleted.

- For more information on using multiple keywords and their order in element declaration, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing textual notation' space=''] section on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing textual notation' space=''] page.
- The modifier keyword's display on the element symbol can be controlled via the Show Modifiers symbol style. See [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Modifying styles of individual symbols' space=''] section on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Modifying styles of individual symbols' space=''] page.

##### Specifying the direction modifier for features

###### Specifying the direction modifier for features via the Textual Editor

To specify the direction modifier for features via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, before the element metaclass keyword, specify the needed direction keyword: *in, out*, or *inout.*
2. Click the Synchronize button. partdefTank:>Container{ 
initemfuelFlow:Fuel; 
}

###### Specifying the direction modifier for features via the element shortcut menu

To specify the direction modifier for features via the element shortcut menu

- Right-click a usage element in the Containment tree or its symbol in a view and in its shortcut menu, in the Properties group, click**Direction**, and in its submenu, clickeither *in*, *out*, or *inout* .

##### Specifying the derived modifier for features

###### Specifying the derived modifier for features via the Textual Editor

To specify the derived modifier for features via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, before the element metaclass keyword, specify the derived keyword.
2. Click the Synchronize button. itemdefTestRecord{ 
//... 
derivedattributeaverageScore[1] :Rational; 
}

##### Specifying the abstract modifier for features

###### Specifying the abstract modifier for features via the Textual Editor

To specify the abstract modifier for features via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, before the element metaclass keyword, specify the abstract keyword.
2. Click the Synchronize button. abstractpartdefVehicle;

##### Specifying the constant modifier for features

###### Specifying the constant modifier for features via the Textual Editor

To specify the constant modifier for features via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, before the element metaclass keyword, specify the constant keyword.
2. Click the Synchronize button. occurrencedefApprovedFlight:>Flight{ 
constant ref partapprovedAircraftredefinesaircraft; 
}

##### Specifying the ref modifier for features

#### NOTE: Referential, composite and reference usages

Referential, composite and reference usages

50%

Referential usage references a usage that is an instance of another composition. If a usage is not referential, it is composite, which is deleted once its featuring instances are deleted.

partdefVehicle{ 
parteng:Engine;// composite 
refpartdriver:Person;// referential 
}

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

50%

Reference usages are declared without the element kind keyword and are not restricted to being defined by a definition of any specific kind. Its declaration may optionally include the *ref*keyword, however, reference usages are implicitly referential.

part def Container { 
 item content; 
} 
part def Tank :> Container { 
 fuel : Fuel :>> content; // reference usage 
}

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Specifying usages as composite or referential via the Textual Editor

To specify usages as composite or referential via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, before the element metaclass keyword, specify one of the following:
  1. For a referential usage, specify the keyword ref .
  2. For a composite usage, remove the keyword ref .
2. Click the Synchronize button. partdefVehicle{ 
parteng:Engine;// composite 
refpartdriver:Person;// referential 
}

###### Specifying usages as composite or referential via the element shortcut menu

To specify usages as composite or referential via the element shortcut menu

1. Right-click a usage in the Containment tree or its symbol in a view and in the shortcut menu, and do one of the following:
  1. For a referential usage, deselect the **Is Composite** property.
  2. For a composite usage, select the **Is Composite** property.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE
