style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><h3>Specifying the constant modifier for features</h3><h4>Specifying the constant modifier for features via the Textual Editor</h4><p>To specify the constant modifier for features via the Textual Editor</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Textual Editor, before the element metaclass keyword, specify the <em>constant </em>keyword.</li><li>Click the <strong>Synchronize </strong>button.<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="95ed3935-f047-4fec-ad98-00c4bdeaa3f6"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">occurrence</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">ApprovedFlight</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Flight</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">constant ref part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">approvedAircraft</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">redefines</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">aircraft</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><h3>Specifying the ref modifier for features</h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1a5b6681-7348-4df6-96e5-58a171173d7f"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Referential, composite and reference usages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="section" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c2333f04-9e7e-4e21-81c1-69c1305e7f3a"><ac:rich-text-body><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a70bb80c-8f1d-4ad0-a7d5-ed19c621d6cc"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">50%</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Referential usage references a usage that is an instance of another composition. If a usage is not referential, it is composite<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">, which is deleted once its featuring instances are deleted.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bc8328b0-a6a4-4ebd-a726-ed8fbda5199f"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Vehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">eng</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Engine</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;           </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// composite</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">ref</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">driver</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Person</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;   </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// referential</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="referential usage.png" /></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="82b387ab-d856-41ee-954b-5a70981a71bb"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">50%</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Reference usages are declared without the element kind keyword and are not restricted to being defined by a definition of any specific kind. Its declaration may optionally include the <em>ref </em>keyword, however, reference usages are implicitly referential. </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d4b4514f-cfce-439f-a9ca-2f16c931494b"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Container</span> {<br />    <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">item</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">content</span>;<br />}<br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Tank</span> :&gt; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Container</span> {<br />    <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">fuel</span> : <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Fuel</span> :&gt;&gt; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">content</span>; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// reference usage</span><br />}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="reference usage.png" /></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Specifying usages as composite or referential via the Textual Editor</h4><p>To specify usages as composite or referential via the Textual Editor</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Textual Editor, before the element metaclass keyword, specify one of the following:<ol><li>For a referential usage, specify the keyword <em>ref</em>. </li><li>For a composite usage, remove the keyword <em>ref</em>. </li></ol></li><li>Click the <strong>Synchronize </strong>button.<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="21b28bad-5999-41f1-b924-1b8bebdbbcf6"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Vehicle</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">eng</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Engine</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;           </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// composite</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">ref</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">driver</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Person</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;   </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// referential</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><h4>Specifying usages as composite or referential via the element shortcut menu</h4><p>To specify usages as composite or referential via the element shortcut menu</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click a usage in the Containment tree or its symbol in a view and in the shortcut menu, and do one of the following:<ol><li>For a referential usage, <span>deselect the <strong>Is Composite</strong> property.</span></li><li>For a composite usage, <span>select the <strong>Is Composite</strong> property.</span></li></ol></li></ol>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249577873 space=SYSML2P version=18 -->
## PAGE 00638: Modifying styles of individual symbols

- page_id: `249577873`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/249577873/Modifying+styles+of+individual+symbols
- version_number: 18
- version_date: `2026-06-18T11:15:32.128+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > Model composition > Views > Symbol styles
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can customize the styles of individual symbols. Using the Symbol Style dialog, you can specify style attributes for specific symbols, determining their appearance.

Changing the styles for an individual symbol via the Symbol Style dialog overwrites both predefined and custom model-based symbol styles; the modifications persist even after updating [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Applying style sheets' space='']. To reset, see the procedure.

##### Opening the Symbol Style dialog

To open the Symbol Style dialog

Do one of the following:

- Right-click the selected element symbol(s). In the shortcut menu, click **Symbol Style**. To invoke the Symbol Style dialog for a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Docked symbols' space=''], you must right-click the symbol's header.
- Select the element symbol(s) and press Alt+Enter .

##### Modifying individual symbol styles

To modify individual symbol styles

1. Select the symbol(s) whose styles you want to modify and .
2. Locate the attribute you want to modify and change its value to the needed one in the Value column.
3. Click Close .

##### Resetting changes made in the Symbol Style dialog

While changing the styles for an individual symbol via the Symbol Style dialog overwrites both predefined and custom model-based symbol styles, you can easily undo them following the procedure below.

To reset changes made in the Symbol Style dialog

1. .
2. Locate the attribute you want to reset to the style of the currently applied style sheet. Its check box in the Default column is deselected. [ATTACHMENT filename='Resetting_changes.png']
3. Select the check box in the Default column for the needed attribute.
4. Click Close .

##### The Symbol Style dialog

The table below lists the style attributes available for modification and their descriptions.

- Modifying a symbol style changes its Default value to false .
- You can hide unwanted symbol styles from the Symbol Style dialog for specific element kinds. See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Hiding style attributes from the Symbol Style dialog' space=''] page.

50%

In the **Symbol Style** dialog, symbol styles are ordered into the following groups:

- **Main**: this attribute group displays symbol styles available to the majority of element kinds.
- **Compartment-based attributes**: the availability of specific symbol style groups depends on the element kind, as [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Compartments' space='']. Some of the symbol styles available to individual compartment-based symbol style groups may be the same as in the Main group, while others are compartment-specific. Compartment-based style groups can be:
  - Doc,
  - Objective,
  - Subject,
  - Actors,
  - Stakeholders,
  - Include use cases,
  - Parts,
  - Ports,
  - Parameters,
  - Attributes,
  - Ends,
  - Satisfy requirements ,
  - Items,
  - Constraints,
  - Require constraints,
  - Assume constraints,
  - Assert constraints,
  - Actions,
  - Perform actions,
  - Exhibit states,
  - While condition,
  - Until condition,
  - For iterator,
  - If condition
  - Frames,
  - Enums,
  - Metadata,
  - Result,
  - Rules,
  - Filters,
  - Features,
  - Members.

50%

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

| Name | Value options | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Main |  |  |
| Line Width | Positive number | Specify the width of the symbol's outline. |
| Fill Color | SwatchesHSVHSLRGBCMYK | Specify the color of the symbol's header.To enable a gradient for this attribute, enable the view style attribute. |
| Use Fill Color | true/false | Specify whether to use a specific color for the symbol's header. |
| Pen Color | SwatchesHSVHSLRGBCMYK | Specify the color of the symbol's outline. |
| Text Color | SwatchesHSVHSLRGBCMYK | Specify the color of the symbol's text. |
| Font | Multiple font optionsMultiple style options (italic, bold, etc.)Multiple size options | Specify the font style of the symbol's text. |
| Wrap | true/false | Specify whether text and expressions should wrap to fit within the width of the element symbol. |
| Label Position | OutsideInsideInside Enclosing Shape | Specify the position of labels relative to the shape. Outside: outside the shape and not within its enclosing shape. Inside: within the shape. Inside Enclosing Shape: outside the shape but within its enclosing shape.Available for: (e.g., port, attribute, ref, item), (labels). |
| Label Orientation | HorizontalVerticalAutomatic | Specify the label orientation. Horizontal: displays labels horizontally. Vertical: displays labels vertically. Automatic: adjusts the label to match the orientation of the symbol it belongs to.Available for: (e.g., port, attribute, ref, item), (labels), paths. |
| Compartment Fill Color | SwatchesHSVHSLRGBCMYK | Specify the color of the symbol's compartments. |
| Compartment Text Color | SwatchesHSVHSLRGBCMYK | Specify the color for the text in the symbol's compartments. |
| Show Name In Tab | true/false | Specify whether the package name and keywords should be displayed in the tab. |
| Show Short Name | true/false | Specify whether the element's should be displayed on the element symbol. |
| Show Name | true/false | Specify whether the element's should be displayed on the element symbol. |
| Show Alias Short Name | true/false | Specify whether the element's short name should be displayed on the element symbol. |
| Show Alias Name | true/false | Specify whether the element's name should be displayed on the element symbol. |
| Show Modifiers | true/false | Specify whether the element's (e.g., in, ref, abstract, etc.) should be displayed on the element symbol. The attribute does not control the individual and portion kind (timeslice, snapshot) modifiers. |
| Show Full Text | true/false | Specify whether element values should be displayed in full or abbreviated. Modifying this attribute affects the values displayed in both the selected symbol's name and its compartments.Modifying this attribute does not affect element values displayed in the metadata compartment. Values in this compartment are always displayed in full.The attribute is available for specific symbols based on the element kind. |
| Content Fill Color | SwatchesHSVHSLRGBCMYK | Specify the color of the symbol's body.To enable a gradient for this attribute, enable the view style attribute.The attribute is available for specific symbols based on the element kind. |
| Text Vertical Position | TopCenterBottom | Specify the element name position for the symbol.The attribute is available for specific symbols based on the element kind.The attribute is only effective for element symbols displayed without their compartments. |
| Show Specialization | true/false | Specify whether the textual symbols should be displayed in the element name (e.g., the textual symbol :> or the keyword specializes, in the case of subclassification). The attribute is available for specific symbols based on the element kind. |
| Show Qualified Name for Specialization | true/false | Specify whether the specialization's full should be displayed in the element name.Enabling the attribute also requires the 'Show Specialization' attribute to be enabled.The attribute is available for specific symbols based on the element kind. |
| Orientation | HorizontalVertical | Specify the orientation for the symbol.The attribute is available for specific symbols based on the element kind. |
| Path Style | RectilinearObliqueBezier | Specify the style of the path.The attribute is available for specific symbols based on the element kind. |
| Show Value | true/false | Specify whether the element's defined value should be displayed.The attribute is available for specific symbols based on the element kind. |
| Show Inherited Sign | true/false | Specify whether the inherited sign should be displayed on the symbol.The attribute is available for specific symbols based on the element kind. |
| Show Payload | true/false | Specify whether the payload name and sign should be displayed on the connector path.The attribute is available for specific symbols based on the element kind. |
| Text Horizontal Position | LeftCenterRight | Specify the text position for the vertical and horizontal separator symbols. |
| Line Style | SolidDashedDottedetc. | Specify the line style for:the vertical and horizontal separator symbols.the swimlane symbols. |
| Is Rounded | true/false | Specify whether the rectangular shape symbol's corners should be rounded. |
| Show Locale | true/false | Specify whether the annotating element's locale should be displayed on the element symbol. Available for: Comment, Doc. |
| Show Direction Icon | true/false | Specify whether the direction icon should be displayed on the element symbol.Available for: (e.g., port, attribute, ref, item). |
| Compartment-based symbol styles (in compartment-based symbol style groups, e.g., Documentation, Objective, Subject, Actors, etc.) |  |  |
| Show Compartment | true/false | Specify whether the compartment should be displayed or suppressed. |
| Show Inherited | true/false | Specify whether the inherited elements should be displayed on the symbol. |
| Show Only Owned | true/false | Specify whether only the owned elements or imported ones, too, should be displayed in the symbol compartment.The attribute is only available for the package symbol and applies to its Members compartment. |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

data-uuid="c33972c8-c705-4598-8c27-ad6afb76bd5a"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Constraints, </span></li><li data-uuid="9d38370c-cf54-40bb-8649-d85e2d3fd01a"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Require constraints, </span></li><li data-uuid="7e512056-31cc-4b86-a8be-3e751e8b4807"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Assume constraints, </span></li><li data-uuid="9ad57423-6495-4426-9f7c-27d292f89bf1"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Assert constraints, </span></li><li data-uuid="eb87a39e-8052-43e7-9eeb-f3d7ad02acd6">Actions,</li><li data-uuid="5929028e-6fa1-497b-a48d-edb656b5875e">Perform actions,</li><li data-uuid="8346481a-9941-4662-b677-abf9db5b1a1f">Exhibit states,</li><li data-uuid="3def1a7f-38f0-4e1b-b968-fbce29a3b063">While condition,</li><li data-uuid="d4cc5ddc-931e-40c7-96f3-49f0ab1c09af">Until condition,</li><li data-uuid="b2751176-efba-4e35-b237-499e91d622f5">For iterator,</li><li data-uuid="1deb6c99-b121-4148-9913-a9837d8caf5b">If condition</li><li data-uuid="84bdea45-7c19-4b44-9be5-e8692c7780e9">Frames,</li><li data-uuid="e91c16fa-b567-45ff-93f9-030b34383578">Enums, </li><li data-uuid="1a648176-e8a1-4f2e-a0ac-c6f9a367e37b">Metadata,</li><li data-uuid="37ddfb02-9ee2-49e7-90fd-f20372921b6a">Result,</li><li data-uuid="dfff0fed-7f51-4410-b9f3-da6d803ac731">Rules,</li><li data-uuid="68564a40-28e3-4167-a6cf-a748b989a3f9">Filters,</li><li data-uuid="25692b52-3732-40ef-90a0-8b9bd00c682b">Features,</li><li data-uuid="66a79cb5-5d00-459c-8076-eb0efaaf72cc">Members.</li></ul></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0c006efe-2369-416e-b227-b7a588026ab0"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">50%</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="symbol style dialog.png" /></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><table class="relative-table wrapped" style="width: 74.0672%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 22.4322%;" /><col style="width: 25.7747%;" /><col style="width: 51.7722%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Name</th><th>Value options</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><th colspan="3"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Main</span></th></tr><tr><td>Line Width</td><td>Positive number</td><td>Specify the width of the symbol's outline.</td></tr><tr><td>Fill Color</td><td>Swatches<br />HSV<br />HSL<br />RGB<br />CMYK</td><td><p>Specify the color of the symbol's header.</p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">To enable a gradient for this attribute, enable the <ac:link ac:anchor="The View Style dialog"><ri:page ri:content-title="Modifying styles of individual views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Use Gradient Fill]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> view style attribute.</span></p></td></tr><tr><td>Use Fill Color</td><td>true/false</td><td>Specify whether to use a specific color for the symbol's header.</td></tr><tr><td>Pen Color</td><td><p>Swatches<br />HSV<br />HSL<br />RGB<br />CMYK</p></td><td>Specify the color of the symbol's outline.</td></tr><tr><td>Text Color</td><td>Swatches<br />HSV<br />HSL<br />RGB<br />CMYK</td><td>Specify the color of the symbol's text.</td></tr><tr><td><p>Font</p></td><td><p>Multiple font options<br />Multiple style options (italic, bold, etc.)<br />Multiple size options</p></td><td>Specify the font style of the symbol's text.</td></tr><tr><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Wrap</span></p></td><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">true/false</span></p></td><td>Specify whether text and expressions should wrap to fit within the width of the element symbol.</td></tr><tr><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Label Position</span></p></td><td><p>Outside<br />Inside<br />Inside Enclosing Shape</p></td><td><p>Specify the position of labels relative to the shape. Outside: outside the shape and not within its enclosing shape. Inside: within the shape. Inside Enclosing Shape: outside the shape but within its enclosing shape.</p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Available for: </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Style sheet styles tables" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[shapes on border]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> (e.g., port, attribute, ref, item), <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Style sheet styles tables" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[path end shapes]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> (labels).</span></p></td></tr><tr><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Label Orientation</span></p></td><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Horizontal<br />Vertical<br />Automatic</span></span></p></td><td><p>Specify the label orientation. Horizontal: displays labels horizontally. Vertical: displays labels vertically. Automatic: adjusts the label to match the orientation of the symbol it belongs to.</p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Available for: </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Style sheet styles tables" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[shapes on border]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> (e.g., port, attribute, ref, item), <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Style sheet styles tables" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[path end shapes]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> (labels), paths.</span></p></td></tr><tr><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Compartment Fill Color</span></p></td><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Swatches<br />HSV<br />HSL<br />RGB<br />CMYK</span></p></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Specify the color of the symbol's compartments.</span></td></tr><tr><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Compartment Text Color</span></p></td><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Swatches<br />HSV<br />HSL<br />RGB<br />CMYK</span></p></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Specify the color for the text in the symbol's compartments.</span></td></tr><tr><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Show Name In Tab</span></p></td><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">true/false</span></p></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Specify whether the package name and keywords should be displayed in the tab.</span></td></tr><tr><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Show Short Name</span></p></td><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">true/false</span></span></p></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Specify whether the element's <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Element names" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[declared short name]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> should be displayed on the element symbol.</span></td></tr><tr><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Show Name </span></p></td><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">true/false</span></span></p></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Specify whether the element's <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Element names" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[declared name]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> should be displayed on the element symbol.</span></span></td></tr><tr><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Show Alias Short Name</span></p></td><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">true/false</span></span></p></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Specify whether the element's <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Alias" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[alias]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> short name should be displayed on the element symbol.</span></span></td></tr><tr><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Show Alias Name</span></p></td><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">true/false</span></span></p></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Specify whether the element's <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Alias" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[alias]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> name should be displayed on the element symbol.</span></span></td></tr><tr><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Show Modifiers</span></p></td><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">true/false</span></span></p></td><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Specify whether the element's <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Modifiers" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[modifiers]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">(e.g., <em>in</em>, <em>ref</em>, <em>abstract, </em>etc.) </span>should be displayed on the element symbol. The attribute does not control the <em>individual </em>and portion kind </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">(<em>timeslice</em>, <em>snapshot</em>) modifiers.</span></span></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Show Full Text</p></td><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">true/false</span></span></p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Specify whether element values should be displayed in full or abbreviated. Modifying this attribute affects the values displayed in both the selected symbol's name and its compartments.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5633ec45-d80c-4dc5-b058-5b908b207597"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li data-uuid="153f914e-b902-4550-b735-eeadce7538ec">Modifying this attribute does not affect element values displayed in the metadata compartment. Values in this compartment are always displayed in full.</li><li data-uuid="1a005f85-db54-4a0b-b02d-1d664c444292"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The attribute is available for specific symbols based on the element kind.</span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Content Fill Color</span></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Swatches</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">HSV</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">HSL</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">RGB</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">CMYK</span></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Specify the color of the symbol's body.</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">To enable a gradient for this attribute, enable the <ac:link ac:anchor="The View Style dialog"><ri:page ri:content-title="Modifying styles of individual views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Use Gradient Fill]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> view style attribute.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="181a2543-8bfe-4b48-a61f-b4b7d7ef8d1d"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The attribute is available for specific symbols based on the element kind.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Text Vertical Position</span></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Top<br />Center<br />Bottom</span></span></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Specify the element name position for the symbol.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d19e5dc1-19de-4ba6-9120-4c594fbf980f"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li data-uuid="9e07d7e7-fd4a-4ac7-8711-a9e53e2a9f8b"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The attribute is available for specific symbols based on the element kind.</span></li><li data-uuid="61c52eca-72d5-457d-ac1e-0cc5dd3572bc"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The attribute is only effective for element symbols displayed without their compartments. </span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr><td>Show Specialization</td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">true/false</span></span></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Specify whether the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specializations" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[specialization]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> textual symbols should be displayed in the element name (e.g., the textual symbol :&gt; or the keyword <em>specializes</em>, in the case of subclassification). </span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8f6ae28a-ad88-4e21-a893-42de58a6d9bc"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The attribute is available for specific symbols based on the element kind.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr><td>Show Qualified Name for Specialization</td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">true/false</span></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Specify whether the specialization's full <ac:link ac:anchor="Name resolution"><ri:page ri:content-title="Managing textual notation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[qualified name]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> should be displayed in the element name.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="25bd0556-e0e4-4d6e-afe2-7ae57f550240"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Enabling the attribute also requires the 'Show Specialization' attribute to be enabled.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The attribute is available for specific symbols based on the element kind.</span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Orientation</span></td><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Horizontal</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Vertical</span></p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Specify the orientation for the symbol.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bcc28d68-0cb4-4244-b700-d4ea239d9f47"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The attribute is available for specific symbols based on the element <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">kind</span>.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Path Style</span></td><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Rectilinear</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Oblique</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Bezier</span></p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Specify the style of the path.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="afbeef39-f520-4253-a6c4-43758a8248fb"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The attribute is available for specific symbols based on the element <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">kind</span>.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Show Value</span></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">true/false</span></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Specify whether the element's defined value should be displayed.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9e810e94-b2a3-43a4-9514-a75e979c1a56"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The attribute is available for specific symbols based on the element <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">kind</span>.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Show Inherited Sign</span></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">true/false</span></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Specify whether the inherited sign should be displayed on the symbol.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="62bf6d3d-8743-4662-9e53-fb83d29258b8"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The attribute is available for specific symbols based on the element <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">kind</span>.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Show Payload</span></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">true/false</span></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Specify whether the payload name and sign should be displayed on the connector path.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="55673add-bc24-4b51-b3b1-1269c2ed4d91"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The attribute is available for specific symbols based on the element <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">kind</span>.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Text Horizontal Position<br /></span></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Left<br />Center<br />Right</span></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Specify the text position for the vertical and horizontal separator symbols.</span></p></div></td></tr><tr><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Line Style<br /></span></td><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Solid<br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Dashed<br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Dotted<br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">etc.</span></p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Specify the line style for:</span></p><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;"><ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">the vertical and horizontal separator symbols.</span></li><li data-uuid="d7094c58-faee-4572-9884-2d8c9018bb99"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">the swimlane symbols.</span></li></ul></li></ul></div></td></tr><tr><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Is Rounded<br /></span></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">true/false</span></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Specify whether the rectangular shape symbol's corners should be rounded.</span></p></div></td></tr><tr><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Show Locale </span></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">true/false</span></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Specify whether the annotating element's locale should be displayed on the element symbol. </span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Available for: Comment, Doc.</span></p></div></td></tr><tr><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Show Direction Icon</span></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">true/false</span></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Specify whether the direction icon should be displayed on the element symbol.</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Available for: </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Style sheet styles tables" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[shapes on border]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> (e.g., port, attribute, ref, item).</span></p></div></td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-grey" colspan="3" data-highlight-colour="grey"><div class="content-wrapper" title=""><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><strong>Compartment-based symbol styles (in compartment-based symbol style groups, e.g., Documentation, Objective, Subject, Actors, etc.)</strong></span></p></div></td></tr><tr><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Show </span>Compartment</span></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">true/false</span></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Specify whether the compartment should be displayed or suppressed.</span></div></td></tr><tr><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Show Inherited</span></span></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">true/false</span></span></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Specify whether the inherited elements should be displayed on the symbol.</span></span></div></td></tr><tr><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Show Only Owned</span></span></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">true/false</span></span></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Specify whether only the owned elements or imported ones, too, should be displayed in the symbol compartment.</span></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fe7a65db-dbe1-4e47-bffb-d5f14279bad0"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The attribute is only available for the package symbol and applies to its Members compartment.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249577876 space=SYSML2P version=9 -->
## PAGE 00639: Modifying styles of individual views

- page_id: `249577876`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/249577876/Modifying+styles+of+individual+views
- version_number: 9
- version_date: `2026-05-22T10:40:49.511+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > Model composition > Views > Symbol styles
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The primary ways to customize the styles of individual views:

- Applying style sheets to a specific view.
- Managing style properties for a specific view via the View Style dialog.

Changing the styles for an individual view via the View Style dialog overwrites both predefined and custom model-based style sheets; the modifications persist even after updating [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Applying style sheets' space='']. To reset, see the procedure.

##### Applying style sheets to a specific view

- For more information, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Applying style sheets' space=''] procedure on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Applying style sheets' space=''] page.

##### Managing style properties for a specific view via the View Style dialog

###### Opening the View Style dialog

To open the View Style dialog

- Right-click the selected view. In the shortcut menu, click **View Style**.

###### Modifying an individual view's style

To modify an individual view's style

1. Select the view(s) whose styles you want to modify and .
2. Locate the property you want to modify and change its value to the needed one in the Value column.
3. Click Close .

###### Resetting changes made in the View Style dialog

While changing the styles for an individual view via the View Style dialog overwrites both predefined and custom model-based style sheets, you can easily undo them following the procedure below.

To reset changes made in the View Style dialog

1. .
2. Locate the property you want to reset to the style of the currently applied style sheet. Its check box in the Default column is deselected.
3. Select the check box in the Default column for the needed property.
4. Click Close .

###### The View Style dialog

The table below lists the style properties available for modification and their descriptions.

Modifying a view style property changes its **Default**value to *false*.

Currently, other view style properties displayed in the dialog besides the **Use Gradient Fill**are not functional.

| Name | Value options | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Main |  |  |
| Use Gradient Fill | true/false | Specify whether a gradient should be used for the Fill Color and Content Fill Color . |
| Show Line Jumps On | NoneHorizontal LineVertical Line | Specify whether line jumps should be used for intersecting lines (e.g., paths).None - line jumps are not used.Horizontal Line - line jumps are used only on horizontal lines (default).Vertical Line - line jumps are used only on vertical lines. |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249577218 space=SYSML2P version=3 -->
## PAGE 00640: Multiplicity

- page_id: `249577218`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/249577218/Multiplicity
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-09-30T14:10:01.240+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > Element management > Working with elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Usages can have a multiplicity specifying how many values they can have for the instances of theirfeaturing definition or usage.The multiplicity is indicated as a range of natural numbers in square brackets, e.g., [lowerBound..upperBound].

- The lower bound must be finite. If the lower bound is unspecified, then it is taken to be equal to the upper bound, except when the upper bound is *, in which case it is treated as 0.
- The upper bound must be equal to or greater than the lower bound number. It can be unbounded, indicated by the * sign.

If a multiplicity is not declared for a usage, then the usage inherits the multiplicity constraints of any other usages it subsets or redefines. If no tighter constraint is inherited, the effective default is the most general multiplicity [0..*]. However, a tighter default of [1..1] is implicitly declared for the usage if all of the following conditions hold:

- The usage is an attribute usage, an item usage (including a part usage, except if it is a connection usage), or a port usage.
- The usage is owned by a definition or another usage (not a package).
- The usage does not have any explicit owned subsettings or owned redefinitions.

The following keywords can also be used after the multiplicity or in place of it:

- *nonunique*– a non-unique usage indicates that the same usage value can appear more than once. Usages are unique by default, i.e., two usage values can be the same.
- *ordered*– an ordered usage indicates that the usage values can be placed in an order from 1 to the number of values. The features are unordered by default.

For information on cross multiplicity, which is used only within the context of cross features and is usually specified for the*end*elements of a connector, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Cross features' space=''] page.

##### Specifying usage multiplicity

###### Specifying usage multiplicity via the Textual Editor

To specify usage multiplicity via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, follow the part name with brackets.
2. Specify a range of natural numbers in square brackets, e.g., [lowerBound..upperBound] .
3. Click the Synchronize button.

partbodyAssy:BodyAssy{ 
partdoors[2..4] :Door; 
partbody; 
partsunroof[0..1] :Sunroof; 
}

###### Specifying usage multiplicity via the Specification panel

To specify usage multiplicity via the Specification panel

- See the procedure [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification panel' space=''] on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification panel' space=''] page.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249577440 space=SYSML2P version=5 -->
## PAGE 00641: N-ary connection

- page_id: `249577440`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/249577440/N-ary+connection
- version_number: 5
- version_date: `2025-09-24T13:12:29.623+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > Model composition > Structure > Connection
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An n-ary connection is a complex connector between three or more elements. The n-ary connection is displayed as a dot symbol with segments connecting the ends of the connection.

The kind keyword (e.g., *part*) is optional for connection ends. The keyword is mandatory if the ends have owned [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Cross features' space=''].

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Creating n-ary connections

###### Creating n-ary connections via the Textual Editor

To create n-ary connections via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare the connection keyword.
2. Specify the element name (optional), (optional), then the connect keyword, then state the features explicitly (using the connect...to syntax ) or in a comma-separated list of related features. If the connection has no name and no specialization, the keyword *connection*can be omitted.You can also name connection ends, associating them to features via[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specializations' space=''].
3. Click the Synchronize button.

connectiondefCanBus{ 
endparttransmission:Transmission; 
endpartelectricalPowerController:ElectricalPowerController; 
endpartinternalCombustionEngine:InternalCombustionEngine; 
}

part'power subsystem'{ 
partelectricalPowerController; 
parttransmission; 
partinternalCombustionEngine;

// named specialized (feature typing) connection usage with named endsconnecting features in a comma-separated list: 
connection'can bus':CanBusconnect(t::>transmission,epc::>electricalPowerController,ice::>internalCombustionEngine);

// unnamed unspecialized connection usage connecting featuresin a comma-separated list: 
connect(transmission,electricalPowerController,internalCombustionEngine);}

###### Creating n-ary connections via the view palette

To create n-ary connections via the view palette

1. In the view palette, under the Connectors group, click the **n-ary connection** button. If the needed button is not displayed, click the down-facing arrow next to the **connection** or the **connection****def**button and click the **n-ary connection** button in the dropdown menu.
2. Click in the view pane where you want the n-ary connection symbol to be created.
3. Select the n-ary connection symbol and in its smart manipulator, click the end command.
4. Draw the end from the n-ary connection symbol to the symbol of the element you want to connect. Repeat for all elements you want to connect.

##### Converting binary and n-ary connectors

###### Converting binary connectors to n-ary via the view

To convert binary connectors to n-ary via the view

1. In the view pane, right-click the binary connector you want to convert to an n-ary.
2. In the shortcut menu, click Refactor , then Convert to N-ary Dot . The binary connector is converted and the n-ary dot symbol is displayed.

###### Converting n-aryconnectorstobinaryvia the view

To convert n-ary connectors to binary via the view

1. In the view pane, right-click the n-ary dot symbol of the connector you want to convert to a binary.
2. In the shortcut menu, click Refactor , then Convert to Path . The n-ary connector dot symbol is converted and the binary path is displayed. The **Convert to Path**command is only available if the connector has no more than two ends.

##### Creating an n-ary connection definition

###### Creating n-ary connection definitions via the Textual Editor

To create n-ary connection definitions via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare the connection def keyword.
2. Name the element, then specify the connection definition features in the element body:
  1. Specify the connection definition ends by declaring the end keyword, followed by the end name and/or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specializations' space=''] of the needed element.
3. Click the **Synchronize**button. connectiondefCanBus{ 
endparttransmission:Transmission; 
endpartelectricalPowerController:ElectricalPowerController; 
endpartinternalCombustionEngine:InternalCombustionEngine; 
}

###### Auto-creating n-ary connection definition elements via the Extract Definition commands

To auto-create n-ary connection definition elements via the Extract Definition commands

- See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Extract Definition and Usage' space='']page.

###### Creating n-ary connection definitions via the view palette

To create n-ary connection definitions via the view palette

1. In the view palette, under the Connectors group, click the**connection def** button. If the needed button is not displayed, click the down-facing arrow next to either the **connection**or the **n-ary connection******button and select the needed button in the dropdown menu.
2. Click in the view pane where you want to create the connection def symbol. The connection def symbol is created containing two end element symbols.
  1. Create additional ends via the Create Element button's end command. See the procedure [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Compartments' space=''] on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Compartments' space=''] page.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">An n-ary connection is a complex connector between three or more elements. The n-ary connection is displayed as a dot symbol with segments connecting the ends of the connection.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f4624f90-33ce-4490-9077-d120e7335029"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The kind keyword (e.g., <em>part</em>) is optional for connection ends. The keyword is mandatory if the ends have owned <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Cross features" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[cross features]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment palette</h4><p>To create n-ary connections via the view palette</p><hr /><ol><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the view palette, under the Connectors group, click the <strong>n-ary connection</strong> button. </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="769c3a3a-7b27-471d-b151-165748c95ab6"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If the needed button is not connection</strong> button in the dropdown menu.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>Click in the view pane where you want the n-ary connection symbol to be created.</li><li>Select the n-ary connection symbol and in its smart manipulator, click the <strong>end </strong>command. </li><li>Draw the end from the n-ary connection symbol to the symbol of the element you want to The n-ary connector dot symbol is converted and the binary path is displayed.<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0dcc5e6d-b75e-4dc3-9a76-b8575964a7c5"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The <strong>Convert to Path </strong>command is only available if the connector has no more than two ends. </p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><h3>Creating an n-ary connection definition</h3><h4>Creating n-ary connection definitions via the Textual Editor</h4><p>To create n-ary connection definitions via the Textual Editor</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare the <em>connection def</em> keyword.</li><li>Name the element, then specify the connection definition features in the element body:<ol><li>Specify the connection definition ends by declaring the <em>end</em> keyword, followed by element. </li></ol></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Click the <strong>Synchronize </strong>button. </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249577462 space=SYSML2P version=6 -->
````
## PAGE 00642: N-ary interface

- page_id: `249577462`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/249577462/N-ary+interface
- version_number: 6
- version_date: `2025-09-24T12:29:13.152+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > Model composition > Structure > Interface
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An n-ary interface is a complex connector between three or more ports. The n-ary interface is displayed as a dot symbol with segments connecting the ends of the interface.

- The ends of an interface definition or usage must be port usages.
- The port keyword is optional for interface ends. The keyword is mandatory if the ends have owned [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Cross features' space=''] .

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Creating n-ary interfaces

###### Creating n-ary interfaces via the Textual Editor

To create n-ary interfaces via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare the interface keyword.
2. Specify the element name (optional), [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specializations' space=''] (optional), then the connect keyword, then state the feature ports explicitly (using the connect...to syntax ) or in a comma-separated list of related features. If the interface has no name and no specialization, the keyword *connect*can be omitted.You can also name interface end ports, associating them to feature ports via[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Reference subsetting' space=''].
3. Click the Synchronize button.

interfacedefCanBusInterface{endporttransmissionPort:CanBusPorts; 
endportelectricalPowerControllerPort:CanBusPorts; 
endportinternalCombustionEnginePort:CanBusPorts; 
}

part'power subsystem'{ 
partelectricalPowerController{portfp;} 
parttransmission{portfp;} 
partinternalCombustionEngine{portfp;}

//named specialized (feature typing) interface usage with named end portsconnecting feature portsin a comma-separated list:interface'can bus':CanBusInterfaceconnect(transmissionPort::>transmission.fp,electricalPowerControllerPort::>electricalPowerController.fp,internalCombustionEnginePort::>internalCombustionEngine.fp);

// unnamed unspecialized interface usage connecting feature portsin a comma-separated list:interface(transmission.fp,electricalPowerController.fp,internalCombustionEngine.fp);}

###### Creating n-ary interfaces via the view palette

To create n-ary interfaces via the view palette

1. In the view palette, under the Connectors group, click the n-ary interface button. If it is not displayed, click the down-facing arrow next to the interface button and click the n-ary interface button in the dropdown menu.
2. Click in the view pane where you want the n-ary interface symbol to be created.
3. Select the n-ary interface symbol and in its smart manipulator, click the end command.
4. Draw the end from the n-ary interface symbol to the symbol of the port you want to connect. Repeat for all ports you want to connect.

##### Converting binary and n-ary interfaces

###### Converting binary interface to n-ary via the view

To convert binary interface to n-ary via the view

1. In the view pane, right-click the binary interface you want to convert to an n-ary.
2. In the shortcut menu, click Refactor , then Convert to N-ary Dot . The binary interface is converted and the n-ary dot symbol is displayed.

###### Converting n-aryinterfacetobinaryvia the view

To convert n-ary interface to binary via the view

1. In the view pane, right-click the n-ary dot symbol of the interface you want to convert to a binary.
2. In the shortcut menu, click Refactor , then Convert to Path . The n-ary interface dot symbol is converted and the binary path is displayed. The **Convert to Path**command is only available if the dependency has no more than two ends.

##### Creating n-ary interface definition

###### Creating n-ary interfaces definition via the Textual Editor

To create n-ary interfaces definition via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare the interface def keyword followed by its name.
2. Name the element, then specify the interface definition features in the element body:
  1. Specify the interface definition ends by declaring the end keyword, followed by the end name and/or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specializations' space=''] of the needed port.
3. Click the **Synchronize**button. interfacedefCanBusInterface{endporttransmissionPort:CanBusPorts; 
endportelectricalPowerControllerPort:CanBusPorts; 
endportinternalCombustionEnginePort:CanBusPorts; 
}

###### Auto-creating n-ary interfacedefinition elements via the Extract Definition commands

To auto-create n-ary interface definition elements via the Extract Definition commands

- See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Extract Definition and Usage' space='']page.

###### Creating n-ary interface definitions via the view palette

To create n-ary interface definitions via the view palette

1. In the view palette, under the Connectors group, click the**interface def** button. If the needed button is not displayed, click the down-facing arrow next to either the **interface**or the **n-ary interface**button and select the needed button in the dropdown menu.
2. Click in the view pane where you want to create the interface def symbol. The interface def symbol is created containing two end element symbols.
  1. Create additional ends:
    1. Via the Create Element button's end command. See the procedure [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Compartments' space=''] on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Compartments' space=''] page.
    2. In the view palette, under the Items/Ports/Attributes group, click the port button. Click on the interface def symbol to create a port for it.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

/></span></span></span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span  </span></span>interface </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">(</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">transmission</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">internalCombustionEngine</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">fp</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">);<br /></span></span></span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Creating n-ary interfaces via the view palette</h4><p>To create n-ary interfaces via the view palette</p><hr /><ol><li>In the view palette, under the Connectors group, click the <strong>n-ary interface</strong> button. If it is not displayed, click the down-facing arrow next to the <strong>interface </strong>button and click the <strong>n-ary interface </strong>button in the dropdown menu.</li><li>Click in the view pane where you want the n-ary interface symbol to be created.</li><li>Select the n-ary interface symbol and in its smart manipulator, click the <strong>end </strong>command. </li><li>Draw the end from the n-ary interface symbol to the symbol of the port you want to connect. Repeat for all ports you want to connect.</li></ol><h3>Converting binary and n-ary interfaces</h3><h4>Converting binary interface to n-ary via the view</h4><p>To convert binary interface to n-ary via the view</p><hr /><ol><li>In the view pane, right-click the binary interface you want to convert to an n-ary.</li><li>In the shortcut menu, click <strong>Refactor</strong>, then <strong>Convert to N-ary Dot</strong>. The binary interface is converted and the n-ary dot symbol is displayed.</li></ol><h4>Converting <span>n-ary </span><span>interface </span><span>to </span><span>binary </span><span>via the view</span></h4><p>To convert n-ary interface to binary via the view</p><hr /><ol><li>In the view pane, right-click the n-ary dot symbol of the interface you want to convert to a binary.</li><li>In the shortcut menu, click <strong>Refactor</strong>, then <strong>Convert to Path</strong>. The n-ary interface dot symbol is converted and the binary path is displayed.<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0dcc5e6d-b75e-4dc3-9a76-b8575964a7c5"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The <strong>Convert to Path </strong>command is only available if the dependency has no more than two ends. </p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><h3>Creating n-ary interface definition</h3><h4>Creating n-ary interfaces definition via the Textual Editor</h4><p>To create n-ary interfaces definition via the Textual Editor</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare the <em>interface def</em> keyword followed by its name.</li><li>Name the element, then specify the interface definition features in the element body:<ol><li>Specify the interface definition ends by declaring the <em>end</em> keyword, followed by the end name and/or <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specializations" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[specialization]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> of the needed port.</li></ol></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span>Click the <strong>Synchronize</strong></span><span> button. </span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="802b05e2-76b4-4c33-bb2a-fa228b066a7c"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">interface<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span>def </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">CanBusInterface</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">end<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"> <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">port</span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">transmissionPort</span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">CanBusPorts</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   </span>end<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"> <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">port</span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">electricalPowerControllerPort </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">: </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">CanBusPorts</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   </span>end<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"> <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">port</span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">internalCombustionEnginePort</span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">CanBusPorts</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><h4>Auto-creating <span>n-ary interface </span>definition elements via the Extract Definition commands</h4><p><span>To auto-create n-ary interface definition elements via the Extract Definition commands</span></p><hr /><ul><li><span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">See the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Extract Definition and Usage" /></ac:link></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">page.</span></span></li></ul><h4>Creating n-ary interface definitions via the view palette</h4><p>To create n-ary interface definitions via the view palette</p><hr /><ol><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the view palette, under the Connectors group, click the<strong> interface def</strong> button. </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="769c3a3a-7b27-471d-b151-165748c95ab6"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If the needed button is not displayed, click the down-facing arrow next to either the <span><strong>interface </strong>or the <strong>n-ary interface </strong></span>button and select the needed button in the dropdown menu.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>Click in the view pane where you want to create the interface def symbol. The interface def symbol is created containing two <strong>end </strong>element symbols.<ol><li data-uuid="c08d6faa-201e-42d6-af51-9f20bd1e4288">Create additional ends:<ol><li data-uuid="7a64217e-9f55-4afd-8470-2e739d6f2e35">Via the <strong>Create Element </strong>button's <strong>end </strong>command. See the procedure<span> </span><ac:link ac:anchor="Creating elements via compartments"><ri:page ri:content-title="Compartments" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Creating elements via compartments]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><span> </span>on the<span> </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Compartments" /></ac:link><span> </span>page. </li><li data-uuid="39d6b358-66ce-4d79-a18c-5d49d4e43a4f">In the view palette, under the Items/Ports/Attributes group, click the<strong> port</strong> button. Click on the interface def symbol to create a port for it.</li></ol></li></ol></li></ol>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=263094940 space=SYSML2P version=18 -->
## PAGE 00643: Numerical Functions

- page_id: `263094940`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/263094940/Numerical+Functions
- version_number: 18
- version_date: `2025-11-13T10:10:26.171+01:00`
- ancestors: SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > Model analysis > Functions
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The table below lists the functions available in the *NumericalFunctions* namespace of the Standard Libraries.

- 
- ← **VEIKIA NETEISINGAI?**
- 
- 
- 
- 
- 
- 
- ← **VEIKIA NETEISINGAI?**
- 
- 
- ]]>
- 
- =]]>
- 
- 
- 
- 
- 
- 

| Function | Example | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| isZeroabstract function isZero { in ref feature x[1] : NumericalValue} | calc def Example { private import NumericalFunctions::**; in x; isZero(x) }attribute a = 0;attribute result = Example(a);Output>> result = Example(a) true | Returns true if the given value is zero. |
| isUnitabstract function isUnit { in ref feature x[1] : NumericalValue} | calc def Example { private import NumericalFunctions::**; in x; isUnit(x) }attribute a = 10[SI::kg];attribute result = Example(a);Output>> result = Example(a) false NEVEIKIA? OUTPUT TURETU BUTI TRUE | Returns true if the given value represents a unit of measurement. |
| absabstract function abs { in ref feature x[1] : NumericalValue} | calc def Example { private import NumericalFunctions::**; in x; abs(x) }attribute result = Example(-10);Output>> result = Example(-10) 10 | Returns a non-negative magnitude of a number. |
| additionabstract function + { in ref feature x[1] : NumericalValue in ref feature y[0..1] : NumericalValue} | calc def Example { private import NumericalFunctions::**; in x; in y; x + y}attribute result = Example(50, 60);Output>> result = Example(50, 60) 110 | Returns the sum of x and y.Related: |
| subtractionabstract function - { in ref feature x[1] : NumericalValue in ref feature y[0..1] : NumericalValue} | calc def Example { private import NumericalFunctions::**; in x; in y; x - y}attribute result = Example(50, 60);Output>> result = Example(50, 60) -10 | Returns the difference between x and y.Related: |
| multiplicationabstract function * { in ref feature x[1] : NumericalValue in ref feature y[1] : NumericalValue} | calc def Example { private import NumericalFunctions::**; in x; in y; x * y}attribute result = Example(50, 60);Output>> result = Example(50, 60) 3000 | Returns the product of x and y.Related: |
| divisionabstract function / { in ref feature x[1] : NumericalValue in ref feature y[1] : NumericalValue} | calc def Example { private import NumericalFunctions::**; in x; in y; x / y}attribute result = Example(50, 60);Output>> result = Example(50, 60) 0.8333 | Returns the quotient of x divided by y.Related: |
| exponentiationabstract function ** { in ref feature x[1] : NumericalValue in ref feature y[1] : NumericalValue} | calc def Example { private import NumericalFunctions::**; in x; in y; x ** y}attribute result = Example(50, 60);Output>> result = Example(50, 60) 2147483647 | Returns x raised to the power of y (exponentiation).Related: |
| XORabstract function ^ { in ref feature x[1] : NumericalValue in ref feature y[1] : NumericalValue} | calc def Example { private import NumericalFunctions::**; in x; in y; x ^ y}attribute result = Example(50, 60);Output>> result = Example(50, 60) 2147483647NEVEIKIA? EXPECTED RESULT: 00001110 in decimal = 14Currently works same as exponentiation (**) | Returns the bitwise exclusive OR (XOR) of x and y.Related: |
| modulusabstract function % { in ref feature x[1] : NumericalValue in ref feature y[1] : NumericalValue} | calc def Example { private import NumericalFunctions::**; in x; in y; x % y}attribute result = Example(50, 60);Output>> result = Example(50, 60) 50 | Returns the remainder of x divided by y (modulus).Related: |
| less thanabstract function < { in ref feature x[1] : NumericalValue in ref feature y[1] : NumericalValue} | calc def Example { private import NumericalFunctions::**; in x; in y; x < y}attribute result = Example(50, 60);Output>> result = Example(50, 60) true | Returns true if x is less than y.Related: |
| greater thanabstract function > { in ref feature x[1] : NumericalValue in ref feature y[1] : NumericalValue} | calc def Example { private import NumericalFunctions::**; in x; in y; x > y}attribute result = Example(50, 60);Output>> result = Example(50, 60) false | Returns true if x is greater than y.Related:]]> |
| less than or equal toabstract function <= { in ref feature x[1] : NumericalValue in ref feature y[1] : NumericalValue} | calc def Example { private import NumericalFunctions::**; in x; in y; x <= y}attribute result = Example(50, 60);Output>> result = Example(50, 60) true | Returns true if x is less than or equal to y.Related: |
| greater than or equal toabstract function >= { in ref feature x[1] : NumericalValue in ref feature y[1] : NumericalValue} | calc def Example { private import NumericalFunctions::**; in x; in y; x >= y}attribute result = Example(50, 60);Output>> result = Example(50, 60) false | Returns true if x is greater than or equal to y.Related:=]]> |
| maxabstract function max { in ref feature x[1] : NumericalValue in ref feature y[1] : NumericalValue} | calc def Example { private import NumericalFunctions::**; in x; in y; max(x, y)}attribute a = 50;attribute b = 60;attribute result = Example(a, b);Output>> result = Example(a, b) 60 | Returns the greater of x and y.Related: |
| minabstract function min { in ref feature x[1] : NumericalValue in ref feature y[1] : NumericalValue} | calc def Example { private import NumericalFunctions::**; in x; in y; min(x, y)}attribute a = 50;attribute b = 60;attribute result = Example(a, b);Output>> result = Example(a, b) 50 | Returns the lesser of x and y.Related: |
| sumabstract function sum { in ref feature collection[0..*] : ScalarValue} | calc def Example { private import NumericalFunctions::**; in x; sum(x) }attribute result = Example((50, 60)); // collections must pass values in parentheses; Otherwise, the values are recognized as separate argumentsOutput>> result = Example((50, 60))110 | Returns the total of all numeric values in the collection x. If the collection is empty, returns 0. |
| sum0function sum0 { in ref feature collection[0..*] : ScalarValue} | calc def Example { private import NumericalFunctions::**; in x; sum0(x) }attribute result = Example((50, 60)); // collections must pass values in parentheses; Otherwise, the values are recognized as separate argumentsOutput>> result = Example((50, 60))110 | Returns the total of all numeric values in the collection x. If the collection is empty, returns the feature zero instead of 0. |
| productabstract function product { in ref feature collection[0..*] : ScalarValue} | calc def Example { private import NumericalFunctions::**; in x; product(x) }attribute result = Example((50, 60)); // collections must pass values in parentheses; Otherwise, the values are recognized as separate argumentsOutput>> x = Example((50, 60))3000 | Returns the product of all numeric values in the collection x. If the collection is empty, returns 1. |
| product1function product1 { in ref feature collection[0..*] : ScalarValue} | calc def Example { private import NumericalFunctions::**; in x; product1(x) }attribute result = Example((50, 60)); // collections must pass values in parentheses; Otherwise, the values are recognized as separate argumentsOutput>> x = Example((50, 60))3000 | Returns the product of all numeric values in the collection x. If the collection is empty, returns the feature one instead of 1. |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The table below lists the functions available in the <em>NumericalFunctions</em> namespace of the Standard Libraries.</p><ul><li data-uuid="094aa102-17d0-4525-931f-e1ef90072af0"><ac:link ac:anchor="isZero" /></li><li data-uuid="7493fceb-9ddb-4628-9ae9-d755cfe3ac85"><ac:link ac:anchor="isUnit" /> <strong>← </strong><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-red-subtle,#f87168);"><strong>VEIKIA NETEISINGAI?</strong></span></li><li data-uuid="42790175-0ab5-4747-89b3-d85b78d5af7a"><ac:link
````
