<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249577283 space=SYSML2P version=10 -->
## PAGE 00644: Occurrences

- page_id: `249577283`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/249577283/Occurrences
- version_number: 10
- version_date: `2026-07-01T14:56:09.886+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > Model composition > Common elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An occurrence is used to designate something that has an extent in time, which is the occurrence's lifetime. Occurrences can be structural (parts, items) and behavioral (actions).

##### Creating occurrence usage and definition elements

###### Creating occurrence usage and definition elements via the Textual Editor

To create occurrence usage and definition elements via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare one of the following keywords:
  1. *occurrence def*for an occurrence definition.
  2. occurrence for an occurrence usage.
2. Name the element.
3. (Optional) Specify the occurrence features in the element body.
4. Click the Synchronize button. occurrencedefCruiseControlInteraction;// occurrence definition 
occurrencecruiseController;// occurrence usage

###### Creating occurrence usage and definition elements via the Create Element command

To create occurrence usage and definition elements via the Create Element command

1. In the Containment tree, right-click an element, and in the shortcut menu, click **Create Element**.
2. In the Create Element dialog, select one of the following:
  1. occurrence def to create an occurrence definition element.
  2. occurrence to create an occurrence usage element.
3. Name the element.
4. (Optional) Specify the occurrence features via the Textual Editor.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>An occurrence is used to designate something that has an extent in time, which is the occurrence's lifetime. Occurrences can be structural (parts, items) and behavioral (actions). </p><h3>Creating occurrence usage and definition elements</h3><h4>Creating occurrence usage and definition elements via the Textual Editor</h4><p>To create occurrence usage and definition elements via the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Textual Editor</span></p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="ce441e4e-23b2-4f36-8457-67cebb33ad60">In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare one of the following keywords:<ol><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span> </span><em>occurrence def</em><span> </span>for an occurrence definition.</p></li><li><span> </span><em>occurrence </em>for an occurrence usage.</li></ol></li><li data-uuid="9e8df664-5651-4fa6-ac98-b69a3bb0c251">Name the element.</li><li data-uuid="1c84ca50-86f8-4faf-aee4-2ee65b2e8df6">(Optional) Specify the occurrence features in the element body.</li><li data-uuid="aeca3e3c-837b-4837-8644-9903b8776a27">Click the <strong>Synchronize </strong>button.<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="03f18383-07ac-491a-8792-cb0c1f4eda9a"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">occurrence</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">CruiseControlInteraction</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;  </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// occurrence definition</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">occurrence</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">cruiseController</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;              </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// occurrence usage</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><h4>Creating occurrence usage and definition elements via the Create Element command</h4><p>To create occurrence usage and definition elements via the Create Element command</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="9a97aa5d-677b-4ce9-acb6-72cb09f947d2"><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the Containment tree, right-click an element, and in the shortcut menu, click <strong>Create Element</strong>.</p></li><li data-uuid="ff46d490-773b-4065-8fca-e49fb24f952c">In the <strong>Create Element</strong> dialog, select one of the following:<ol><li><strong>occurrence def</strong> to create an occurrence definition element.</li><li><strong>occurrence </strong>to create an occurrence usage element.</li></ol></li><li data-uuid="c0b398c4-4faf-4ec2-9198-b595c2c77daf">Name the element.</li><li data-uuid="33efcafe-3bd8-44bb-9cfd-aa4086735f37">(Optional) Specify the occurrence features via the Textual Editor.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=283247498 space=SYSML2P version=16 -->
## PAGE 00645: Open in Table

- page_id: `283247498`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/283247498/Open+in+Table
- version_number: 16
- version_date: `2026-05-06T21:25:32.444+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > Model composition > Views > Creating views > Tabular views
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **Open in Table** command allows you to view the selected element's content in any tabular view available in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Activating View Creation dialog' space=''] **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Custom View Creation dialog' space='SYSML2PTWRT']**dialog without creating a tabular view in the model. It recursively exposes the selected element's content and automatically [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Filtering elements for views' space='']. The table itself is not a model element, and it is marked as Temporary. Closing the temporary table and opening it anew via the **Open in Table** command opens a new temporary table.

You can perform all the same actions as in regular tables, except for:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Modeling tabular view columns' space='']Manage columns via the **Columns** button
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing tabular view rows' space='']

Any table-specific modifications ([CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying elements in tabular views' space=''], column [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Modeling tabular view columns' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Modeling tabular view columns' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Modeling tabular view columns' space=''], etc.) do not persist.

##### Opening element content in temporary tables

To open element content in temporary tables

1. Right-click an element in the Containment tree or an element symbol in a view.
2. In the element shortcut menu, click the Open in Table command.
3. In the dropdown menu, select the needed table type. The selected element's content is recursively displayed in the selected temporary table.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p style="text-align: left;">The <strong>Open in Table</strong> command allows you to view the selected element's content in any tabular view available in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Activating View Creation dialog" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[active]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="SYSML2PTWRT" ri:content-title="Custom View Creation dialog" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[View Creation]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> </strong>dialog without creating a tabular view in the model. It recursively exposes the selected element's content and automatically <ac:link ac:anchor="Specifying filter conditions"><ri:page ri:content-title="Filtering elements for views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[filters out KerML library elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. The table itself is not a model element, and it is marked as Temporary. Closing the temporary table and opening it anew via the <strong>Open in Table</strong> command opens a new temporary table.</p><p style="text-align: left;">You can perform all the same actions as in regular tables, except for: </p><ul><li style="text-align: left;" data-uuid="40973884-2dfa-436f-a420-6190c6967cb8"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Modeling tabular view columns" ac:anchor="Hiding elements from the table via the Remove From Table command"><ri:page ri:content-title="Managing tabular view rows" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Hide elements from the table via the Remove From Table command]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="35d9bf33-8598-4191-a8bf-c46a0dcfd7f8"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Any table-specific modifications (<ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Displaying elements in tabular views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[the display mode]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, column <ac:link ac:anchor="Modifying table column width"><ri:page ri:content-title="Modeling tabular view columns" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[pinning]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, <ac:link ac:anchor="Sorting tabular rows"><ri:page ri:content-title="Modeling tabular view columns" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[sorting]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, etc.) do not persist. </p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Opening element content in temporary tables</h3><p>To open element content in temporary tables</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="f1368424-afc6-4d48-ba2f-475a37328d77">Right-click an element in the Containment tree or an element symbol in a view.</li><li data-uuid="3e35cbaa-ddb0-4cad-b1f8-15fadfdd41fe">In the element shortcut menu, click the <strong>Open in Table</strong> command.</li><li data-uuid="93ad8413-744d-4f2d-a801-f98cf92ee03b">In the dropdown menu, select the needed table type.<br />The selected element's content is recursively displayed in the selected temporary table. </li></ol><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Open in Tables.png" /></ac:image></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=306282663 space=SYSML2P version=2 -->
## PAGE 00646: Opening used projects

- page_id: `306282663`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/306282663/Opening+used+projects
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-05-08T12:51:19.608+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > Project management > Working with projects > Managing local used projects
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Projects can only use other projects in read-only mode. If you want to edit a used project, you must open it as a stand-alone. You can do this from within the main project in which the project is used by following the procedures listed below.

###### Opening a used project via the Containment tree

To open a used project via the Containment tree

1. In the Containment tree, expand the Used Projects node.
2. Right-click a used project and click Open Latest Version . The used project is opened. [ATTACHMENT filename='Opening a used project via the Containment tree.png']

###### Opening a used project via the Project Usages dialog

To open a used project via the Project Usages dialog

1. Open the **Project Usages**dialog .
2. In the Project Usages dialog, select a used project, then on the right side section of the dialog, click the Open Latest Version button.
3. Click the OK button to close the Project Usages dialog. The used project is opened. [ATTACHMENT filename='Opening a used project via the Project Usages dialog.png']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Projects can only use other projects in read-only mode. If you want to edit a used project, you must open it as a stand-alone. You can do this from within the main project in which the project is used by following the procedures listed below.</p><h4>Opening a used project via the Containment tree</h4><p>To open a used project via the Containment tree</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="156ce0df-3f70-495d-a881-460fe041c520">In the Containment tree, expand the<span> </span><strong>Used Projects</strong><span> </span>node.</li><li data-uuid="7d7570fb-70f2-4fce-9b6c-ca3ef2bcd1b0">Right-click a used project and click <strong>Open Latest Version</strong>.<br />The used project is opened.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Opening a used project via the Containment tree.png" /></ac:image></li></ol><h4>Opening a used project via the Project Usages dialog</h4><p>To open a used project via the Project Usages dialog</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="2fe95945-9d51-417b-b8e3-5a09e832e257"><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Project Usages dialog"><ac:link-body>Open the <strong>Project Usages </strong>dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link>.</li><li data-uuid="a4567379-e0c9-413b-9dd1-b9c067a932a0">In the <strong>Project Usages </strong>dialog, select a used project, then on the right side section of the dialog, click the <strong>Open Latest Version</strong> button.</li><li data-uuid="77d75c32-a4ef-419d-9d11-c6a3717e6692">Click the <strong>OK </strong>button to close the <strong>Project Usages </strong>dialog.<br />The used project is opened.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Opening a used project via the Project Usages dialog.png" /></ac:image></li></ol><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249577794 space=SYSML2P version=3 -->
## PAGE 00647: Opening views

- page_id: `249577794`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/249577794/Opening+views
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-09-19T15:46:47.628+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > Model composition > Views
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

See the procedures on this page for opening views.

##### Opening views

###### Opening views via the Containment tree

Opening vews via the Containment tree

1. Select the view element in the Containment tree and do one of the following:
  1. Double-click the view element.
  2. Right-click the view element and in the shortcut menu, click Open or Open in New Tab .
  3. On the keyboard, press Ctrl+Enter or Enter . **Open**is equivalent to **Ctrl+Enter**. The commands open the selected view in place of the currently active view.**Open in New Tab**is equivalent to **Enter**. The commands open the selected view in a new view tab.

###### Opening views via the Textual Editor

Opening views via the Textual Editor

- In the Textual Editor, right-click the view you want to open and in the shortcut menu, click Open View .

###### Opening views via the view element symbol in a view

Opening views via the view element symbol in a view

1. Select the view element symbol in a view and do one of the following:
  1. Double-click the view element symbol.
  2. Right-click the view element symbol and in the shortcut menu, click Open or Open in New Tab .

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>See the procedures on this page for opening views.</p><h3>Opening views</h3><h4>Opening views via the Containment tree</h4><p>Opening vews via the Containment tree</p><hr /><ol><li>Select the view element in the Containment tree and do one of the following:<ol><li>Double-click the view element.</li><li>Right-click the view element and in the shortcut menu, click <strong>Open</strong> or <strong>Open in New Tab</strong>.</li><li>On the keyboard, press <strong>Ctrl+Enter </strong>or <strong>Enter</strong>. <br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6392ba39-8ce6-4a63-97fc-6dc431efd7dd"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><strong>Open </strong>is equivalent to <strong>Ctrl+Enter</strong>. The commands open the selected view in place of the currently active view. </li><li><strong>Open in New Tab </strong>is equivalent to <strong>Enter</strong>. The commands open the selected view in a new view tab.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol></li></ol><h4>Opening views via the Textual Editor</h4><p>Opening views via the Textual Editor</p><hr /><ul><li data-uuid="2ec863d2-d2d6-4f29-9fdd-ef68c247b40d">In the Textual Editor, right-click the view you want to open and in the shortcut menu, click <strong>Open View</strong>.</li></ul><h4>Opening views via the view element symbol in a view</h4><p>Opening views via the view element symbol in a view</p><hr /><ol><li>Select the view element symbol in a view and do one of the following:<ol><li>Double-click the view element symbol.</li><li>Right-click the view element symbol and in the shortcut menu, click <strong>Open</strong> or <strong>Open in New Tab</strong>.</li></ol></li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249577263 space=SYSML2P version=7 -->
## PAGE 00648: Package

- page_id: `249577263`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/249577263/Package
- version_number: 7
- version_date: `2026-06-18T11:47:27.577+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > Model composition > Common elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Packages are namespaces that contain member elements that may be owned or unowned. It is used as a container for organizing other elements within the model. For more information, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Key concepts' space=''] page.Additionally, a package can import members of another package as unowned members. You can also filter imported elements based on certain conditions.

##### Creating package elements

###### Creating a package element via the Textual Editor

To create a package element via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare the keyword:
  1. *package*.
  2. *library package* .
2. Specify the element name.
3. (Optional) .
4. Click the Synchronize button. library package Policies; // library package elementpackage'Vehicle Features'{ // package element 
privateimportPolicies::**;filter@SysML::Systems::PartUsage;}

###### Creating a package element via the Create Element command

To create a package element via the Create Element command

1. In the Containment tree, right-click a root namespace, and in the shortcut menu, click Create Element .
2. In the Create Element dialog, select one of the following:
  1. package to create a package element.
  2. library package to create a library package element.
3. Name the element.
4. (Optional) .

##### Specifying packages

###### Importing elements to packages

An import relationship allows one package to import memberships from another package. The member elements from imported memberships become (unowned) members of the importing package in addition to being members of the imported package. In particular, this allows members of the imported package to be referenced in textual notations within the scope of the importing package without having to qualify the member names with the name of the imported package.

In the graphical notation, imported packages are displayed with a dashed outline.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

- For more information and procedures, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Importing and filtering elements' space=''] procedure on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Importing and filtering elements' space=''] page.

###### Specifying element import filters

Packages have the capability to filter elements upon import based on certain conditions, usually defined in terms of the metadata provided by annotations of those elements. Only elements that meet all filter conditions become imported members of the packages.

- For more information and procedures, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Importing and filtering elements' space=''] section on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Importing and filtering elements' space=''] page.

###### Package members display in symbolic views

- To display package members in the Members compartment of the package symbol, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Compartments' space=''].
- To control whether the Members compartment displays only owned members or imported ones as well, use the **Show Only Owned** [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Modifying styles of individual symbols' space=''].

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Packages are namespaces that contain member elements that may be owned or unowned. It is used as a container for organizing other elements within the model. <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">For more information, see the <ac:link ac:anchor="Package"><ri:page ri:content-title="Key concepts" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Key concepts]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> page. </span>Additionally, a package can import members of another package as unowned members. You can also filter imported elements based on certain conditions.<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span></span></p><h3 class="auto-cursor-target">Creating package elements</h3><h4>Creating a package element via the Textual Editor</h4><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">To create a package element via the Textual Editor</span></p><hr /><ol><li>In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare the keyword: <ol><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span> </span><em>package</em>.</p></li><li><span> <em>library package</em></span>.</li></ol></li><li>Specify the element name.</li><li>(Optional) <ac:link ac:anchor="Specifying packages"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Specify the package]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li>Click the <strong>Synchronize </strong>button.<ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="eb178161-53d8-4310-9951-d1d380111bdd"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">library package</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">Policies</span>;  <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// library package element</span>  </span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">package<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">'Vehicle Features'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span> </span>{  <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// package element</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   <span> </span></span>private<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span> </span></span>import<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Policies</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">::**; <br /></span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   <span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">filter</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span> </span>@</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">SysML::Systems::PartUsage</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;<span> <br /></span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">} </span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><h4>Creating a package element via the Create Element command</h4><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">To create a package element via the Create Element command</span></p><hr /><ol><li>In the Containment tree, right-click a root namespace, and in the shortcut menu, click <strong>Create Element</strong>.</li><li>In the <strong>Create Element</strong> dialog, select one of the following:<ol><li><strong>package </strong>to create a package element.</li><li><strong>library package </strong>to create a library package element.</li></ol></li><li>Name the element.</li><li>(Optional) <ac:link ac:anchor="Specifying packages"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Specify the package]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li></ol><h3 class="auto-cursor-target">Specifying packages</h3><h4>Importing elements to packages</h4><p><span>An import relationship allows one package to import memberships from another package. The member elements from imported memberships become (unowned) members of the importing package in addition to being members of the imported package. In particular, this allows members of the imported package to be referenced in textual notations within the scope of the importing package without having to qualify the member names with the name of the imported package. </span></p><p><span>In the graphical notation, imported packages are displayed with a dashed outline.</span></p><p><span><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="imported packages.png" /></ac:image></span></p><ul><li><span>For more information and procedures, see the </span><ac:link ac:anchor="Importing elements"><ri:page ri:content-title="Importing and filtering elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Importing elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> procedure on the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Importing and filtering elements" /></ac:link> page.</li></ul><h4>Specifying element import filters </h4><p>Packages have the capability to filter elements upon import based on certain conditions, usually defined in terms of the metadata provided by annotations of those elements. Only elements that meet all filter conditions become imported members of the packages. </p><ul><li><span>For more information and procedures, see the <ac:link ac:anchor="Element import filtering"><ri:page ri:content-title="Importing and filtering elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Element import filtering]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> section on the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Importing and filtering elements" /></ac:link> page.</span></li></ul><h4><span>Package members display in symbolic views</span></h4><ul><li data-uuid="7d57743d-bdc0-4eed-8626-602323185313"><span>To display package members in the Members compartment of the package symbol, <ac:link ac:anchor="Displaying and suppressing compartments"><ri:page ri:content-title="Compartments" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[display the compartment]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></li><li data-uuid="0f7b1a13-9ae9-458c-9368-ceec5c91e2c7"><span>To control whether the Members compartment displays only owned members or imported ones as well, use the <strong>Show Only Owned</strong> <ac:link ac:anchor="The Symbol Style dialog"><ri:page ri:content-title="Modifying styles of individual symbols" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[symbol style]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></li></ul><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249577637 space=SYSML2P version=2 -->
## PAGE 00649: Parallel states

- page_id: `249577637`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/249577637/Parallel+states
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-19T15:43:30.783+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > Model composition > Behavior > States
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A state definition or usage may hierarchically contain state usages in its body. The performance of these substates depends on whether the containing state is parallel or non-parallel:

- In non-parallel states (the default), substate performances occur sequentially, meaning only one substate is active at any given time. Transitions control the activation of these substates.
- In parallel states (triggered by adding the keyword parallel before the body), substates run concurrently, with their performances overlapping in time. No transitions are allowed between concurrent states.

Contained substates may also decompose further, employing transitions in sequential states.

##### Specifying parallel states

###### Specifying parallel states via the Textual Editor

To specify parallel states via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, declare the keyword parallel before the state's body.
2. Click the Synchronize button.

stateonparallel{ // The following substates are performed concurrentlystatespeed{ ...} // Any contained substates are performed sequentially 
stateheat{ ...} // Any contained substates are performed sequentially 
}

###### Specifying parallel states via the element shortcut menu

To specify parallel states via the element shortcut menu

- Right-click a state element in the Containment tree or its symbol in a view and in its shortcut menu, in the Properties group, select the Is Parallel property.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>A state definition or usage may hierarchically contain state usages in its body. The performance of these substates depends on whether the containing state is parallel or non-parallel:</p><ul><li>In <strong>non-parallel states</strong> (the default), substate performances occur sequentially, meaning only one substate is active at any given time. Transitions control the activation of these substates.</li><li>In <strong>parallel states</strong> (triggered by adding the keyword <em>parallel</em> before the body), substates run concurrently, with their performances overlapping in time. No transitions are allowed between concurrent states.</li></ul><p>Contained substates may also decompose further, employing transitions in sequential states.</p><h3>Specifying parallel states</h3><h4>Specifying parallel states via the Textual Editor</h4><p>To specify parallel states via the Textual Editor</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Textual Editor, declare the keyword <em>parallel</em> before the state's body.</li><li>Click the <strong>Synchronize<span> </span></strong>button.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="584d9e6c-3fed-4086-9c45-16ad582d0d71"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">state</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">on</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">parallel</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {       <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// The following substates are performed concurrently<br /></span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">      </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">state</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">speed</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> { ... </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}  <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// Any contained substates are <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">performed sequentially </span></span></span></span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">      </span>state</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">heat</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">{ ... </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}    <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// Any contained substates are <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">performed sequentially </span></span></span></span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Specifying parallel states via the element shortcut menu</h4><p>To specify parallel states via the element <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">shortcut menu</span></p><hr /><ul><li>Right-click a state element in the Containment tree or its symbol in a view and in its shortcut menu, in the<span> </span><strong>Properties</strong><span> </span>group, select the<span> </span><strong>Is Parallel<span> </span></strong>property.</li></ul><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249577414 space=SYSML2P version=4 -->
## PAGE 00650: Part decomposition

- page_id: `249577414`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/249577414/Part+decomposition
- version_number: 4
- version_date: `2026-04-20T13:00:08.239+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > Model composition > Structure > Parts
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Part definitions and part usages can be decomposed allowing you to build elaborate model structures composed of related parts. You can create a parts tree representing a composition of a part from other parts.

##### Decomposing part definitions

You can represent the next level of decomposition of a part definition using the feature typing (*defined by*) relationship.

###### Decomposing part definitions via the Textual Editor

To decompose part definitions via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, identify the part usage that you want to define, nested within a part definition.
2. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Feature typing' space=''] .

partdefVehicle{ 
partengine:Engine; 
parttransmission:Transmission; 
partdriveshaft:Driveshaft; 
partrearAxleAssembly:RearAxleAssembly;// part usage, owned by a part definition, defined by another part definitionpartfuelTank:FuelTank; 
} 
partdefRearAxleAssembly{ 
partdifferential:Differential; 
partrearAxle:Axle; 
partrearWheel1:Wheel; 
partrearWheel2:Wheel; 
}

###### Displaying decompositions of part definitions in a view

To display decompositions of part definitions in a view

- In a view, select the part definition symbol and the part usage symbol it defines, then use the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying elements in symbolic views' space=''] command. The feature typing relationship is displayed.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Decomposing part usages

You can display a parts tree representing a composition of a part from other parts.

###### Decomposing part usages via the Textual Editor

To decompose part usages via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, within the body of the part you want to decompose, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating parts' space=''] .
2. Click the Synchronize button.

partvehicle_b : Vehicle{ // the owning part usage with nested part usages 
partengine:Engine{ 
partcyl:Cylinder; 
} 
parttransmission:Transmission; 
partfuelTank:FuelTank; 
partrearAxleAssembly:AxleAssembly; 
}

###### Decomposing part usages via a view by creating a composite part

To decompose part usages via a view by creating a composite part

1. In a view, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating parts' space=''] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying elements in symbolic views' space=''] the part for which you want to create a composite part.
2. Create a composite part in one of the following ways:
  1. Via the view palette (part symbol option):
    1. In the view palette, under the parts group, click the part button to create a part usage element.
    2. Click on the part symbol displayed in the view pane to create a part owned by another.
  2. Via the view palette (feature membership option):
    1. In the view palette, under the Other Relationships group, click the feature membership button.
    2. First, click on the part symbol displayed in the view pane and then click elsewhere in the view pane to create a part owned by another.
  3. Via the smart manipulator (feature membership option):
    1. Click the part symbol for which you want to create a composite part.
    2. In its smart manipulator, click the feature membership icon , then click elsewhere in the view pane to create a part owned by another.

###### Decomposing part usages via a view by designating an existing part as a composite one

To decompose part usages via a view by designating an existing part as a composite one

1. In a view, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating parts' space=''] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying elements in symbolic views' space=''] the parts you want to designate as the owner and the owned.
2. Designate an existing part as owned by another in one of the following ways:
  1. Via the view palette (feature membership option):
    1. In the view palette, under the Other Relationships group, click the feature membership button.
    2. First, click on the part symbol you want to designate as the owner, then the one you want to designate as the owned one.
  2. Via the smart manipulator (feature membership option):
    1. Click the part symbol you want to designate as the owner.
    2. In its smart manipulator, click the feature membership icon, then click on the part you want to designate as the owned one.

###### Displaying decompositions of part usages in a view

To display decompositions of part usages in a view

- In a view, select the owning part usage symbol and use the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying elements in symbolic views' space=''] command under the Tree-View Style category. The feature membership and the owned features (part usages) are displayed.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Reusing and modifying the parts tree

A part can subset another part, facilitating parts tree reuse and modification. The subsetting part inherits the part decomposition and other features. You can then modify the inherited parts and features through redefinition and subsetting in addition to adding new parts.

###### Reusing and modifying the parts tree via the Textual Editor

To reuse and modify the parts tree via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating parts' space=''] you want to reuse, then [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Subsetting' space=''] it.
2. (Optional) [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Redefinition' space=''] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Subsetting' space=''] the part features in the element body.
3. Click the Synchronize button.

partvehicle_b: Vehicle{// subsetted part 
partengine:Engine{ 
partcyl[4..8] :Cylinder; 
} 
parttransmission:Transmission; 
partfuelTank:FuelTank; 
partrearAxleAssembly:AxleAssembly; 
} 
partvehicle_c:> vehicle_b{ // a part reuses the parts tree by subsetting another part 
parteng:>engine{ 
partcy:>cyl[4]; 
} 
}

###### Reusing the parts tree via the view palette

To reuse the parts tree via the view palette

1. In a view, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating parts' space=''] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying elements in symbolic views' space=''] the part whose decomposition you want to reuse.
2. In the view palette, under the Specializations group, click the subsetting button.
3. Click on the part you want to denote as the subsetting one and then click on the part you want to denote as the subsetted (reused) one.

###### Displaying the parts tree reuse in a view

To display the parts tree reuse in a view

1. In a view, select the subsetting and the subsetted part usage symbols, then u se the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying elements in symbolic views' space=''] command . The subsetting relationship is displayed.
2. (Optional) To display owned features of the subsetting or subsetted parts, use the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying elements in symbolic views' space=''] command. The owned features (part usages) are displayed. [ATTACHMENT filename='Parts tree_reuse_and_modification.png']

facilitating parts tree reuse and modification. The subsetting part inherits the part decomposition and other features. You can then modify the inherited parts and features through redefinition and subsetting in addition to adding new parts.</p><h4>Reusing and modifying the parts tree via the Textual Editor</h4><p>To reuse and modify the parts tree via the Textual Editor</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Textual Editor, <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating parts" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[declare the part usage]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> you want to reuse, then <ac:link ac:anchor="Subsetting a usage via the Textual Editor"><ri:page ri:content-title="Subsetting" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[subset]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> it.</li><li>(Optional) <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Redefinition" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Redefine]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> or <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Subsetting" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[subset]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> the part features in the element body.</li><li>Click the <strong>Synchronize<span> </span></strong>button.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8811e49e-ed80-4671-a9c5-db96a9276d25"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">vehicle_b</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> : <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Vehicle </span>{<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">                                     <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// subsetted part</span></span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">engine</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Cylinder</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">FuelTank</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">rearAxleAssembly</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">AxleAssembly</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">vehicle_c</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">vehicle_b </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">{                               <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// a part reuses the parts tree by subsetting another part</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">eng</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">engine</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Reusing the parts tree via the view palette</h4><p>To reuse the parts tree via the view palette</p><hr /><ol><li>In a view, <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating parts" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[create]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> or <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Displaying elements in symbolic views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[display]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> the part whose decomposition you want to reuse. </li><li>In the view palette, under the Specializations group, click the <strong>subsetting </strong>button.</li><li>Click on the part you want to denote as the subsetting one and then click on the part you want to denote as the subsetted (reused) one.</li></ol><h4>Displaying the parts tree reuse in a view</h4><p>To display the parts tree reuse in a view</p><hr /><ol><li>In a view, select the subsetting and the subsetted part usage symbols, then u<span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">se the </span><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Displaying elements in symbolic views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Display In-Between Relations]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> </strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">command</span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">. The subsetting relationship is displayed.</span></li><li>(Optional) To display owned features of the subsetting or subsetted parts, use the <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Displaying elements in symbolic views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Display Features]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> </strong>command. The owned features (part usages) are displayed.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Parts tree_reuse_and_modification.png" /></ac:image></li></ol>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249577428 space=SYSML2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00651: Part interconnection

- page_id: `249577428`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/249577428/Part+interconnection
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-15T18:53:20.325+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > Model composition > Structure > Parts
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Parts can be connected to define their interactions and relationships within a model. The primary methods for connecting parts are:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Connection' space=''] (i.e., without ports) that specify a higher level of abstraction, likely establishing physical or logical connections.
  - Parts may also be connected using:
    - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Flow' space=''] connections
    - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Binding' space=''] connections

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']Parts connected via a connection.

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Interface' space=''] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Port' space=''] (i.e., with ports) that specify more detailed, formalized interactions.
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Port' space=''] on parts must be compatible with the interface definition typing the interface usage that connects the parts via ports.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']Parts connected through ports via an interface.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Parts can be connected to define their interactions and relationships within a model. The primary methods for connecting parts are: </p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Connection" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Connections]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> (i.e., without ports) that specify a higher level of abstraction, likely establishing physical or logical connections. <ul><li>Parts may also be connected using: <ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Flow" /></ac:link> connections</li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Binding" /></ac:link> connections</li></ul></li></ul></li></ul><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="display_connection.png" /></ac:image>Parts connected via a connection.</h6><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Interface" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Interfaces]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Port" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[ports]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> (i.e., with ports) that specify more detailed, formalized interactions.<ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Port" /></ac:link> on parts must be compatible with the interface definition typing the interface usage that connects the parts via ports.</li></ul></li></ul><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="display_interface.png" /></ac:image>Parts connected through ports via an interface.</h6>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249577558 space=SYSML2P version=15 -->
## PAGE 00653: Perform action

- page_id: `249577558`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/249577558/Perform+action
- version_number: 15
- version_date: `2026-02-17T21:43:44.277+01:00`
- ancestors: SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > Model composition > Behavior > Actions
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A perform action usage specifies that an action is performed by the owner of the performed action usage. A perform action usage is referential, which allows the performed action behavior to be defined in a different context than that of the performer.

A perform action usage can be a feature of:

- a part definition or usage, specifying that the referenced action is performed by the containing part.
- an action definition or usage, whereby the perform action usage represents a referential "call" from the containing action to the performed action.

There are two ways an action can be performed:

50%

- by referencing the action usage defined elsewhere in the model.
  - The element symbol is displayed with the perform keyword (unnamed element) or the perform action keyword (named element). [ATTACHMENT filename='perform_symbol.png'] [ATTACHMENT filename='perform_action_symbol_2.png']
  - See the procedures:
    - :
      - 
      - 
      - 
      - 
      - 
      - 

50%

- by containing an owned perform action usage, i.e., the performed action is the perform action usage itself.
  - The element symbol is displayed with the perform action keyword. [ATTACHMENT filename='perform_action_symbol.png']
  - See the procedures:
    - :
      - 
      - 
      - 

##### Performing an action by referencing an action usage defined elsewhere in the model

###### Performing actions via the Textual Editor1Performing actions via the Textual Editor

To perform actions via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, within the body of the element that you want to perform an action, do one of the following:
  1. For an unnamed perform action, declare the perform keyword followed by the name of the performed action.
  2. For a named one, declare the perform action keyword, specify the element name, then [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Reference subsetting' space=''] the performed action.
2. Click the Synchronize button.

action 'run mission'; // referenced action

part'drone in any mission'{

// unnamed perform action referencing an action defined elsewhere in the modelperform'run mission';

// named perform action referencing an action defined elsewhere in the modelperformaction'run delivery mission' references'run mission';performaction'run delivery mission'::>'run mission';

}

###### Performing actions via the Drag and Drop handler in a view

To perform actions via the Drag and Drop handler in a view

- Drag an action element from the Containment tree or use its symbol's [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Drag and Drop handler' space=''] and drop it on:
  - the target element symbol that you want to perform the action by referencing it using the **Create Perform Action** command.
  - the perform action usage symbol that you want to perform the action by referencing it using the **Set Performed Action** command.

###### Performing actions via the view palette

To perform actions via the view palette

1. In the view palette, under the Actions group, click perform . If the needed button is not displayed, click the down-facing arrow next to the**perform****action**button and select the needed button in the dropdown menu.
2. Do one of the following:
  1. To perform an existing action, in the view pane, click on the part element symbol, then on the action element symbol.
  2. To create a new perform action, in the view pane, click on the part element symbol, then elsewhere in the view pane to create an action element symbol. The perform path is drawn between the element symbols.

###### Performing actions via the smart manipulator in a view

To perform actions via the smart manipulator in a view

- In a view , do one of the following:
  - To perform an existing action, select the part element symbol, and in its smart manipulator, click the perform command. Click on the action element symbol.
  - To perform a new action, select the part element symbol, and in its smart manipulator, click the perform command. Click elsewhere in the view pane to create an action element symbol . The perform path is drawn between the element symbols.

###### Performing actionsvia the Create Relation command

To perform actions via the Create Relation command

1. Right-click on the client (source) element.
2. In the shortcut menu, click Create Relation , then in the submenu, under Outgoing , click perform . The Create... dialog opens.
3. In the Create... dialog, do one of the following:
  1. In a view, click the element symbol you want to be the supplier (target). Hold Shift to select multiple elements.
  2. Click the Add [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. The element selection dialog opens. In the element selection dialog, select the element you want to be the supplier (target) and click OK .
  3. Drag and drop the needed element you want to be the supplier (target) from the Containment tree onto the Create... dialog.
4. In the Create... dialog, click OK .

###### Performing actionsvia the Specification panel

To perform actions via the Specification panel

- Drag and drop an action element from the Containment tree or use its symbol's [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Drag and Drop handler' space=''] and drop it on the **Performed Action** field in the Properties section of the Specification panel for the perform action element. The appropriate action is specified for the element.

##### Performing an action via the owned perform action usageitselfas the performed action

###### Performing actions via the Textual Editor

To perform actions via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, within the body of the element that you want to perform an action, declare the perform action keyword followed by its name.
2. Click the Synchronize button.

part'drone in any mission'{

// performed action is the defined perform action usage itself contained by the parentperformaction'run mission';

}

###### Performing actions via the Create Element command

To perform actions via the Create Element command

1. In the Containment tree, right-click an element, and in the shortcut menu, click **Create Element**.
2. In the Create Element dialog, select the perform action element.
3. Name the element.
4. Specify the perform action's features.

###### Performing actions via the view palette2Performing actions via the view palette

To perform actions via the view palette

1. In the view palette, under the Actions group, click the perform action button.
2. Click in the view pane where you want to create the perform action symbol.
3. Name the element.
4. Specify the perform action's features.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

  </span>perform</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">action</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'run delivery mission'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> ::&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'run mission'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Performing actions via the Drag and Drop handler in a view </h4><p>To perform actions via the Drag and Drop handler in a view</p><hr /><ul><li><span>Drag an action element from the Containment tree or use its symbol's <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Drag and Drop handler" /></ac:link> and drop it on:</span><ul><li><span>the target element symbol that you want to perform the action </span><span>by referencing it </span><span>using the</span><span> <strong>Create Perform Action </strong></span><span>command.</span><span> </span></li><li><span>the perform action usage symbol that you want to perform the action </span><span>by referencing it </span><span>using the</span><span> <strong>Set Performed Action </strong></span><span>command.</span><span> </span></li></ul></li></ul><h4>Performing actions via the view palette</h4><p><span>To perform actions via the view palette</span></p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="56d8f301-940e-436a-9248-6fcfd1eab62b">In the view palette, under the Actions group, click <strong>perform</strong>.<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b02da26b-933f-494b-9d1f-46da8cbdc3b4"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">If the needed button is not displayed, click the down-facing arrow next to the<span> <strong>perform </strong></span></span><strong style="text-align: left;">action<span> </span></strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">button and select the needed button in the dropdown menu.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="13f4a5db-a0e7-46df-8edc-440aa139ecae">Do one of the following:<ol><li><span>To perform an existing action, in the view pane, click on the part element symbol, then on the action element symbol. </span></li><li><span>To create a new perform action, in the view pane, click on the part element symbol, then elsewhere in the view pane to create an action element symbol. <br /></span>The <strong>perform</strong><span> path is drawn between the element symbols.</span></li></ol></li></ol><h4><span>Performing actions via the smart manipulator in a view</span></h4><p><span>To perform actions via the smart manipulator in a view</span></p><hr /><ul><li data-uuid="32ba9d2d-4eca-464e-90d0-7175a47fad59">In a<span> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">view</span>, do one of the following:<ul><li><span>To perform an existing action, </span>select the part element symbol, and in its smart manipulator, click the<strong><span> perform </span></strong>command. Click on the action element symbol.</li><li><span>To perform a new action, </span>select the part element symbol, and in its smart manipulator, click the<strong><span> perform </span></strong>command. Click <span>elsewhere in the view pane to create an action element symbol</span>.<br />The <strong>perform</strong><span> path is drawn between the element symbols.</span></li></ul></li></ul><h4><span>Performing actions </span><span>via the Create Relation command</span></h4><p>To perform actions via the Create Relation command</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="cde67f00-4df1-4301-8293-2445c0ed4a08">Right-click on the client (source) element.</li><li data-uuid="b881b775-dc4e-4170-8563-97289870ce95">In the shortcut menu, click <strong>Create Relation</strong>, then in the submenu, under <strong>Outgoing</strong>, click <strong>perform</strong>. The <strong>Create... </strong>dialog opens.</li><li data-uuid="fc10845c-3568-4ad8-8abb-4d5f8dd2f5de">In the <strong>Create...</strong><strong> </strong>dialog, do one of the following:<ol><li>In a view, click the element symbol you want to be the supplier (target). Hold Shift to select multiple elements.</li><li>Click the <strong>Add <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="add_button.png" /></ac:image> </strong>button. The element selection dialog opens. In the element selection dialog, select the element you want to be the supplier (target) and click <strong>OK</strong>.</li><li>Drag and drop the needed element you want to be the supplier (target) from the Containment tree onto the <strong>Create... </strong>dialog.</li></ol></li><li data-uuid="c8d316de-87de-4190-9ec2-6c3cad8cc6f0">In the <strong>Create...</strong><strong> </strong>dialog, click <strong>OK</strong>. </li></ol><h4>Performing actions<span> via the Specification panel</span></h4><p>To perform actions via the Specification panel</p><hr /><ul><li>Drag and drop an action element from the Containment tree or use its symbol's <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Drag and Drop handler" /></ac:link> and drop it on the<span> <strong>Performed Action</strong></span><strong><span> </span></strong>field in the Properties section of the Specification panel for the perform action element. <span><br /></span>The appropriate action is specified for the element.</li></ul><h3><span style="letter-spacing: -0.006em;">Performing an action via the owned perform action usage </span><span>itself </span><span style="letter-spacing: -0.006em;">as the performed action </span></h3><h4>Performing actions via the Textual Editor</h4><p>To perform actions via the Textual Editor</p><hr /><ol><li class="auto-cursor-target">In the Textual Editor, within the body of the element that you want to perform an action, declare the <em style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">perform action </em><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">keyword followed by its name.</span></li><li>Click the <strong>Synchronize </strong>button.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e8e3dcb4-b540-4205-af85-28339128c8cb"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'drone in any mission'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// performed action is the defined perform action usage itself contained by the parent<br /></span></span></span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span>perform</span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">action</span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'run mission'</span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span></p><p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Performing actions via the Create Element command</h4><p>To perform actions via the Create Element command</p><hr /><ol><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the Containment tree, right-click an element, and in the shortcut menu, click <strong>Create Element</strong>.</p></li><li>In the <strong>Create Element</strong> dialog, select the <strong>perform action </strong>element.</li><li>Name the element.</li><li>Specify the perform action's features.</li></ol><h4 class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="269bd4ec-ac7e-44b3-8055-32f076bc4b0f"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Performing actions via the view palette2</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Performing actions via the view palette</h4><p>To perform actions via the view palette</p><hr /><ol><li>In the view palette, under the Actions group, click the <strong>perform action </strong><span>button.</span></li><li>Click in the view pane where you want to create the perform action symbol.</li><li>Name the element.</li><li><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Specify the perform action's features. </span></li></ol></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249577907 space=SYSML2P version=18 -->
## PAGE 00654: Performing SysML v1 to v2 model transformation

- page_id: `249577907`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/249577907/Performing+SysML+v1+to+v2+model+transformation
- version_number: 18
- version_date: `2026-06-23T13:07:08.690+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > SysML v1 to SysML v2 transition > SysML v1 to v2 model conversion > Transforming the SysML v1 model to v2
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Use the SysML v1 to SysML v2 Migration plugin to enable model transformation features, allowing you to transform SysML v1 models to SysML v2.The migration of SysML v1 models to SysML v2 is done by exporting a SysML v1 project to a local SysML v2 project with the file name '<project_name>_migrated'.

#### NOTE: Prerequisite

Prerequisite

The SysML v1 to v2 Migration plugin, which is available in the Resource Manager via the *<tool_installation_directory>\data\resourcemanager\distribution\core.rdzip* file, must be [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Installation and system requirements' space=''] in the modeling tool.

##### Migrating SysML v1 to v2

To export a SysML v1 project to a local SysML v2 project file

- In the main menu, click File > Export To > SysML v2 Model . Specify the project name and location in the Save window and click Save . The exported project is opened in the modeling tool. Migration statusIf failures occur during the migration, a migration status file *MigrationReportOf<projectName>*.*xlsx* is generated. The link to the file is displayed in the Notification window. The file contains two sheets: 'Not Migrated Elements' and 'Not Migrated Element Properties', listing failed element and property migrations, respectively.

##### SysML v1 to v2 element migration map

#### WARNING: SysML v1 to v2 element migration - a work in progress

SysML v1 to v2 element migration - a work in progress

- Currently, only partial SysML v1 element migration is supported by the modeling tool, as SysML v1 to v2 element migration is a work in progress, covering over 80% of the SysML v1 to SysML v2 Transformation specification.
- Diagram migration currently supports only symbolic diagram to view usage migration. Tables, maps, matrices, etc., will be supported in future releases.

See the table below for elements covered by the migration.

| SysML v1 input | Result in the SysML v2 model |
| --- | --- |
| Block | Part Definition |
| Package | Package |
| Comment | Comment |
| Generalization | Subclassification |
| Interface Block | Interface Definition |
| Proxy Port | Port Usage |
| Port | Port Usage |
| ~Interface Block | Conjugated Port Definition |
| Signal | Item Definition |
| Flow Property | Item Usage |
| Part Property | Part Usage |
| Value Property | Attribute Usage |
| Constraint Property | Assert Constraint Usage / Calculation UsageAssert Constraint Usage if the type in the target model is a Constraint Definition.Calculation Usage if the type in the target model is a Calculation Definition. |
| Reference Property | Attribute Usage |
| Value Type | Attribute Definition |
| Connector | Connection Usage / Interface Usage |
| Connector End | Reference Usage (IsEnd=true) |
| Requirement | Requirement Usage |
| Enumeration | Enumeration Definition |
| Enumeration Literal | Enumeration Usage |
| Dependency | Dependency |
| Use case | Use Case Definition |
| Include | Include Use Case Usage |
| Extend | No alternative match |
| ExtensionPoint | No alternative match |
| Actor | Part Usage owned via Actor Membership |
| Activity | Action Definition |
| Call Behavior Action | Action Usage |
| Structured Activity Node | Action Usage |
| Activity Node | abstract |
| Input Pin | Reference Usage (Direction=in) |
| Output Pin | Reference Usage (Direction=out) |
| Action Input Pin | Reference Usage (Direction=in) |
| Pin | abstract |
| Value Pin | Reference Usage (Direction=in) |
| Fork Node | Fork Node |
| Join Node | Join Node |
| Merge Node | Merge Node |
| Decision Node | Decision Node |
| Parameter | Reference Usage |
| Behavior | abstract |
| Object Flow | Succession Flow Usage / Flow UsageIf the object flow end is an activity parameter node, it is migrated to a binding connector. |
| Object Node | abstract |
| Control Flow | Succession Usage / Transition Usage |
| Activity Edge | abstract |
| Transition | Transition Usage |
| State | State Usage |
| State Machine | State Definition |
| Region | State Usage |
| Trigger | Payload of Accept Action Usage |
| Central Buffer Node | Action Usage |
| Activity Parameter Node | No alternative match |
| Opaque Expression | Calculation Usage |
| Send Signal Action | Send Action Usage |
| Accept Event Action | Accept Action Usage |
| Item/Information Flow | Flow Usage / Flow Definition |
| Binding Connector | Binding Connector As Usage |
| Association | Connection Definition / Interface Definition |
| Class | Occurrence Definition |
| Satisfy | Satisfy Requirement Usage |
| Derive | Connector + Metadata |
| Verify | Requirement Verification Membership |
| Property | Reference UsageIf the property:Has no type: Reference usageTyped by data type: Attribute usageTyped by signal: Item usage Typed by class: Occurrence usageTyped by activity: Action usage Typed by a state machine: State usage Typed by use case: Use case usage Typed by interface: Port usageIf the property is typed by an element that is not in the list, the property is migrated to a reference usage. |
| Refine | Dependency + Refinement MetadataIf refine is between a constraint and a requirement, it is migrated to a require constraint. |
| Initial Node | Start Node |
| Activity Final Node | Terminate Action Usage |
| Pseudo State (kind=initial) | Start Node |
| Activity Partition | Perform Action |
| Constraint Parameter | Attribute Usage with direction 'in' |
| Constraint | Textual Representation |
| Specification | Language and Body |
| Constraint Block | ConstraintDefinition / Calculation DefinitionCalculation Definition if the constraint block does not have an outgoing refine relationship to a requirement.Constraint Definition if the constraint block has an outgoing refine relation to a requirement. |
| Trace | Dependency |
| Note | Note |
| Allocate | Allocation Definition / Allocation Usage / DependencyAllocation Definition if both ends are migrated to "KerML::Classifier".Allocation Usage if both ends in the target model are "KerML::Feature".If both ends are not KerML::Type or they do not match by metatype (e.g., feature and classifier), it creates a Dependency. |
| TimeExpression | Payload feature value |
| TimeEvent | Trigger Invocation Expression (kind=at)If 'isRelative = true' then it is migrated to 'kind=after'. |
| ChangeEvent | Trigger Invocation Expression (kind=when) |
| Variable | Reference UsageIf the variable:Has no type: Reference usageTyped by data type: Attribute usageTyped by signal: Item usage Typed by class: Occurrence usageTyped by activity: Action usage Typed by a state machine: State usage Typed by use case: Use case usage Typed by interface: Port usageIf the variable is typed by an element that is not in the list, it is migrated to a reference usage. |
| Connection Point Reference | State Usage |
| PseudoState 'kind=entry' | State Usage |
| PseudoState 'kind=exit' | State Usage |
| Usage | Dependency |
| Realization | Dependency |
| Substitution | Dependency |
| Opaque Action | Action Usage |
| Element | Abstract |
| Profile | Model Library |
| Metaclass | Annotated Element |
| Opaque Behavior | Action Definition |
| Final State | Done state |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

data-uuid="d149dc00-935f-4e9e-b77c-c74e03673580">Typed by data type: Attribute usage</li><li data-uuid="6d7fbf4c-7c4e-4f25-b6a1-f6594faa5c50">Typed by signal: Item usage </li><li data-uuid="5e3191d1-bdc4-48c2-be8b-9185badb77c8">Typed by class: Occurrence usage</li><li data-uuid="a86b0a2b-e9fe-4363-8305-1186d358037e">Typed by activity: Action usage </li><li data-uuid="39797692-7171-4b3d-8632-0c3ce480aaa9">Typed by a state machine: State usage </li><li data-uuid="f959f837-ac10-4c93-836e-8ea37f9dff39">Typed by use case: Use case usage </li><li data-uuid="9d6a4df0-1a1f-423e-845d-b43e74f80ca8">Typed by interface: Port usage</li></ul><p>If the property is typed by an element that is not in the list, the property is migrated to a reference usage.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>Refine</p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Dependency + Refinement Metadata</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cf64c9a5-2ac4-4b84-9149-b85cfff73cff"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If refine is between a constraint and a requirement, it is migrated to a require constraint.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>Initial Node</p></td><td>Start Node</td></tr><tr><td><p>Activity Final Node</p></td><td>Terminate Action Usage</td></tr><tr><td><p>Pseudo State (kind=initial)</p></td><td>Start Node</td></tr><tr><td><p>Activity Partition</p></td><td>Perform Action</td></tr><tr><td><p>Constraint Parameter</p></td><td>Attribute Usage with direction 'in'</td></tr><tr><td><p>Constraint</p></td><td>Textual Representation</td></tr><tr><td><p>Specification</p></td><td>Language and Body</td></tr><tr><td><p>Constraint Block</p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>ConstraintDefinition / Calculation Definition</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cb8440d5-67bf-4f6e-bebb-4ed3fab8e5e9"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li data-uuid="d32b54f8-aec8-4697-bfcc-596a1d933945">Calculation Definition if the constraint block does not have an outgoing refine relationship to a requirement.</li><li data-uuid="2a782f61-d84c-447c-bb86-3cbbb21dde36">Constraint Definition if the constraint block has an outgoing refine relation to a requirement.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>Trace</p></td><td>Dependency</td></tr><tr><td><p>Note</p></td><td>Note</td></tr><tr><td><p>Allocate</p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Allocation Definition / Allocation Usage / Dependency</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bf6a8279-d585-4db6-bf42-7c5df6a2f1c2"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li data-uuid="ae994562-c6ca-4d07-a7b6-6da9998c645c">Allocation Definition if both ends are migrated to &quot;KerML::Classifier&quot;.</li><li data-uuid="1abd0189-c6e6-4913-af86-5533de8cba31">Allocation Usage if both ends in the target model are &quot;KerML::Feature&quot;.</li><li data-uuid="187c6e5c-e520-4984-b6ac-e2882bdd3068">If both ends are not KerML::Type or they do not match by metatype (e.g., feature and classifier), it creates a Dependency.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>TimeExpression</p></td><td>Payload feature value</td></tr><tr><td><p>TimeEvent</p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Trigger Invocation Expression (kind=at)</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="78239483-7131-4e3e-b9e3-685b7f628ab7"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If 'isRelative = true' then it is migrated to 'kind=after'.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>ChangeEvent</p></td><td>Trigger Invocation Expression (kind=when)</td></tr><tr><td><p>Variable</p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Reference Usage</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="54dc2d33-c702-4f58-83df-7cd307800ecb"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If the variable:</p><ul><li data-uuid="c53f05e4-441d-4392-9b92-c3a1ec60eb8f">Has no type: Reference usage</li><li data-uuid="ad535b19-de10-4a4b-9ec8-720ac5979bbf">Typed by data type: Attribute usage</li><li data-uuid="895ac5e3-80e3-40ed-9b52-3034fdf52e9b">Typed by signal: Item usage </li><li data-uuid="353bbabf-6695-48d5-91e0-4af407b483f7">Typed by class: Occurrence usage</li><li data-uuid="7c184849-43c5-4075-a583-390a9807d9fa">Typed by activity: Action usage </li><li data-uuid="ecca8cc1-57e3-4223-a6ce-6f0fe613fd61">Typed by a state machine: State usage </li><li data-uuid="3ac728a0-f20e-4c19-aa9c-d4b594874120">Typed by use case: Use case usage </li><li data-uuid="45e07bec-389d-444f-a028-1b54d29d5a7d">Typed by interface: Port usage</li></ul><p>If the variable is typed by an element that is not in the list, it is migrated to a reference usage.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection Point Reference</p></td><td>State Usage</td></tr><tr><td><p>PseudoState 'kind=entry'</p></td><td>State Usage</td></tr><tr><td><p>PseudoState 'kind=exit'</p></td><td>State Usage</td></tr><tr><td><p>Usage</p></td><td>Dependency</td></tr><tr><td><p>Realization</p></td><td>Dependency</td></tr><tr><td><p>Substitution</p></td><td>Dependency</td></tr><tr><td><p>Opaque Action</p></td><td>Action Usage</td></tr><tr><td><p>Element</p></td><td>Abstract</td></tr><tr><td><p>Profile</p></td><td>Model Library</td></tr><tr><td><p>Metaclass</p></td><td>Annotated Element</td></tr><tr><td><p>Opaque Behavior</p></td><td>Action Definition</td></tr><tr><td><p>Final State</p></td><td>Done state</td></tr></tbody></table><p><br /></p>
