<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249577451 space=SYSML2P version=7 -->
## PAGE 00655: Port

- page_id: `249577451`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/249577451/Port
- version_number: 7
- version_date: `2025-09-30T14:53:03.420+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > Model composition > Structure
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A port is a connection point through which a part definition makes some of its features available in a limited way. A port definition defines features that can be made available via ports. Two ports are compatible for connection if they have directed features that match with inverse directions, which can be established using port conjugation.

##### Creating port usage and definition elements

###### Creating port usage and definition elements via the Textual Editor

To create port usage and definition elements via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare the keyword:
  1. *port def*for a port definition.
  2. port for a port usage.
2. Specify the element name.
3. (Optional) Specify the port features in the element body.
4. Click the Synchronize button.

portdefOnOffCmdPort{outonOffCmd:OnOffCmd;}portonOffCmdPort:OnOffCmdPort;

###### Creating port usage and definition elements via the Create Element command

To create port usage and definition elements via the Create Element command

1. In the Containment tree, right-click an element, and in the shortcut menu, click **Create Element**.
2. In the Create Element dialog, select one of the following:
  1. port for port usage.
  2. port def for port definition.
3. Name the element.
4. (Optional) Specify the port's features.

###### Creating port usage and definition elements via the view palette

To create port usage and definition elements via the view palette

- To create an interface definition:
  1. In the view palette, under the Items/Ports/Attributes group, click the **port** def button to create an interface definition element.
  2. Click in the view pane where you want to create the port definition symbol.
- To create an interface usage:
  1. In the view palette, under the Items/Ports/Attributes group, click the **port** button to create an interface usage.
  2. In a view, click on an element symbol for which you want to create a port. If the needed button is not displayed, click the down-facing arrow next to either the **port**or the **port****def**button and select the needed button in the dropdown menu.

###### Creating port usage elements via the smart manipulator in a view

To create port usage elements via the smart manipulator in a view

- In a view, select an element symbol, and in its smart manipulator, click the port command. A port is created for the element.

###### Creating port usage elements via the symbol compartment in a view

For more information see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Compartments' space=''] page.

To create port usage elements via the symbol compartment in a view

- Do one of the following:
  - Select an element symbol and click the Create Element button on it. In the dropdown menu, click the port element.
  - Select an element symbol and on the right side of the ports compartment, click the Create port button. The new element is created and displayed in the ports compartment.

###### Creating port usage elements via the Specification panel

To create port usage elements via the Specification panel

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification panel' space=''].
2. Do one of the following:
  1. In the Properties section bar, click the **Create Element** [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. The shortcut menu appears. Click the **port**element in the shortcut menu.
  2. In the **ports**compartment section's bar, click the **Create port**[IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. The element is created in the **ports** compartment.

###### Auto-creating port definition elements via the Extract Definition commands

To auto-create a port definition element via the Extract Definition commands

- See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Extract Definition and Usage' space='']page.

##### Conjugating ports

Every port definition has an implicit conjugate port definition that reverses input and output features. You can easily use the conjugate port definitions by prepending the ~ symbol to the port definition name.

###### Using conjugated ports definitions via the Textual Editor

To use conjugate port definitions via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want the element to be created and declare the port keyword.
2. (Optional) Follow with the port name.
3. Follow with the feature typing relationship.
4. Specify the conjugated port definition:
  1. Full conjugation notation:
    1. Port definition name followed by :: (qualified name notation), followed by the port definition name with the ~ symbol prepended.
  2. Shorthand notation:
    1. Port definition name with the ~ symbol prepended.
5. Click the Synchronize button.

portdefFuelPort{ // Port definition 
attributetemperature:Temp; 
outitemfuelSupply:Fuel; 
initemfuelReturn:Fuel; 
} 
partdefFuelTankAssembly{ 
portfuelTankPort:FuelPort; 
} 
partdefEngine{ 
 port engineFuelPort : FuelPort::'~FuelPort'; //Conjugate port definition 
portengineFuelPort: ~FuelPort; // Using a ~ symbol on the port type is a shorthand for using the conjugate port definition 
}

##### Directed port features

A feature with a direction (in, out or inout) is a directed feature. Two ports are compatible for connection if they have directed features that match with inverse directions.

###### Creating directed port features

To create directed port features

1. In the Textual Editor, prepend the direction keyword - in , out , inout - to the feature.
2. Click the Synchronize button. portdefFuelOutPort{ 
attributetemperature:Temp; 
outitemfuelSupply:Fuel;// feature fuelSupply with direction out 
initemfuelReturn:Fuel;// feature fuelReturn with compatible direction in 
} 
portdefFuelInPort{ 
attributetemperature:Temp; 
initemfuelSupply:Fuel;// feature fuelSupply with compatible direction in 
outitemfuelReturn:Fuel;// feature fuelReturn with compatible direction out 
}

style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// Using a ~ symbol on the port type is a shorthand for using the conjugate port definition</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Directed port features</h3><p>A feature with a direction (in, out or inout) is a directed feature. Two ports are compatible for connection if they have directed features that match with inverse directions.</p><h4>Creating directed port features</h4><p>To create directed port features</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Textual Editor, prepend the direction keyword - <em>in</em>, <em>out</em>, <em>inout</em> - to the feature.</li><li>Click the <strong>Synchronize </strong>button.<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="77ae2334-7366-4b0a-877c-e7480662bac9"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">port</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">FuelOutPort</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=314180966 space=SYSML2P version=8 -->
## PAGE 00656: Post-processing the SysML v2 model

- page_id: `314180966`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/314180966/Post-processing+the+SysML+v2+model
- version_number: 8
- version_date: `2026-06-25T11:26:50.144+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > SysML v1 to SysML v2 transition > SysML v1 to v2 model conversion
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**[IMAGE alt='' src='']**

##### Overview

This activity diagram describes activities performed after the initial transformation has completed.

The objective of post-processing is to adapt the generated SysML v2 model to organizational modeling practices and fully exploit SysML v2 capabilities.

##### Process

The process starts with evaluating the generated SysML v2 model.

Systems engineers then perform a series of manual improvement activities:

- Reorganize the model structure.
- Refactor model elements.
- Apply SysML v2 modeling practices.
- Ensure compliance with organizational modeling methods.

Examples of post-processing activities include introducing usage-focused modeling patterns, restructuring model ownership hierarchies, and aligning the model with organizational conventions.

The process is iterative. If the model does not satisfy organizational requirements, additional improvements are applied until compliance is achieved.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><strong><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Post-processing the SysML v2 model.jpg" /></ac:image></strong></p><h3>Overview</h3><p>This activity diagram describes activities performed after the initial transformation has completed.</p><p>The objective of post-processing is to adapt the generated SysML v2 model to organizational modeling practices and fully exploit SysML v2 capabilities.</p><h3>Process </h3><p>The process starts with evaluating the generated SysML v2 model.</p><p>Systems engineers then perform a series of manual improvement activities:</p><ul><li data-uuid="ed4d1b7e-de11-4488-8c7e-401a41e3c17a">Reorganize the model structure.</li><li data-uuid="3dad46b9-9f3d-449f-b048-f1ff5ced5566">Refactor model elements.</li><li data-uuid="db4b75eb-bf7d-4237-a8f5-1694cad506eb">Apply SysML v2 modeling practices.</li><li data-uuid="f3b90b48-afcb-4341-b6d6-70e5801307e9">Ensure compliance with organizational modeling methods.</li></ul><p>Examples of post-processing activities include introducing usage-focused modeling patterns, restructuring model ownership hierarchies, and aligning the model with organizational conventions.</p><p>The process is iterative. If the model does not satisfy organizational requirements, additional improvements are applied until compliance is achieved.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=314180960 space=SYSML2P version=8 -->
## PAGE 00657: Pre-processing the SysML v1 model

- page_id: `314180960`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/314180960/Pre-processing+the+SysML+v1+model
- version_number: 8
- version_date: `2026-06-25T11:25:00.089+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > SysML v1 to SysML v2 transition > SysML v1 to v2 model conversion
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**[IMAGE alt='' src='']**

##### Overview

This activity diagram describes the activities required to prepare a SysML v1 model before transformation.

The objective of this phase is to improve model quality, reduce migration risks, and ensure that the model is suitable for conversion to SysML v2.

##### Process

The preprocessing workflow starts by understanding the capabilities and limitations of the selected transformation tooling.

Depending on the migration strategy, the process follows one of two paths:

- One-off Migration

The source model is prepared and migrated once. Future development continues in the SysML v2 model.

- Incremental Migration

The source model continues to evolve. Projects may be partitioned into smaller subprojects and migrated incrementally. Subsequent migrations evaluate model changes and introduce additional subprojects when necessary.

##### Model Preparation Activities

The following activities are performed before transformation:

- Remove obsolete and no longer maintained model content.
- Apply organizational modeling conventions.
- Review element naming.
- Execute SysML v1 conformance checks.
- Minimize ambiguities and redundancies.
- Identify tool-specific extensions.
- Remove unsupported tool-specific extensions.
- Retain required stereotypes and profiles.

Particular attention should be given to element naming. While unnamed elements are common in SysML v1 models, SysML v2 textual notation relies heavily on explicitly named elements. Therefore, reviewing and improving naming consistency is considered a critical preparation activity.

Profiles and stereotypes intended for migration should be redesigned as SysML v2 libraries and metadata definitions and made available within the target SysML v2 environment.

The preprocessing phase concludes by identifying unsupported features and ensuring that appropriate handling strategies are defined before transformation begins.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><strong><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Pre-processing the SysML v1 model.jpg" /></ac:image></strong></p><h3>Overview</h3><p>This activity diagram describes the activities required to prepare a SysML v1 model before transformation.</p><p>The objective of this phase is to improve model quality, reduce migration risks, and ensure that the model is suitable for conversion to SysML v2.</p><h3>Process </h3><p>The preprocessing workflow starts by understanding the capabilities and limitations of the selected transformation tooling.</p><p>Depending on the migration strategy, the process follows one of two paths:</p><ul><li data-uuid="5d208b39-6be7-4c4e-908f-71cf0849b2fe">One-off Migration</li></ul><p>The source model is prepared and migrated once. Future development continues in the SysML v2 model.</p><ul><li data-uuid="9250e489-f8a3-4ead-99a3-01b536d255ed">Incremental Migration</li></ul><p>The source model continues to evolve. Projects may be partitioned into smaller subprojects and migrated incrementally. Subsequent migrations evaluate model changes and introduce additional subprojects when necessary.</p><h3>Model Preparation Activities</h3><p>The following activities are performed before transformation:</p><ul><li data-uuid="d5b7dda6-1c8a-4b8b-bb95-ae16f3c5a46a">Remove obsolete and no longer maintained model content.</li><li data-uuid="0b887959-be84-4247-ad98-030ad07dafd1">Apply organizational modeling conventions.</li><li data-uuid="08a9495d-a265-431c-aed3-570e028966e6">Review element naming.</li><li data-uuid="3e4ec9a1-5f53-4233-acd4-d04f6ef1f8da">Execute SysML v1 conformance checks.</li><li data-uuid="566dcc07-ca72-43c1-885e-dfe7e55d78e1">Minimize ambiguities and redundancies.</li><li data-uuid="fcd623c4-4b42-4c0d-90e1-7659b30f7f7f">Identify tool-specific extensions.</li><li data-uuid="12e0dc3c-efd3-4382-9413-4dc71ab3986e">Remove unsupported tool-specific extensions.</li><li data-uuid="e5b23b67-679e-4ac1-8d5b-31c90c386ded">Retain required stereotypes and profiles.</li></ul><p>Particular attention should be given to element naming. While unnamed elements are common in SysML v1 models, SysML v2 textual notation relies heavily on explicitly named elements. Therefore, reviewing and improving naming consistency is considered a critical preparation activity.</p><p>Profiles and stereotypes intended for migration should be redesigned as SysML v2 libraries and metadata definitions and made available within the target SysML v2 environment.</p><p>The preprocessing phase concludes by identifying unsupported features and ensuring that appropriate handling strategies are defined before transformation begins.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249577845 space=SYSML2P version=13 -->
## PAGE 00658: Predefined style sheets

- page_id: `249577845`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/249577845/Predefined+style+sheets
- version_number: 13
- version_date: `2026-04-01T12:17:57.513+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > Model composition > Views > Symbol styles
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Each SysML v2 project is automatically loaded with the *3DS**SysML Customization* library containing predefined style sheets. Each style sheet contains symbol style rules based on the element metaclasses.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Symbol styles represented by the *DefaultStyleSheet*(left)**and *BlackAndWhiteStyle*Sheet**(right) style sheets.

There are two predefined style sheets in the *DS_Styles::StyleSheets*package:

50%

- DefaultStyleSheet The DefaultStyleSheet style sheet contains rule-based styles that apply to all element symbols in SysML v2 projects by default based on the element metaclass.
  - You can view the predefined style definitions used for symbol styles in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Style sheet styles tables' space=''] and the *DS_Styles::StyleSheets::ColorDefinitions*package. [ATTACHMENT filename='defaultstylesheetpartdefinition.png']

50%

- BlackAndWhiteStyleSheet The BlackAndWhiteStyleSheet style sheet contains rule-based styles for representing all element symbols in a black-and-white style. [ATTACHMENT filename='blackandwhitestylesheetpartdef.png']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=286557638 space=SYSML2P version=13 -->
## PAGE 00659: Predefined validation suites

- page_id: `286557638`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/286557638/Predefined+validation+suites
- version_number: 13
- version_date: `2026-06-26T14:07:10.925+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > Model analysis > Validation
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The validation rules are bundled into sets called validation suites. The modeling tool contains the following predefined validation suites, each with its own set of validation rules:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML Constraints Suite' space='']. The suite contains SysML v2 and KerML validation rules.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Dassault Systemes Validation Suite' space='']. The suite contains custom Dassault Systèmes validation rules, supplementing the SysML v2 and KerML validation rules.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Dassault Systemes Composition Integrity Validation Suite' space='']. The suite contains custom Dassault Systèmes validation rules, supplementing the SysML v2 and KerML validation rules.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

- The model is only validated against enabled validation suites. See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Enabling validation suites' space=''] page.
- In addition to using the predefined validation suites, you can model your own validation suites with custom validation rules. See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Custom validation suites and rules' space=''] page.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The validation rules are bundled into sets called validation suites. The modeling tool contains the following predefined validation suites, each with its own set of validation rules:</span></p><ul><li data-uuid="553d01c4-dc35-4394-aa93-655d87a0cb56"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SysML Constraints Suite" /></ac:link>. The suite contains SysML v2 and KerML validation rules.</span></li><li data-uuid="3b93ddf3-28a4-40d1-9877-614ecd7672f5"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Dassault Systemes Validation Suite" /></ac:link>. The suite contains custom Dassault Systèmes validation rules, supplementing the SysML v2 and KerML validation rules.</span></li><li data-uuid="6fbced23-e4b4-4faf-ab55-e058e7246e4e"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Dassault Systemes Composition Integrity Validation Suite" /></ac:link>. </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The suite contains custom Dassault Systèmes validation rules, supplementing the SysML v2 and KerML validation rules.</span></li></ul><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Predefined_validation_suites.png" /></ac:image></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e726f7a1-1bf8-4b53-8226-9a87b2f67ad8"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li data-uuid="322815fa-625f-44c1-8195-47da36cb9c05"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The model is only validated against enabled validation suites. See the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Enabling validation suites" /></ac:link> page.</span></span></li><li data-uuid="9ba23f46-1453-4574-8411-29fd0175aceb"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">In addition to using the predefined validation suites, you can model your own validation suites with custom validation rules. ````
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=314180954 space=SYSML2P version=9 -->
## PAGE 00660: Preparing the organization for transition

- page_id: `314180954`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/314180954/Preparing+the+organization+for+transition
- version_number: 9
- version_date: `2026-06-25T11:23:35.347+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > SysML v1 to SysML v2 transition > SysML v1 to v2 model conversion
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**[IMAGE alt='' src='']**

##### Overview

This activity diagram describes the activities required to prepare an organization for the adoption of SysML v2.

The process involves multiple stakeholder groups including systems engineers, IT engineers, software developers, and an interdisciplinary cross-functional transition team.

##### Process

The preparation phase begins with establishing a transition team responsible for defining the overall migration strategy and transition plans. These plans typically address migration goals, schedules, budgets, pilot projects, training activities, and methodology evolution.

Pilot projects are then conducted to assess the impact of SysML v2 on existing MBSE practices. Typical pilot objectives include:

- Increasing organizational awareness of SysML v2.
- Building initial SysML v2 expertise.
- Evaluating candidate SysML v1 models for migration.
- Assessing available SysML v2 tooling.
- Establishing migration approaches and practices.
- Evaluating integrations, plugins, and reporting solutions.
- Defining metrics for tracking migration progress.

Systems engineers evaluate and select SysML v2 modeling tools, while IT engineers deploy and configure the required modeling infrastructure and model repositories.

In parallel, software developers adapt existing tool customizations and integrations to operate in a SysML v2 environment. This frequently includes updates to:

- Plugins.
- Reports.
- Scripts.
- External integrations.

After the infrastructure is established, systems engineers update organizational methodologies, training materials, community resources, reference architectures, and reusable assets to support SysML v2.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><strong><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Preparing the organization for transition.jpg" /></ac:image></strong></p><h3>Overview</h3><p>This activity diagram describes the activities required to prepare an organization for the adoption of SysML v2.</p><p>The process involves multiple stakeholder groups including systems engineers, IT engineers, software developers, and an interdisciplinary cross-functional transition team.</p><h3>Process</h3><p>The preparation phase begins with establishing a transition team responsible for defining the overall migration strategy and transition plans. These plans typically address migration goals, schedules, budgets, pilot projects, training activities, and methodology evolution.</p><p>Pilot projects are then conducted to assess the impact of SysML v2 on existing MBSE practices. Typical pilot objectives include:</p><ul><li data-uuid="88a33fd9-2c01-4fe3-9771-d9d224246a18">Increasing organizational awareness of SysML v2.</li><li data-uuid="5688ac4c-dcbc-4792-b9a3-f8c9412a2c5c">Building initial SysML v2 expertise.</li><li data-uuid="93c3bc14-b48e-4b51-a5f3-de1b69bb32cd">Evaluating candidate SysML v1 models for migration.</li><li data-uuid="3a0f4c30-3385-4fd0-9f94-c69a324901c8">Assessing available SysML v2 tooling.</li><li data-uuid="f161ca3f-5165-486f-a9de-fbebc81bca89">Establishing migration approaches and practices.</li><li data-uuid="52146c88-7ebf-439f-98a1-2c56cfbef44f">Evaluating integrations, plugins, and reporting solutions.</li><li data-uuid="7375bca3-bdb9-4a62-a846-88411777b682">Defining metrics for tracking migration progress.</li></ul><p>Systems engineers evaluate and select SysML v2 modeling tools, while IT engineers deploy and configure the required modeling infrastructure and model repositories.</p><p>In parallel, software developers adapt existing tool customizations and integrations to operate in a SysML v2 environment. This frequently includes updates to:</p><ul><li data-uuid="bafc7d52-8d9f-4444-8fe7-89a1eaa866fc">Plugins.</li><li data-uuid="6c72ab79-21ad-4540-8765-f9c982f60d9a">Reports.</li><li data-uuid="166be5fb-fc3d-49a5-9764-a9a3dc9c142f">Scripts.</li><li data-uuid="9de9e6d3-35b5-4312-9ff7-f8ff19811764">External integrations.</li></ul><p>After the infrastructure is established, systems engineers update organizational methodologies, training materials, community resources, reference architectures, and reusable assets to support SysML v2.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249576720 space=SYSML2P version=10 -->
## PAGE 00661: Prerequisites

- page_id: `249576720`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/249576720/Prerequisites
- version_number: 10
- version_date: `2026-06-25T11:46:31.118+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > Getting started
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To work with all of the SysML v2 features, the following software should be [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Installation and system requirements' space='']:

- One of the following CATIA Magic/No Magic modeling tools:
  - Cameo Systems Modeler (Enterprise Edition only)
  - Cameo Enterprise Architecture
  - Magic Cyber Systems Engineer
  - Magic Systems of Systems Architect
- SysML v1 Plugin and Cameo Requirements Modeler. The plugins must be installed in addition to the following plugins to use SysML v2 in the modeling tool.
- SysML v2 Plugin. It provides the modeling tool with the majority of the SysML v2 features. It also includes the SysML v2 Textual Editor, allowing you to work with the SysML v2 features using textual notation via a textual editor.
- (Optional) [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML v2 Evaluation Plugin Documentation' space='SYSML2EP']. It allows you to conduct static SysML v2 model evaluations (mathematical calculations, calculation usage evaluation, constraints evaluation, and requirements evaluation).
- (Optional) [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Performing SysML v1 to v2 model transformation' space='']. It enables model transformation from SysML v1 to SysML v2 features.
- (Optional) SysML v2 Simulation Plugin . It enables execution of SysML v2 models (state machine simulations, mathematical calculations, and requirements verification).

For the SysML v2 Open API documentation, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML v2' space='DEVG'] page in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Developer Guides Home' space='DEVG']documentation.

#### NOTE: Font Compatibility on macOS

Font Compatibility on macOS

The default font used in the modeling tool for SysML v2 is Roboto. However, because Roboto is not pre-installed on macOS, certain non-Latin characters may not render correctly when using the tool on macOS. To avoid character rendering issues, please [download](https://fonts.google.com/specimen/Roboto) and [install](https://support.apple.com/guide/font-book/install-and-validate-fonts-fntbk1000/mac) the Roboto font on your machine.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249577148 space=SYSML2P version=3 -->
## PAGE 00662: Printing

- page_id: `249577148`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/249577148/Printing
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-12-17T15:00:31.102+01:00`
- ancestors: SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > Model management > Working with views > View image export
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can print:

- Active diagram
- Any diagram(s)
- All open diagrams
- Selected symbols of active diagram

You can specify the printing options or preview pages by using the following dialogs:

- Print Dialog
- Print Options Dialog
- Print Preview Window

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']Print dialog

##### Printing

###### Printing an active diagram

To print an active diagram

1. Open a diagram you want to print.
2. Do one of the following:
  - In the main toolbar, click the [ATTACHMENT filename='print.png'] Print Active Diagram button.
  - In the main menu, click File > Print or press Ctrl+P on the keyboard . On the Print dialog, Print what area, select Active diagram , and click the Print button.

###### Printing any diagram(s)

To print any diagram(s)

1. In the main menu, click File > Print or press Ctrl+P on the keyboard . The Print dialog opens.
2. In the Print what area, choose Selected diagram(s) . The Select Diagram dialog opens.
3. Select the diagram(s) you want to print.
4. Click OK .
5. Click the Print button.

###### Printing all open diagrams

To print all open diagrams

1. In the main menu, click File > Print or press Ctrl+P on the keyboard . The Print dialog opens.
2. In the Print what area, select All opened diagram(s) .
3. Click the Print button.

###### Printing selected symbols of active diagram

To print selected symbols of active diagram

1. On the diagram, select the symbols you want to print.
2. In the main menu, click File > Print or press Ctrl+P on the keyboard. The Print dialog opens.
3. In the Print dialog, Print what area, choose Selected symbols of active diagram
4. Click the Print button.

##### Print preview

You can preview diagrams before printing them using the **Print Preview**window.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']Print Preview window

###### Previewing diagrams before printing

To preview diagrams before printing

1. Open the Print Preview window in one of the following ways:
  1. In the main toolbar, click the [ATTACHMENT filename='print_preview_button.png'] Print Preview button.
  2. In the main menu, click File > Print Preview .
2. Select the diagram you want to preview from the list on the left side of the window. The diagram is previewed on the right side of the window.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

The diagram is previewed on the right side of the window. </li></ol><p><br /></p>

## PAGE 00664: Project options

- page_id: `249576791`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/249576791/Project+options
- version_number: 9
- version_date: `2026-05-06T12:22:14.564+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > Project management > Working with projects
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Use the**Project Options**dialog to specify general project-specific options.The**Project Options**dialog includes option groups, each designated for specific features and containing lists of corresponding options. Groups are represented using the tree structure.

##### Opening the Project Options dialog

To open the Project Options dialog

- In the main menu, click Options > Project . [ATTACHMENT filename='project_options_dialog.png']

##### Modifying project options

To modify project options

1. Open the **Project Options** dialog .
2. Locate the option you want to modify. Use the quick filter to find the needed option in the list. Filtering is performed in all available options, not only the selected option group. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']Click the **Show Description** button to display the project option description section at the bottom of the dialog to read about the option functionality. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. An option value can be changed by typing a new value, setting a value to true / false , or selecting a value from the list. Click the **Reset to Defaults** button to restore default option values.
4. Click OK .

##### Project Options

The table below displays the default project options. Additional options may appear based on installed plugins.

| Option | Default value | Value options | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| General |  |  |  |
| Diagrams |  |  |  |
| Image Export |  |  |  |
| Time Limit to Build Diagram | 60 | Number | Specify the maximum time (in seconds) allotted for diagram building while exporting a diagram. If the time limit is exceeded, the diagram is not exported. Set to 0 to export the diagram without the time limit. >]]> |
| Image Height Limit | 10000 | Number | Specify the maximum image height (in pixels) for diagram export. If the height limit is exceeded, the exported diagram is cropped, or the diagram is not exported at all, depending on the selected preference. Set to 0 to export the diagram without the image height limit. >]]> |
| Image Width Limit | 10000 | Number | Specify the maximum image width (in pixels) for diagram export. If the width limit is exceeded, the exported diagram is cropped, or the diagram is not exported at all, depending on the selected preference. Set to 0 to export the diagram without the image width limit. >]]> |
| Export Preference When Limits Exceeded | Export Cropped Diagram | Export Cropped DiagramDo Not Export | Specify the diagram export preference if the diagram image height and/or width limits are exceeded. >]]> |
| General |  |  |  |
| General |  |  |  |
| Path to Used Project | <project.dir><install.root>\profiles<install.root>\modelLibraries | Custom | Specify the paths to store used projects and extension elements. |
| Decimal Places | 4 | Number | Specify the maximum number of decimal places to be used when displaying the real number in tables and element Specification windows. |
| SysML v2 |  |  |  |
| SysML v2 |  |  |  |
| Active Relationship Creation Dialog | SysMLRelationshipCreationDialog | Custom | Specify the Active Create Relationship Dialog configuration. >]]> |
| Active Element Creation Dialog | SysMLElementCreationDialog | Custom | Specify the Active Create Element Dialog configuration. >]]> |
| Active View Creation Dialog | SysMLViewCreationDialog | Custom | Specify the Active Create View Dialog configuration. >]]> |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249576812 space=SYSML2P version=3 -->
## PAGE 00665: Project Statistics

- page_id: `249576812`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/249576812/Project+Statistics
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-09-19T15:12:25.935+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > Project management > Working with projects > Tools
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The project statistics feature calculates detailed statistics for the entire project and exports the results to a separate file. The exported statistical report will help solve performance problems you may be experiencing. Project statistics are calculated in the main project and, if selected, in used projects. The generated detailed project statistics file includes quantitative data about diagrams, elements, Project/Environment options, hardware & system, Teamwork Cloud projects, and performance.

#### NOTE: Confidential Information

Confidential Information

The exported file contains the names of the main project, used projects (if selected), and used elements. If they are confidential, you should select **Confidential**to keep their names hidden.

##### [IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Exporting detailed statistics

To export detailed statistics

1. In the main menu, select Tools > Project Statistics .
2. In the Project Statistics dialog, click the Export Detailed Statistics button. The Export Detailed Statistics Options dialog opens.
3. Select data to export along with the OS and hardware, diagrams/elements by types, and project options statistics that are included by default.
4. Specify other export options:
  - Select Include Used Project Statistics to generate and export used project statistics as well.
  - Select Confidential to keep the project element names hidden in the exported statistics report.
5. Click Export . The Select Location dialog opens.
6. Select the location where the file should be exported and click Save .
7. When the statistics file is generated, click Close .

##### The project statistics file

The generated detailed project statistics file includes the following quantitative data:

**Teamwork Cloud projects**

- Teamwork Cloud project local cache size on disk
- Teamwork Cloud project local cache latest blob size
- Teamwork Cloud project local cache blob size by revision
- Teamwork Cloud project local cache blob count by revision
- Teamwork Cloud project history depth
- Teamwork Cloud local project binary cache size
- Teamwork Cloud local project cache dir size
- Root object count by type
- Used projects count

**OS & Hardware**

- Hardware (RAM, OS, disk size and type) and Java info (heap)
- Restricted dir size

**Diagrams**

- Total size of diagrams binaries
- Diagram count by type
- Diagram count by kind

**Elements**

- Total element count
- Total size of strings
- Elements by meta-type
- Features by size
- String features by string length
- KerML based Elements statistics
  - Non-Standard Library Elements
  - Standard Library Elements

**Metadata**

- Diagram statistics
- Element statistics

**Standard Libraries**

- Diagram statistics
- Element statistics

**Symbol Styles**

- Diagram statistics
- Element statistics

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">The project statistics feature calculates detailed statistics for the entire project and exports the results to a separate file. The exported statistical report will help solve performance problems you may be experiencing. Project statistics are calculated in the main project and, if selected, in used projects. The generated detailed project statistics file includes quantitative data about diagrams, elements, Project/Environment options, hardware &amp; system, Teamwork Cloud projects, and performance.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="811f00dd-6f47-4a6a-99ee-daa68a80650c"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Confidential Information</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The exported file contains the names of the main project, used projects (if selected), and used elements. If they are confidential, you should select <strong>Confidential </strong>to keep their names hidden.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="project_statistics_dialog.png" /></ac:image></h3><h3>Exporting detailed statistics</h3><p>To export detailed statistics</p><hr /><ol><li>In the main menu, select <strong>Tools </strong>&gt; <strong>Project Statistics</strong>.</li><li>In the <strong>Project Statistics</strong> dialog, click the <strong>Export Detailed Statistics</strong> button. The <strong>Export Detailed Statistics Options </strong>dialog opens.</li><li>Select data to export along with the OS and hardware, diagrams/elements by types, and project options statistics that are included by default.</li><li>Specify other export options:<ul><li>Select <strong>Include Used Project Statistics</strong> to generate and export used project statistics as well.</li><li>Select <strong>Confidential </strong>to keep the project element names hidden in the exported statistics report. </li></ul></li><li>Click <strong>Export</strong>. The <strong>Select Location </strong>dialog opens.</li><li>Select the location where the file should be exported and click <strong>Save</strong>.</li><li>When the statistics file is generated, click <strong>Close</strong>.</li></ol><h3>The project statistics file</h3><p>The generated detailed project statistics file includes the following quantitative data:</p><p><strong>Teamwork Cloud projects</strong></p><ul><li>Teamwork Cloud project local cache size on disk</li><li>Teamwork Cloud project local cache latest blob size</li><li>Teamwork Cloud project local cache blob size by revision</li><li>Teamwork Cloud project local cache blob count by revision</li><li>Teamwork Cloud project history depth</li><li>Teamwork Cloud local project binary cache size</li><li>Teamwork Cloud local project cache dir size</li><li>Root object count by type</li><li>Used projects count</li></ul><p><strong>OS &amp; Hardware </strong></p><ul><li>Hardware (RAM, OS, disk size and type) and Java info (heap)</li><li>Restricted dir size</li></ul><p><strong>Diagrams</strong></p><ul><li>Total size of diagrams binaries</li><li>Diagram count by type </li><li>Diagram count by kind</li></ul><p><strong>Elements</strong></p><ul><li>Total element count</li><li>Total size of strings</li><li>Elements by meta-type</li><li>Features by size</li><li>String features by string length</li><li>KerML based Elements statistics<ul><li>Non-Standard Library Elements</li><li>Standard Library Elements</li></ul></li></ul><p><strong>Metadata</strong></p><ul><li>Diagram statistics</li><li>Element statistics</li></ul><p><strong>Standard Libraries</strong></p><ul><li>Diagram statistics</li><li>Element statistics</li></ul><p><strong>Symbol Styles</strong></p><ul><li>Diagram statistics</li><li>Element statistics</li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249576796 space=SYSML2P version=8 -->
## PAGE 00666: Quick find

- page_id: `249576796`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/249576796/Quick+find
- version_number: 8
- version_date: `2026-02-17T19:09:45.059+01:00`
- ancestors: SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > Project management > Working with projects
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Use the Quick Find functionality to search for specific elements in the model. The simple and easy-to-use dialog is designed to locate the needed element by its declared or short name.

##### Opening the Quick Find dialog

To open the Quick Find dialog, do one of the following

- On the Containment tab toolbar click the [ATTACHMENT filename='quick_find_button.png'] Quick Find button. **[IMAGE alt='' src='']**
- On the main menu, click Edit > Quick Find .
- On the keyboard, press Ctrl+Alt+F or Cmd+Opt+F .

Search results are displayed in the drop-down list, which opens when you start typing the search phrase. Click the element you need in this list or correct the search query to perform a new search. The element will be selected in the Containment tree and on the active view if the element has symbols on the active view.

##### Quick Find dialog elements

The following table provides descriptions of the **Quick Find** dialog elements.

| Dialog element name/image | Dialog element type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Search for | Options group | Click Any Element to search in all elements of your project.Click View to search only in views. |
| Search By Name | Text box with a drop-down list | Type a phrase to search for in element names. See the search results in the drop-down list.The following elements are excluded from the search results: (e.g., in, abstract, derived, etc.)Element metaclasses (e.g., part, attribute, package)Feature values (owned expressions) (except for Association and Connector)Expressions (except for ConstraintUsage and CalculationUsage)Features owned by MetadataFeature (e.g., Documentation, Comment, TextualRepresentation)Special actions (e.g., ControlNodes, AssignActionUsage, SendActionUsage)Features owned by special actions (parameters) |
|  | Menu button | Click the button and customize search options. Match Text AnywhereTo search for matching phrase in any part of the element name, click the command to add the check mark.To explicitly search from the beginning of element name, click the command to remove the check mark.Use Camel CaseTo search for matching phrase typed in camel case, click the command to add the check mark.To search for matching phrase typed in regular case, click the command to remove the check mark.You can use camel case as follows:Type only the first letters (either capitalized or not) of each word, for example, "and" or "AND" to find "Analysis and Design".Type skipping spacers, for example, "cv3" to find "CV-3". |
| More | Menu button | Click the button to load the next 100 matches. By default, the first 100 matches are shown in the dialog. |
| Expand | Menu button | Click the button to load all model elements. |
| TreeList | Tabs | Click to switch from the Tree view to the List, and vice versa. |
| Previous Next | Menu button | Click to navigate to a previous or next result.Available in the Tree view only. |
| Apply Filter (Ctrl+Space) | Check box | Click to clear the check box, if you need to remove the search scope filter. If you clear the Apply Filter check box once, your selection is remembered for the next time. |
| Filter Options | Menu button | Click the button and customize the search scope filter options. Ensure the Apply Filter check box is selectedHide Standard/System LibrariesReduces the search results list by hiding elements that belong to the Standard/System Libraries. |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249579792 space=SYSML2P version=9 -->
## PAGE 00667: Quick Properties Panel

- page_id: `249579792`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/249579792/Quick+Properties+Panel
- version_number: 9
- version_date: `2025-12-18T13:30:55.203+01:00`
- ancestors: SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > Project management > Modeling tool environment > User interface
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

TheQuick properties panelhas two tabs, as shown in the figure below.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']Quick properties panel

- The**Zoom**tab zooms in on the current view.
- The**Change Sets**tab shows the latest changes compared against the most recent project version saved in the local repository

##### Zoom tab

The Zoom tab allows you to preview and zoom in or out of the active view.

###### Opening the Zoom tab

To open the Zoom tab

- Do one of the following:
  - In the Model Browser, click the Zoom tab.
  - In the main menu, click Window > Zoom .

###### Displaying a view in the Zoom tab

To display a view in the Zoom tab

- Do one of the following:
  - Click the view in the Containment tree.
  - Click the view tab.

###### Zooming in or out of the active view

To zoom in or out of the active view

- In the Zoom tab, drag the corners of the blue square.

###### Quickly accessing any part of the view

To quickly access any part of the view

- In the Zoom tab, drag the blue square to the desired part of the view. The selected view part will be displayed in the view pane.

###### Fitting the view to the window

To fit the view to the window

- In the Zoom tab, on the blue square [ATTACHMENT filename='fit_window_button.png'] , click the Fit in Window button or press CTRL+W .

##### Change Sets tab

Use the Change Sets tab to track project modifications. The latest changes are compared against the last saved state (via **Save Project** or **Ctrl+S**commands) of the project in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing local projects' space=''].

- The panel legend explains the modification markings.
- Click the Refresh icon to update the display.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

The latest changes are compared against the last saved state (via <strong>Save Project</strong> or <strong>Ctrl+S </strong>commands) of the project in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Managing local projects" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[local repository]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p><ul><li data-uuid="be74c481-063f-421a-bcb5-2319b365827b">The panel legend explains the modification markings.</li><li data-uuid="b99b5d43-e37c-4f17-9df4-3e813432ef51">Click the <strong>Refresh</strong> icon to update the display.</li></ul><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="change sets tab.png" /></ac:image></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249577350 space=SYSML2P version=6 -->
## PAGE 00668: Redefinition

- page_id: `249577350`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/249577350/Redefinition
- version_number: 6
- version_date: `2026-04-24T14:57:49.149+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > Model composition > Relations > Specializations
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The redefinition relationshipis a kind of subsetting used to replace an inherited feature by redefining.

##### Redefinition

###### Redefining a usage via the symbol shortcut menu in a view

To redefine a usage via the symbol shortcut menu in a view

- Right-click an inherited usage element symbol in a view and in the shortcut menu select**Refactor**, then**Redefine**.

###### Redefining a usage via the Textual Editor

To redefine a usage via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, identify the usage element that you want to be the redefining one.
2. Follow the element name with either one of the following:
  1. keyword redefines
  2. textual symbol :>>
3. Follow the keyword with the name of the redefined usage element.
4. Click the Synchronize button. partdefEngine{ 
partcylinders[4..8] :Cylinders; 
}partdieselEngine:Engine{ 
partdieselEngineCylinders[4] :>>cylinders; 
/* or */ 
partdieselEngineCylinders[4] redefinescylinders; 
}

###### Redefining a usage via the Drag and Drop handler in a view

To redefine a usage via the Drag and Drop handler in a view

- Drag and drop an element from the Containment tree or use its symbol's [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Drag and Drop handler' space=''] on an element symbol executing the Set Redefined Feature command.

###### Redefining a usage via the view palette

To redefine a usage via the view palette

1. In the view palette, under the Specializations group, select redefinition .
2. Draw the line with the arrowhead going from the redefining element to the inherited one. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Redefining a usage via the Create Relation command

To redefine a usagevia the Create Relation command

1. Right-click the element that you want to be the redefining one.
2. In the shortcut menu, click Create Relation , then in the submenu, under Outgoing , click redefinition . The Create... dialog opens.
3. In the Create... dialog, do one of the following:
  1. In a view, click the element symbol you want to redefine. Hold Shift to select multiple elements.
  2. Click the Add [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. The element selection dialog opens. In the element selection dialog, select the element you want to redefine and click OK .
  3. Drag and drop the needed element you want to redefine from the Containment tree onto the Create... dialog.
4. In the Create... dialog, click OK .

###### Redefining a usage via the Specification panel

To redefine a usage via the Specification panel

- See the procedure [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification panel' space=''] on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification panel' space=''] page.

##### Redefining parameters

Parameters are directional features used in behavioral contexts. When it comes to redefining parameters, each owned parameter implicitly redefines the corresponding inherited parameter according to the latter's order:

50%

actiondefImportCustomerData{ 
incustomerId; 
incustomerName; 
incustomerEmail; 
} 
actionimportCustomerData:ImportCustomerData{ 
incustomerId;// implicitly redefines ImportCustomerData::customerId 
incustomerName;// implicitly redefines ImportCustomerData::customerName 
incustomerEmail;// implicitly redefines ImportCustomerData::customerEmail 
incustomerPhoneNumber;// new owned parameter 
}

50%

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

If you redefine the inherited parameters via either of the following procedures:

- 
- 

The redefinitions are created as follows:

- If there are other inherited parameters from the same owner with preceding positions, then all of those parameters are redefined. E.g., if there are three inherited parameters and you redefine the second, then the first and the second parameters are redefined, while the third is unaffected. [ATTACHMENT filename='parameter redefinition 2.png']
- The declared name for each redefining parameter is derived from the redefined parameter name. If multiple parameters are redefined, the names are concatenated. E.g., parameters customerId and validationId are inherited via separate specializations from two different definitions. Both parameters represent the first position in the order of inherited parameters. Redefining either of them creates a single redefining parameter with a concatenated name. [ATTACHMENT filename='parameter redefinition 3.png']
- The direction of each created parameter is the same as, or compatible with, the direction of the redefined parameter.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

subsetting used to replace an inherited feature by redefining.</span></p><h3>Redefinition</h3><h4>Redefining a usage via the symbol shortcut menu in a view</h4><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">To redefine a usage via the symbol shortcut menu in a view</span></p><hr /><ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Right-click an inherited usage element symbol in a view and in the shortcut menu select<span> </span></span><strong style="text-align: left;">Refactor</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">, then<span> </span></span><strong style="text-align: left;">Redefine</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">.</span></span></li></ul><h4>Redefining a usage via the Textual Editor</h4><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">To redefine a usage via the Textual following:<ol><li>keyword<em> redefines</em></li><li>textual symbol <em>:&gt;&gt;</em></li></ol></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Follow the keyword with the name of the redefined usage style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-red-bolder,#c9372c);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">dieselEngine</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Engine</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">4</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">] :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><h4>Redefining a usage via the Drag and Drop handler in a view</h4><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">To redefine a usage via the Drag and Drop handler in a view</span></p><hr /><ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Drag and drop an element from the Containment tree or use its symbol's<span> </span></span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Drag and Drop handler" /></ac:link> <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">on an <span>element symbol executing </span></span>the <strong>Set Redefined Feature </strong>command.</li></ul><h4>Redefining a usage via the view palette</h4><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">To redefine a usage via the view palette</span></p><hr /><ol><li>In the view palette, under the Specializations group, select <strong>redefinition</strong>. </li><li>Draw the line with the arrowhead going from the redefining element to the inherited one.<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Redefinition.png" /></ac:image><br /></span></li></ol><h4>Redefining a usage <span>via the Create Relation command</span></h4><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">To redefine a usage </span>via the Create Relation command</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the element that you want to be the redefining one.</li><li>In the shortcut menu, click <strong>Create Relation</strong>, then in the submenu, under <strong>Outgoing</strong>, click <strong>redefinition</strong>. The <strong style="text-align: left;">Create...</strong><strong> </strong>dialog opens.</li><li>In the <strong style="text-align: left;">Create...</strong><strong> </strong>dialog, do one of the following:<ol><li>In a view, click the element symbol you want to redefine. Hold Shift to select multiple elements. </li><li>Click the <strong>Add <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="add_button.png" /></ac:image> </strong>button. The element selection dialog opens. In the element selection dialog, select the element you want to redefine and click <strong>OK</strong>.</li><li>Drag and drop the needed element you want to redefine from the Containment tree onto the <strong style="text-align: left;">Create... </strong>dialog.</li></ol></li><li>In the <strong style="text-align: left;">Create...</strong><strong> </strong>dialog, click <strong>OK</strong>. </li></ol><h4>Redefining a usage <span>via the Specification panel</span></h4><p>To redefine a usage <span>via the Specification panel</span></p><hr /><ul><li>See the procedure <ac:link ac:anchor="Redefining inherited features via the compartment in the Specification panel"><ri:page ri:content-title="Specification panel" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Redefining inherited features via the compartment in the Specification panel]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> on the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specification panel" /></ac:link> page.</li></ul><h3>Redefining parameters</h3><p>Parameters are directional features used in behavioral contexts. When it comes to redefining parameters, each owned parameter implicitly redefines the corresponding inherited parameter according to the latter's order:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="section" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="492cc2f4-0db2-40d4-9ab8-4b15882d34fd"><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="58de3915-9c04-4e2e-86c7-d7f37a3e5b15"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">50%</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6af31a20-6191-498a-a3bc-51a088203c2f"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">action</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">ImportCustomerData</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">in</span><span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">customerId</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">in</span><span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">customerName</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span>in</span><span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">customerEmail</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">action</span><span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">importCustomerData</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">ImportCustomerData</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">in</span><span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">customerId</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// implicitly redefines ImportCustomerData::customerId</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">in</span><span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">customerName</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// implicitly redefines ImportCustomerData::customerName</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">in</span><span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">customerEmail</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// implicitly redefines ImportCustomerData::customerEmail</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">in</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">customerPhoneNumber</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// new owned parameter</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b571cff3-9ab8-4b82-a9f4-859230621257"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">50%</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="parameter redefinition 1.png" /></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>If you redefine the inherited parameters via either of the following procedures:</p><ul><li data-uuid="407e1133-43ab-4c1d-ab67-899f99dca82f"><ac:link ac:anchor="Redefining a usage via the symbol shortcut menu in a view"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Redefining a usage via the symbol shortcut menu in a view]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="17af3cc5-93f7-4d45-8f93-d7c04f1ae7bc"><ac:link ac:anchor="Redefining a usage via the Specification panel"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Redefining a usage via the Specification panel]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul><p>The redefinitions are created as follows:</p><ul><li data-uuid="8b42643b-6ce5-4470-a66b-4f2dc1d02aa8">If there are other inherited parameters from the same owner with preceding positions, then all of those parameters are redefined. E.g., if there are three inherited parameters and you redefine the second, then the first and the second parameters are redefined, while the third is unaffected. <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="parameter redefinition 2.png" /></ac:image></li><li data-uuid="551561c5-c124-4d9e-a615-d050e39daae3">The declared name for each redefining parameter is derived from the redefined parameter name. If multiple parameters are redefined, the names are concatenated. E.g., parameters <em>customerId </em>and <em>validationId </em>are inherited via separate specializations from two different definitions. Both parameters represent the first position in the order of inherited parameters. Redefining either of them creates a single redefining parameter with a concatenated name.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="parameter redefinition 3.png" /></ac:image></li><li data-uuid="4f92a41c-4772-4b83-85cb-23b1eb0c60dd">The direction of each created parameter is the same as, or compatible with, the direction of the redefined parameter.</li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249577348 space=SYSML2P version=6 -->
## PAGE 00669: Reference subsetting

- page_id: `249577348`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/249577348/Reference+subsetting
- version_number: 6
- version_date: `2026-02-17T20:55:58.541+01:00`
- ancestors: SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > Model composition > Relations > Specializations
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A reference subsettingis a kind of subsetting relationship. While a subsetting usage can have multiple subsetted usages, a reference subsetting usage can only have one specific subsetted usage.

##### Reference subsetting

###### Reference subsetting a usage via the Textual Editor

To reference subset a usage via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, identify the usage element that you want to be the referencing one.
2. Follow the element name with either one of the following:
  1. keyword references
  2. textual symbol ::>
3. Follow the keyword with the name of the referenced usage element.
4. Click the Synchronize button. part'drone in delivery mission'{ 
perform action ::>'deliver goods'; 
/* or */ perform action references'deliver goods'; 
} 
action'deliver goods'{ 
action'start mission';action'fly to destination'; 
first'start mission'then'fly to destination'; 
}

###### Reference subsetting a usage via the view palette

To reference subset a usage via the view palette

1. In the view palette, under the Specializations group, select reference subsetting .
2. Draw the line with the arrowhead going from the referencing usage to the referenced one. [ATTACHMENT filename='Reference subsetting.png']

###### Reference subsetting a usage via the Create Relation command

To reference subset a usagevia the Create Relation command

1. Right-click the element that you want to be the subsetting one.
2. In the shortcut menu, click Create Relation , then in the submenu, under Outgoing , click reference subsetting . The Create... dialog opens.
3. In the Create... dialog, do one of the following:
  1. In a view, click the element symbol you want to subset. Hold Shift to select multiple elements.
  2. Click the Add [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. The element selection dialog opens. In the element selection dialog, select the element you want to subset and click OK .
  3. Drag and drop the needed element you want to subset from the Containment tree onto the Create... dialog.
4. In the Create... dialog, click OK .

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=306282717 space=SYSML2P version=2 -->
## PAGE 00671: Removing used projects

- page_id: `306282717`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/306282717/Removing+used+projects
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-05-08T12:52:26.363+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > Project management > Working with projects > Managing local used projects
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Used projects are removed from the main project with all their references, including their used projects (indirectly used projects).

###### Removing a used project via the Containment tree

To remove a used project via the Containment tree

1. In the Containment tree of the main project, expand the Used Projects node.
2. Right-click a used project and select Remove .
3. When the question dialog opens, click Yes to confirm that you want to remove the used project. [ATTACHMENT filename='Removing a used project via the Containment tree.png']

###### Removing a used project via the Project Usages dialog

To remove a used project via the Project Usages dialog

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing local used projects' space='']Open the **Project Usages**dialog and d o one of the following:
  1. In the dialog's toolbar, click the **Remove with References** [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button .
  2. In the dialog's project usages tree, right-click a used project and in the shortcut menu, click the **Remove with References**command.
2. When the question dialog opens, click Yes to confirm that you want to remove the used project.
3. Click the OK button to close the Project Usages dialog.

###### Removing a used project via the Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog

To remove a used project via the Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing local used projects' space='']Open the **Advanced Project Usages Configuration**dialog .
2. In the Advanced Project Usages Configuration dialog, do one of the following:
  1. S elect a used project and in the dialog's toolbar, click the **Remove with References** [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button .
  2. Right-click a used project and in the shortcut menu, click the **Remove with References** command . The cell in the Action column for the removed used project states ' Remove, delete references '. [ATTACHMENT filename='Removing a used project via the Advanced Usages Configuration dialog.png']
3. (Optional) To undo the action, do one of the following:
  1. In the dialog's toolbar, click the Reset Changes for Selection [ATTACHMENT filename='reset_changes_for_selection_button.png'] button.
  2. Right-click the used project and in the shortcut menu, click the Reset Changes for Selection command.
4. Do one of the following:
  1. To save changes and keep the dialog open, click the Apply button.
  2. To save changes and close the dialog, click the OK button.
  3. To discard changes and close the dialog, click the Cancel button.
5. Click the OK button to close the Project Usages dialog.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249577792 space=SYSML2P version=3 -->
## PAGE 00672: Rendering basics

- page_id: `249577792`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/249577792/Rendering+basics
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-10-13T14:48:24.977+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > Model composition > Views > Rendering views
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

See the basic procedures for working with renderings and rendering definitions.

##### Creating rendering usage and definition elements

###### Creating rendering usage and definition elements via the Textual Editor

To create rendering usage and definition elements via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare the keyword:
  1. *rendering def*for a rendering definition.
  2. *rendering* for a rendering usage.
2. Specify the element name.
3. (Optional) Specify the rendering's features in the element body.
4. Click the Synchronize button.

renderingdefGraphicalRendering;

renderingasTreeDiagram : GraphicalRendering;

###### Creating concern usage and definition elements via the Create Element command

To create concern usage and definition elements via the Create Element command

1. In the Containment tree, right-click an element, and in the shortcut menu, click **Create Element**.
2. In the Create Element dialog, select one of the following:
  1. rendering def to create a rendering definition element.
  2. rendering to create a rendering usage element.
3. Name the element.
4. Specify the rendering.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249577791 space=SYSML2P version=5 -->
## PAGE 00673: Rendering views

- page_id: `249577791`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/249577791/Rendering+views
- version_number: 5
- version_date: `2025-09-30T14:44:47.907+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > Model composition > Views
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A rendering feature specifies how a particular view and its exposed elements are supposed to be rendered, i.e., displayed for viewing. Once you have[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating views' space=''], specified the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Exposing elements for views' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Filtering elements for views' space=''] the exposed elements, you can specify the renderer for the view.

##### Renderings, rendering styles, and view definitions

The following table maps renderings and rendering styles available in the modeling tool to corresponding predefined view definitions.

| View rendering kind | Rendering style | Renderings | Subsets of the renderings | Rendering definitions |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Graphical | Nested-view | Views::asInterconnectionDiagramView definitions using the renderer:Standard predefined symbolic view definitions:'General View' (through inheritance from 'Symbolic View' view def) 'Interconnection View' (through inheritance from 'Symbolic View' view def) 'Action Flow View' (through inheritance from 'Symbolic View' view def)'State Transition View' (through inheritance from 'Symbolic View' view def)Predefined symbolic view definitions that use expressions:NestedViewUsagesNestedView (by subclassifying NestedView)PartsNestedView (by subclassifying UsagesNestedView)Parts&PortsNestedView (by subclassifying UsagesNestedView)ActionsNestedView (by subclassifying UsagesNestedView)StatesNestedView (by subclassifying UsagesNestedView) | - | Views::GraphicalRendering |
| Tree-view | Views::asTreeDiagramView definitions using the renderer:Predefined symbolic view definitions that use expressions:TreeViewPartsTreeView (by subclassifying TreeView)ActionsTreeView (by subclassifying TreeView)RequirementTreeView (by subclassifying TreeView) | - | Views::GraphicalRendering |  |
| Tabular | - | Views::asElementTable | DS_Views::CoreViews::asTableView definitions using the renderer:Standard predefined tabular view definitions:'Generic Table' (subsets asTable inherited from 'Base Table' via subclassification)'Requirements Table' (subsets asTable inherited from 'Base Table' via subclassification) | Views::TabularRendering |

##### Rendering views

Ways to render views:

- . You can render views through inheritance, e.g., if the view is defined by a view definition that contains a renderer, the renderer is inherited by and applied to the defined view.
- . You can specify a renderer after the expose and filter declarations directly for the specific view, using the keyword render .

###### Inheriting a renderer

Generally, renderings are specified for view definitions, which are then used to define specific view usages, the latter inheriting the renderer specified for its definition.

See the table for all renderings available in the modeling tool and the predefined view definitions that use them. Choose the view definition based on the renderer specified for it that would be the most suitable for your view, then define the view by the view definition. However, many predefined view definitions carry specific filters and other customizations that will be inherited in addition to the renderer, thus choose accordingly. For more information, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Symbolic views' space=''] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Tabular views' space=''] pages.

The corresponding procedures are:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating symbolic views' space=''] on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating symbolic views' space=''] page.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating symbolic views' space=''] on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating symbolic views' space=''] page.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating tabular views' space=''] on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating tabular views' space=''] page.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating tabular views' space=''] on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating tabular views' space=''] page.

#### PANEL: Explanation in textual notation

Explanation in textual notation

// explanation and example of a symbolic view *General View*inheriting a renderer through subclassification

abstractviewdef<sv>'Symbolic View':>Visualization{ 
//... 
renderViews::asInterconnectionDiagram; //view definition has the *Views::asInterconnectionDiagram***renderer specified for it 
}

abstractviewdef<bsv>'Base Symbolic View':>sv{ // view definition 'Base Symbolic View' inherits the *Views::asInterconnectionDiagram***renderer through subclassification of 'Symbolic View' 
partbaseViewPalette:ViewPalettes::BasePalette:>>palette; 
}

viewdef<gv>'General View':>bsv,StandardViewDefinitions::gv{// view definition 'General View' inherits the*Views::asInterconnectionDiagram***renderer through subclassification of 'Base Symbolic View' 
partgeneralViewPalette:GeneralPalette:>>baseViewPalette; // view definition also contains a specific view palette 
}

view'General View':DS_Views::SymbolicViews::gv; // the specific view is defined by the view definition 'General View', inheriting its renderer and view palette

// explanation and example of a tabular view *Generic Table* inheriting a renderer through subclassification

abstractviewdef<bt>'Base Table':>bgv{ // view definition *'Base Table'* has the *asTable*renderer specified for it 
renderasTable; 
}

viewdef<gt>'Generic Table':> DS_Views::CoreViews::bt{ // view definition 'Generic Table' subsets the *asTable*renderer inherited through subclassification of *'Base Table'* 
renderrenderingasTable:>>asTable; 
}

view'Generic Table':DS_Views::TabularViews::gt;// the specific view is defined by the view definition 'Generic Table', inheriting its renderer

###### Specifying a renderer

Instead of creating views defined by view definitions that already have specified renderers, you can specify therenderer directly for the new view.

The corresponding procedures are:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating symbolic views' space=''] on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating symbolic views' space=''] page.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating tabular views' space=''] on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating tabular views' space=''] page.

#### PANEL: Explanation in textual notation

Explanation in textual notation

viewnewView{ // new view element is not defined by any view definition 
renderViews::asInterconnectionDiagram; // the view has a renderer *asInterconnectionDiagram*, rendering it as a 'General View' symbolic view with nested-view rendering style 
}

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

