style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">render</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">Views::asInterconnectionDiagram</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">view definition has the <em>Views::asInterconnectionDiagram</em><em> </em>renderer specified for it</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">abstract</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> &lt;bsv&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">'Base Symbolic View'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">sv</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> { <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// view definition 'Base Symbolic View' inherits the <em>Views::asInterconnectionDiagram</em><em> </em>renderer through subclassification of 'Symbolic View'</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">   </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">baseViewPalette</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">ViewPalettes::BasePalette</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">palette</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> &lt;gv&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">'General View'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">bsv</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">, </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">StandardViewDefinitions::gv</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// view definition 'General View' inherits the </span></span><em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Views::asInterconnectionDiagram</span></em><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><em> </em>renderer through subclassification of 'Base Symbolic View'</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">   </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">generalViewPalette</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">GeneralPalette</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">baseViewPalette</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// view definition also contains a specific view palette </span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'General View'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span> </span>:<span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">DS_Views::SymbolicViews</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">::gv</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;       <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// the specific view is defined by the view definition 'General View', inheriting its renderer and view palette </span></span> </p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// explanation and example of a tabular view <em>Generic Table</em> inheriting a renderer through subclassification</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">abstract</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> &lt;bt&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">'Base Table'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">bgv</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> { <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// view definition <em>'Base Table'</em> has the <em>asTable </em>renderer specified for it</span></span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">   </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">render</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">asTable</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> &lt;gt&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">'Generic Table'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">DS_Views::CoreViews::bt</span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {  <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// view definition 'Generic Table' subsets the <em>asTable </em>renderer inherited through subclassification of <em>'Base Table'</em></span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">   </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">render</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">rendering</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">asTable</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">asTable;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'Generic Table'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">DS_Views::TabularViews::gt</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">; </span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// the specific view is defined by the view definition 'Generic Table', inheriting its renderer </span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Specifying a renderer</h4><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Instead of creating views defined by view definitions that already have specified renderers, you can specify the<span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">renderer directly for the new view.<br /></span></p><p>The corresponding procedures are:</p><ul><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Creating undefined symbolic views using renderers via the Textual Editor"><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating symbolic views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Creating undefined symbolic views using renderers via the Textual Editor]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> on the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating symbolic views" /></ac:link> page.</li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Creating undefined tabular views using renderers via the Textual Editor"><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating tabular views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Creating undefined tabular views using renderers via the Textual Editor]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> on the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating tabular views" /></ac:link> page.</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b729039d-3f68-43b7-a173-7378442669ff"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Explanation in textual notation</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">newView</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {   <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">  <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// new view element is not defined by any view definition</span></span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">render</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">Views::asInterconnectionDiagram</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// the view has a renderer <em>asInterconnectionDiagram</em>, rendering it as a 'General View' symbolic view with nested-view rendering style</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=263094421 space=SYSML2P version=9 -->
## PAGE 00674: Rendering views basics

- page_id: `263094421`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/263094421/Rendering+views+basics
- version_number: 9
- version_date: `2026-02-17T21:29:38.070+01:00`
- ancestors: SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > Model composition > Views > Rendering views
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

There are two ways a rendering usage can be specified for a view:

50%

- by referencing the rendering usage defined elsewhere in the model.
  - The element symbol is displayed with the render keyword (unnamed element) or the rendering keyword (named element) . [ATTACHMENT filename='rendering.png']
  - See the procedures:
    - :
      - 
      - 
      - 

50%

- by containing an owned rendering usage, i.e., the view renderer is the owned rendering usage itself.
  - The element symbol is displayed with the rendering keyword. [ATTACHMENT filename='rendering2.png']
  - See the procedures:
    - :
      - 

##### Rendering views by referencing renderings defined elsewhere in the model

###### Rendering views via the Textual Editor

To render views via the Textual Editor

1. In the body of a view for which you want to reference a rendering, declare one of the following:
  1. For an unnamed rendering, declare the render keyword, then follow with the name of the rendering you want to reference.
  2. For a named rendering, declare the rendering keyword, name the new usage, and then [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Reference subsetting' space=''] the rendering you want to reference.
2. Click the Synchronize button.

viewvehicleModel{ 
renderasTreeDiagram;// unnamed rendering 
renderingasNewTreeDiagram::>asTreeDiagram;// named rendering 
}

###### Rendering views via the Drag and Drop handler in a view

To render views via the Drag and Drop handler in a view

- Drag and drop a rendering element from the Containment tree or use its symbol's [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Drag and Drop handler' space=''] and drop it on a rendering usage element symbol (keyword render rendering ) that you want to render the view by referencing the rendering using the Create Satisfy Requirement command.

###### Rendering views via the Specification panel

To render views via the Specification panel

- Drag and drop a rendering element from the Containment tree or use its symbol's [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Drag and Drop handler' space=''] and drop it on the **Referenced Rendering** field in the Properties section of the Specification panel for the rendering usage element (keyword render rendering ). The appropriate rendering is specified for the element.

##### Rendering views via the owned rendering usage itself as the renderer

###### Rendering views via the Textual Editor2Rendering views via the Textual Editor

To render views via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor within the curly braces of the view's body, declare the rendering keyword, specify the element name, and then the rendering's body.
2. Click the Synchronize button.

viewvehicleModel{renderingasCustomTreeDiagram:GraphicalRendering;// // new view-owned rendering usage}
